trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the Ophthalos fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Panoramic Paranoia
entries: The Ophthalos has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight, and it can't be {@status Surprised|XPHB}.

trait.name: Photosensitivity
entries: The Ophthalos has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} against the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition. When it becomes {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, roll a {@dice d4} twice, rerolling duplicates. Until the end of the Ophthalos's next turn, if it rolls either of those numbers when it uses its Searing Gaze, no beam is produced.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Ophthalos makes one Claws attack and one Tail attack. It can replace one of these attacks with a use of Searing Gaze.

action.name: Claws
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Poison damage, and the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution Saving Throw or be {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} until the start of the Ophthalos's next turn.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Searing Gaze
entries: The Ophthalos randomly shoots a beam from eyes in its wings (roll {@dice 1d4}):

items.name: Firebomb
entries: The Ophthalos shoots a beam at a point within 120 feet. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity Saving Throw, taking 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Fire damage on a failure and falling {@condition prone}, or half damage on a success.

items.name: Rapid Fire
entries: The Ophthalos shoots small beams at three different points within 120 feet. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on each point must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity Saving Throw, taking 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Fire damage on a failure, or half damage on a success.

items.name: Scorched Earth
entries: The Ophthalos shoots a beam at a point on the ground within 120 feet and drags it in a straight {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}, creating a wall of flames up to 20 feet long, 10 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall lasts until the start of the Ophthalos's next turn. A creature in the wall when it appears, that enters the wall for the first time on a turn, or that ends its turn there must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity Saving Throw, taking 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Fire damage on a failure, or half damage on a success.

items.name: Superheat
entries: The Ophthalos shoots a beam of concentrated heat against a creature within 120 feet. The target must make a {@dc 16} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, one metal object the target is carrying or wearing suffers the effects of a 4th-level {@spell Heat Metal|XPHB} spell until the start of the Ophthalos's next turn.

bonus.name: Antilight Cone
entries: The Ophthalos's central eye emits a light-consuming wave in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Until the start of the Ophthalos's next turn, that area acts as a 4th-level {@spell Darkness|XPHB} spell.

legendary.name: Swipe
entries: The Ophthalos makes one Claws attack.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The Ophthalos moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Searing Gaze (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The Ophthalos uses its Searing Gaze.

ac.condition: plus a bonus equal to the number of sets of spikes it has while it has fewer than 30 hit points

trait.name: Bristly
entries: The Nergigoblin has a maximum of 4 sets of spikes growing from its body, which it can expend as part of some of its actions. It regrows {@dice 1d4} sets of spikes and regains 5 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

The Nergigoblin's spikes are associated with a Spike Die, which is a {@dice d4}. While the Nergigoblin has 30–59 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its spikes lengthen and its Spike Die becomes a {@dice d6}. While the Nergigoblin has fewer than 30 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the spikes become metallic; its Spike Die becomes a {@dice d8} and it has a bonus to AC equal to the number of sets of spikes it has.

trait.name: Monstrous Resilience
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the Nergigoblin can choose to end one of the following conditions on itself: {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Nergigoblin makes one Claw attack and one Horns attack. It can replace either of these attacks with a use of Spike Fling.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Slashing damage, and the Nergigoblin can expend 1 set of spikes to deal extra Slashing damage equal to 1 roll of its Spike Die.

action.name: Horns
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the Nergigoblin can expend 1 set of spikes to deal extra Piercing damage equal to 1 roll of its Spike Die.

action.name: Spike Fling
entries: The Nergigoblin swings its tail, expending 3 sets of spikes to unleash a shower of shrapnel upon its foes. Each creature in a 20-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes Piercing damage equal to 5 rolls of the Nergigoblin's Spike Die. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

legendary.name: Swipe
entries: The Nergigoblin makes a Claw attack.

legendary.name: Goading Sidestep
entries: The Nergigoblin moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. If a creature makes an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against the Nergigoblin during this movement, the Nergigoblin can make a Claw attack against that creature immediately after the attack hits or misses.

legendary.name: Forceful Growth (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Nergigoblin forces more spikes to protrude from its body, immediately regrowing {@dice 1d4} sets of spikes. It then takes 10 ({@damage 4d4}) Piercing damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the Godmane fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Reckless
entries: At the start of its turn, the Godmane can gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Melee Weapon Attack rolls it makes during that turn, but Attack Rolls against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The Godmane's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 30 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 20 feet with or without a running start.

trait.name: Godrage Form
entries: When the Godmane is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it enters a state of radiant rage, causing its fur to stand on end and become golden. Its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total resets to 250 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance, and it gains the Godslayer trait for 1 hour.

Award a party an additional 15,000 XP (28,000 XP total) for defeating the Godmane after its Godrage Form activates. Once the Godmane's Godrage Form has activated, it can't do so again until the Godmane finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Godslayer
entries: While in Godrage Form, the Godmane ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage and treats {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} as {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Godmane makes two Fist attacks and uses either Primate Piledriver or Simian Spin.

action.name: Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Radiant damage if the Godmane is in its Godrage Form. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it's {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 19}) by one of the Godmane's two fists.

action.name: Rock
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 60/240 ft., one target. {@h}27 ({@damage 6d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage, plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Radiant damage if the Godmane is in its Godrage Form. If the target is a creature with a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, it must succeed on a {@dc 19} Strength Saving Throw or lose its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Primate Piledriver
entries: While the Godmane isn't grappling a Large or larger creature, it drops from a great height with a creature held beneath it. The Godmane takes no damage from Falling from a height of 60 feet or lower.

One creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Godmane must make a {@dc 19} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage for each 10 feet the Godmane Falls in this way. On a success, the target takes half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the creature falls {@condition Prone|XPHB} and is no longer {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Godmane.

action.name: Simian Spin
entries: The Godmane spins and throws a Medium or smaller creature it's grappling up to 30 feet horizontally. If it collides with another creature or object, the thrown creature must make a {@dc 19} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage for each 10 feet it traveled before colliding. On a success, it takes half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the creature falls {@condition Prone|XPHB}.
If the thrown creature collides with another creature, the second creature must succeed on a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw or take the same damage.

legendary.name: Fist
entries: The Godmane makes one Fist attack.

legendary.name: Leap
entries: The Godmane jumps up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB|Walk Speed} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Rumble in the Jungle (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Godmane uses Primate Piledriver or Simian Spin.

legendary.name: Godrage Actions
entries: If the Godmane's Godrage Form has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as Legendary Actions.

items.name: Aerial Dash (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Godmane flies up to its Fly Speed without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

items.name: Godkiller Beam (Costs 3 Actions)
entries: The Godmane shoots a beam of radiant energy from its mouth. Each creature in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a {@dc 18} Dexterity Saving Throw. Creatures {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Godmane have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw. A creature takes 26 ({@damage 4d12}) Radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

trait.name: Boundless Hunger
entries: The Devil Jhoblin can't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls against {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creatures.

trait.name: Musclebound
entries: While the Devil Jhoblin has fewer than 80 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its muscles enlarge grotesquely: it can attempt to Shove a creature as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} (save {@dc 13}), and creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} against being Shoved by the Devil Jhoblin. In addition, while the Devil Jhoblin has fewer than 40 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its muscles become imbued with necrotic energy, and its attacks deal an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Devil Jhoblin makes two Bite attacks. It can replace one of these attacks with a use of Crunch.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it's {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Devil Jhoblin (escape {@dc 13}). While a creature is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} in this way, the Devil Jhoblin can't make Bite attacks against a different target.

action.name: Crunch
entries: The Devil Jhoblin attempts to crush a creature in its powerful jaws. One creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Devil Jhoblin's Bite must make a {@dc 13} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Piercing damage, and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Ability Checks until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half damage only.

legendary.name: Bite
entries: The Devil Jhoblin makes one Bite attack.

legendary.name: Aggressive Charge
entries: The Devil Jhoblin moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} towards a enemy it can see. If it ends this movement within 5 feet of the target, it can attempt to Shove it.

legendary.name: Devour (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Devil Jhoblin consumes the corpse of a Medium or Small creature within 5 feet of it, regaining 14 ({@damage 4d6}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Brainielle fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Mind Reader
entries: The Brainielle can sense the presence and location of any creature within 300 feet of it that has an Intelligence of 3 or higher, regardless of interposing barriers, unless the creature is protected by a {@spell Mind Blank|XPHB} spell.

trait.name: Brainstorm Form
entries: When the Brainielle is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it taps into the full power of the parasite in its brain. Its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total resets to 250 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its creature type changes from Dragon to Aberration, it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance, and it gains the Fry Neurons and Static Body traits for 1 hour.

Award a party an additional 25,000 XP (45,000 XP total) for defeating the Brainielle after its Brainstorm Form activates. Once the Brainielle's Brainstorm Form has activated, it can't do so again until the Brainielle finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Fry Neurons
entries: When the Brainielle deals Lightning damage to a creature, it deals extra Psychic damage equal to half the amount of Lightning damage taken.

entries.name: Static Body
entries: A creature that touches the Brainielle or hits it with a Melee Attack while within 5 feet of it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Lightning damage. This trait only functions if at least one of the Brainielle's Lightning Breath and Mesmerising Wingbeat actions are recharged.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Brainielle makes two Tentacles attacks.

action.name: Tentacles
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it's {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 20}). Until the grapple ends, the Brainielle can't make Tentacles attacks against another target.

action.name: Devour Brain
entries: One creature with a brain that's either {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the Brainielle or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} must make a {@dc 21} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 55 ({@damage 10d10}) Piercing damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage. If this damage reduces the target to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the target is killed as its brain is consumed by the Brainielle. If the Brainielle is in its Brainstorm Form, it gains 22 ({@damage 4d10}) {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when it kills a creature in this way.

action.name: Detect Thoughts
entries: The Brainielle casts {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB}, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 21}).

action.name: Lightning Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: The Brainielle exhales lightning. Each creature in a 5-foot-wide, 100-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 49 ({@damage 14d6}) Lightning damage. If a creature fails the save by 5 or more, it is also {@condition Stunned|XPHB} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half as much damage.

action.name: Mesmerising Wingbeat {@recharge 5}
entries: The Brainielle opens its wings and moves them in a hypnotic manner while they shimmer with an array of colourful lights. Each creature within 30 feet that can see the Brainielle must make a {@dc 20} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, the creature becomes {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the Brainielle. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way, a creature is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0, it takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns, and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. This effect also ends for the creature if it takes any damage except for Psychic damage, or if another creature within 5 feet takes an action to shake the creature out of its stupor.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The Brainielle makes a Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check.

legendary.name: Tentacles
entries: The Brainielle makes one Tentacles attack.

legendary.name: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Brainielle beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the Brainielle must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity Saving Throw, taking 13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage and being knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} on a failure. The Brainielle then flies up to half its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Brainstorm Actions
entries: If the Brainielle's Brainstorm Form has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as Legendary Actions.

entries.name: Telekinesis
entries: One Huge or smaller creature or object within 60 feet that the Brainielle can see must make a {@dc 21} Strength Saving Throw. Tiny creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw, and objects automatically fail. On a failure, the Brainielle telekinetically moves the target up to 30 feet in any direction.

entries.name: Mental Infiltration
entries: The Brainielle invades another creature's mind to muddle its thoughts and anticipate its moves. One creature within 60 feet that the Brainielle can see must make a {@dc 21} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, until the end of the Brainielle's next turn, the Brainielle has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls against the creature, and the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls against the Brainielle. This effect ends early if the Brainielle uses this Legendary Action again.

trait.name: Bubble Blight
entries: Architune's Concussive Bubble attack and Oil Jet Legendary Action can give a creature levels of Bubble Blight, as they become covered in slippery bubbles. A creature with 2 or more levels of Bubble Blight has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} and Dexterity Ability Checks. A creature with 3 or more levels of Bubble Blight must succeed on a {@dc 23} Dexterity Saving Throw the first time it moves on each turn or fall {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

When a creature with Bubble Blight takes Fire damage, it takes an extra 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Fire damage for each level of Bubble Blight it has, then loses all levels of Bubble Blight. A creature also loses all levels of Bubble Blight if it washes itself over the course of 1 minute.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If Architsune fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: Architsune has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Architsune makes two Bite or Concussive Bubble attacks in any combination, and uses Bubble Trouble, if available.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 13} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 2d10 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Concussive Bubble
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 13} to hit, range 20/80 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it gains 1 level of Bubble Blight (see Bubble Blight trait).

action.name: Bubble Trouble {@recharge 4}
entries: Architsune blows an enormous bubble from its pipe, which floats 20 feet above a point on the ground Architune can see within 30 feet of it. While this bubble exists, Architsune can make two extra Concussive Bubble attacks as part of its Multiattack action. These extra attacks are made from the enormous bubble's space, using Architune's own senses. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, Architsune can move the enormous bubble up to 30 feet, but no closer to the ground.

When this action recharges, the large bubble crashes to the ground directly beneath it and ignites in a large explosion. Each creature in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point on the ground directly below the bubble must make a {@dc 23} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 42 ({@damage 12d6}) Fire damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

bonus.name: Slippery Escape
entries: Architsune ends the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions on itself.

legendary.name: Bubble
entries: Architsune makes a Concussive Bubble attack.

legendary.name: Move
entries: Architsune moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Oil Jet (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: Architsune spits out a high-pressure jet of oil in a line. Each creature in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a {@dc 23} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 36 ({@damage 8d8}) Bludgeoning damage, and gains 1 level of Bubble Blight (see Bubble Blight trait). On a success, it takes half as much damage.

trait.name: Bark & Bite
entries: The Infernoguma has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls made against creatures {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by it.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the Infernoguma is motionless at the start of combat, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the Infernoguma move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check to discern that the Infernoguma isn't an ordinary statue.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: The Infernoguma has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Sensitive Nose
entries: The Infernoguma can't use its {@sense Blindsight} while within the area of a {@spell Stinking Cloud} spell or a similar effect that produces a strong odour.

trait.name: Hell's Gate Guard
entries: The Infernoguma is always {@condition Petrified|XPHB}, but remains aware of its surroundings, and magically senses the presence (but not the location) of any non-Devil creatures within 500 feet of it, regardless of interposing barriers.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Infernoguma makes two attacks, using Bite, Claw, or Hurl in any combination.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}25 ({@damage 3d12 + 6}) Piercing damage plus 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Poison damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}19 ({@damage 3d8 + 6}) Slashing damage. If the target is a creature and the Infernoguma moves 20 feet in a straight line towards the target immediately before the attack, the target must succeed on a {@dc 19} Strength Saving Throw or be knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

action.name: Hurl
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 11} to hit, range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d6 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage. The target and each creature within 5 feet of the target must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 10 feet straight away from the Infernoguma.

bonus.name: Blaze (While Not Bloodied Only)
entries: The Infernoguma's mane and mouth flare up with hellfire. For 1 minute, or until the Infernoguma is {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}, it emits {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. In addition, its Melee Attacks deal an extra 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Fire damage on a hit and, when a creature starts its turn within 5 feet of the Infernoguma, the creature takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) Fire damage.

reaction.name: Awakening Roar
entries: {@actTrigger} A non-Devil creature comes within 30 feet of the Infernoguma or it hears another Infernoguma use its Awakening Roar Reaction.

{@actResponse} The Infernoguma gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Petrified|XPHB} condition for 1 hour or until it no longer senses any non-Devil creatures within 500 feet of itself, and emits an intimidating roar that can be heard up to 300 feet away. Each non-Devil creature within 60 feet of the Infernoguma that can hear it must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 22 ({@damage 5d8}) Thunder damage and is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of the Infernoguma. It repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. On a success, a creature takes half damage only.

trait.name: Conductive Secretions
entries: When the Be-Hudra hits a creature with its Constrict attack or a creature fails its save against the Be-Hudra's Slough {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the creature becomes covered in thick, oily secretions that conduct electricity. When a creature covered by these secretions takes Lightning damage, it rolls three additional Lightning damage dice and adds them to the total damage dealt, and the secretions evaporate.

A creature can remove the secretions by standing in heavy rain, being targeted by the {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} spell, immersing itself in at least 10 gallons of water, or taking an action each turn to wash itself with water continuously for 1 minute.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Be-Hudra fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Multiple Heads
entries: The Be-Hudra has six heads. While it has more than one head, the Be-Hudra has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} against being {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, and knocked {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}.

Whenever the Be-Hudra takes 50 or more damage in a single turn, one of its heads dies. If all its heads die, the Be-Hudra dies. The number of Melee Attacks the Be-Hudra makes in a round (including its Legendary Actions) and the number of creatures it can grapple cannot exceed its current number of heads.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Be-Hudra makes three Bite attacks. It can replace any number of those attacks with a Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d10 + 5}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d10 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is covered in fluid per the Be-Hudra's Conductive Secretions trait and is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 20}). The target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until this grapple ends.

action.name: Lightning Tangle {@recharge 5} ({@recharge 6} if the Be-Hudra has 3 heads or fewer)
entries: The Be-Hudra exhales a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} of Lightning from each of its surviving heads. Each creature in one or more 5-foot-wide, 20-foot-long {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Lines} must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Lightning damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

bonus.name: Slough
entries: The Be-Hudra hurls a glob of electrically-conductive secretions at one creature within 60 feet. The target must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is covered in fluid per the Be-Hudra's Conductive Secretions trait.

legendary.name: Frantic Rush
entries: The Be-Hudra moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Constrict
entries: The Be-Hudra makes one Constrict attack.

legendary.name: Play with Food (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Be-Hudra smashes two creatures it has {@condition Grappled|XPHB} together. Both creatures must make a {@dc 20} Constitution Saving Throw, taking 27 ({@damage 5d10}) Bludgeoning damage on a failure, or half as much damage on a success.

trait.name: Air Sacs
entries: While its air sacs are inflated, the Rompopowlbear has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage, {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Piercing damage, and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}) checks. In addition, it has a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 10 feet and can hover. If the Rompopowlbear takes at least 10 Piercing damage in a single instance, its air sacs deflate and it loses the benefits of this trait until it inflates its air sacs again.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Rompopowlbear makes two Talon attacks.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., two targets within 5 feet of each other. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Slashing damage. Each target must make a {@dc 13} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, if the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

action.name: Screech
entries: The Rompopowlbear emits a frightening screech that can be heard up to 15 feet away, or up to 30 feet away if its air sacs are inflated. Each creature that can hear the screech must make a {@dc 13} Wisdom Saving Throw ({@dc 15} if the Rompopowlbear's air sacs are inflated). On a failure, the target is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} until the end of the Rompopowlbear's next turn. Whether the save is a success or failure, the Rompopowlbear's air sacs deflate.

bonus.name: Inhale
entries: The Rompopowlbear's air sacs become inflated for 1 minute.

bonus.name: Airburst
entries: The Rompopowlbear injects air into the ground through the spikes on its tail, launching a burst of air at one Large or smaller creature on the ground within 30 feet. The target must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity Saving Throw ({@dc 15} if the Rompopowlbear's air sacs are inflated). On a failure, the target is launched 10 feet vertically into the air, plus an additional 20 feet horizontally in a direction of the Rompopowlbear's choosing if its air sacs are inflated. Whether the save is a success or failure, the Rompopowlbear's air sacs deflate.

reaction.name: Absorb Toxins
entries: {@actTrigger} The Rompopowlbear would take Poison damage from any source other than itself. {@actResponse} The Rompopowlbear uses its Inhale. On its next turn, if its air sacs are still inflated, the Rompopowlbear can use its Screech as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The first time the Rompopowlbear uses its Screech or Airburst during the turn, creatures affected by either feature must make a {@dc 15} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} until the end of the Rompopowlbear's next turn. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

trait.name: Adhesive Flesh
entries: The Splatacabra adheres to anything smaller than itself that touches it. When a creature hits or misses the Splatacabra with a Melee Weapon Attack, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 14} Strength Saving Throw or the weapon becomes stuck to the Splatacabra. If the weapon's wielder can't or won't let go of the weapon, the wielder is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} while the weapon is stuck. While stuck, the weapon can't be used. A creature can pull the weapon free by taking an action to make a {@dc 14} Strength check and succeeding.

If the attack was an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} from a Huge or smaller creature, the creature adheres to the Splatacabra and is also {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by it (escape {@dc 14}). Ability Checks made to escape this grapple have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

trait.name: Gluetrap Armour
entries: When a Medium or larger object or structure within 5 feet of the Splatacabra is destroyed, the Splatacabra gains a +1 bonus to its AC (to a maximum of 19). When the Splatacabra is hit by a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} or an attack that exceeds its AC by 5 or more, it suffers a -1 penalty to AC (to a minimum of 11), or it drops an object stuck to it with its Adhesive Flesh trait (attacker's choice).

trait.name: Scattered Cognition
entries: The conflicting minds of prey and predator within the Splatacabra make it immune to any effect that would sense its emotions or read its thoughts, and it rolls {@dice 1d4} at the start of each of its turns. On a 1 or 2, it can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} until the end of its turn, and on a 3 or 4, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first Attack Roll it makes that turn.

trait.name: Siege Monster
entries: The Splatacabra deals double damage to objects and structures.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Splatacabra makes one Pseudotongue attack and one Slam attack. If both attacks hit the same Huge or smaller creature, the creature is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Acid damage.

action.name: Pseudotongue
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Acid damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength Saving Throw or be pulled 15 feet straight toward the Splatacabra.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d12 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

legendary.name: Bite
entries: The Splatacabra makes one Bite attack.

legendary.name: Charge
entries: The Splatacabra moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and makes a Slam attack.

legendary.name: Ground Pound (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Splatacabra smashes the ground in front of it, scattering fragments into its body that grant it a +2 bonus to AC as per its Gluetrap Armour trait.

monster.name: Palicopy
languages: understands Common but can't speak it

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Pseudopod
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Medium or larger creature, the Palicopy enters the target's space and attaches to it.

While attached to a target, the Palicopy's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0, it can't benefit from any bonus to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, it moves with the target, and it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls against the target. If an Attack Roll misses the Palicopy, the creature to whom the Palicopy is attached immediately takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage.

A creature can take an action to try to detach the Palicopy from itself or a creature within its reach, doing so with a successful {@dc 13} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check. On its turn, the Palicopy can detach itself by using 5 feet of movement.

trait.name: Avoidance
entries: If the Darkveld is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Saving Throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it's {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

trait.name: Displacement
entries: The Darkveld projects a magical illusion that makes it appear to be standing near its actual location, causing Attack Rolls against it to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. If it is hit by an attack, this trait is suppressed until the end of its next turn. This trait is also suppressed while the Darkveld is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Darkveld fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Darkveld makes three attacks with any combination of Horn and Whip and uses Devour Lifeforce.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d6 + 8}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d8 + 8}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 23}). A {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature is also suffocating, and while suffocating, it gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level at the end of each of its turns. When it can breathe again, it removes all levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} it gained from suffocating.
The Darkveld has five whips and can't use a whip that is grappling a creature to attack another target.

action.name: Devour Lifeforce
entries: The Darkveld drains energy from a creature wrapped in its whips. One creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by one of the Darkveld's whips must make a {@dc 20} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 26 ({@damage 4d12}) Necrotic damage, and the Darkveld's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 30 feet until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Darkveld gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage taken.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Darkveld makes one Horn attack.

legendary.name: Sprint
entries: The Darkveld moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Reform Illusion
entries: The Darkveld loses all its {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains the use of its Displacement trait if it has been suppressed by an attack.

legendary.name: Devour Lifeforce (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Darkveld uses its Devour Lifeforce.

trait.name: Avoidance
entries: If the Darkveld is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Saving Throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it's {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

trait.name: Displacement
entries: The Darkveld projects a magical illusion that makes it appear to be standing near its actual location, causing Attack Rolls against it to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. If it is hit by an attack, this trait is suppressed until the end of its next turn. This trait is also suppressed while the Darkveld is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Darkveld fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Darkveld makes three attacks with any combination of Horn and Whip and uses Devour Lifeforce.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d6 + 6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 19}). A {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature is also suffocating, and while suffocating, it gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level at the end of each of its turns. When it can breathe again, it removes all levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} it gained from suffocating.
The Darkveld has five whips and can't use a whip that is grappling a creature to attack another target.

action.name: Devour Lifeforce
entries: The Darkveld drains energy from a creature wrapped in its whips. One creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by one of the Darkveld's whips must make a {@dc 18} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 19 ({@damage 3d12}) Necrotic damage, and the Darkveld's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 30 feet until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Darkveld gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage taken.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Darkveld makes one Horn attack.

legendary.name: Sprint
entries: The Darkveld moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries: The Darkveld loses all its {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains the use of its Displacement trait if it has been suppressed by an attack.

legendary.name: Devour Lifeforce (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Darkveld uses its Devour Lifeforce.

entries: If the Darkveld is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Saving Throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it's {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

If it is hit by an attack, this trait is suppressed until the end of its next turn. This trait is also suppressed while the Darkveld is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the Darkveld fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Darkveld makes three attacks with any combination of Horn and Whip and uses Devour Lifeforce.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d6 + 5}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 17}). A {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature is also suffocating, and while suffocating, it gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level at the end of each of its turns. When it can breathe again, it removes all levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} it gained from suffocating.
The Darkveld has five whips and can't use a whip that is grappling a creature to attack another target.

action.name: Devour Lifeforce

On a failure, the target takes 13 ({@damage 2d12}) Necrotic damage, and the Darkveld's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 30 feet until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Darkveld gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage taken.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Darkveld makes one Horn attack.

legendary.name: Sprint
entries: The Darkveld moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Reform Illusion
entries: The Darkveld loses all its {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains the use of its Displacement trait if it has been suppressed by an attack.

legendary.name: Devour Lifeforce (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Darkveld uses its Devour Lifeforce.

entries: If the Darkveld is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Saving Throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it's {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

trait.name: Displacement
entries: The Darkveld projects a magical illusion that makes it appear to be standing near its actual location, causing Attack Rolls against it to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}. If it is hit by an attack, this trait is suppressed until the end of its next turn. This trait is also suppressed while the Darkveld is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0 feet.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (1/Day)
entries: If the Darkveld fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Darkveld makes two attacks with any combination of Horn and Whip and uses Devour Lifeforce.

action.name: Horn
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Whip
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 14}). A {@condition Grappled|XPHB} creature is also suffocating, and while suffocating, it gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level at the end of each of its turns. When it can breathe again, it removes all levels of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} it gained from suffocating.
The Darkveld has five whips and can't use a whip that is grappling a creature to attack another target.

action.name: Devour Lifeforce
entries: The Darkveld drains energy from a creature wrapped in its whips. One creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by one of the Darkveld's whips must make a {@dc 13} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 6 ({@damage 1d12}) Necrotic damage, and the Darkveld's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 30 feet until the end of its next turn. On a success, the target takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Darkveld gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage taken.

trait.name: Divine Cloak (Except While Unveiled Form is Active)
entries: The Zohtraferna appears to be a Celestial, including to Divination magic and creatures with {@sense Truesight}.

trait.name: Exposed to Light (Unveiled Form Only)
entries: When the Zohtraferna takes 10 or more Radiant damage in a single instance, it loses its damage {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Zohtraferna fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Zohtraferna's Weapon Attacks are magical.

trait.name: Regrowth
entries: At the start of each of its turns, the Zohtraferna regains 15 ({@damage 1d12 + 9}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} unless it has taken Radiant or Necrotic damage since the start of its last turn.

trait.name: Unveiled Form
entries: When the Zohtraferna is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its feathers and white scales melt away, revealing a fiery, blackened form beneath. Its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total resets to 341 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance. Additionally, the Zohtraferna can now use the options labelled "Unveiled Form Only" for 1 hour. Award a party an additional 50,000 XP (83,000 XP total) for defeating the Zohtraferna after its Unveiled Form activates. Once the Zohtraferna's Unveiled Form has activated, it can't do so again until Zohtraferna finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Zohtraferna makes two Deceiver's Blade attacks or uses Purifying Pulse and makes one Deceiver's Blade attack.

action.name: Deceiver's Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) Slashing damage. If the Zohtraferna is in its Unveiled Form, the target takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Purifying Pulse
entries: The Zohtraferna stabs its blade into the ground, sending a shockwave out from itself. Each other creature within 120 feet must make a {@dc 22} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Force damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: The Zohtraferna swings its tail, attempting to hit any creatures near it. Each other creature within 15 feet must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. If the Zohtraferna is in its Unveiled Form, the creature takes an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Fire damage.

action.name: Sea of Flame (Unveiled Form Only; {@recharge 5})
entries: The Zohtraferna releases a burst of flame, scorching everything around it. Each creature in a 60-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on the Zohtraferna must make a {@dc 22} Dexterity Saving Throw. Flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning. On a failure, a creature takes 44 ({@damage 8d10}) Fire damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

action.name: Vengeful Bolt (Unveiled Form Only; {@recharge 5})
entries: The Zohtraferna sends a powerful arc of lightning from the tip of its blade. Each creature in a 120-foot-long, 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a {@dc 22} Dexterity Saving Throw. Flammable objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried start burning. On a failure, a creature takes 45 ({@damage 10d8}) Lightning damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

bonus.name: Warping Light
entries: If the Zohtraferna is in an area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, it teleports up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see that is also in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}. It then has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first Attack Roll it makes before the end of the turn.

legendary.name: Tail
entries: The Zohtraferna uses Tail.

legendary.name: Flight
entries: The Zohtraferna flies up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Silent Aura
entries: The area within 15 feet of the Zohtraferna becomes magically silenced until the end of its next turn. Creatures entirely within the area have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Thunder damage and are {@condition Deafened|XPHB}.

legendary.name: Unveiled Actions
entries: If the Zohtraferna's Unveiled Form has activated in the last hour, it can use the options below as Legendary Actions.

entries.name: Energize
entries: The Zohtraferna attempts to recharge its expended Sea of Flame and Vengeful Bolt actions, rolling {@dice 1d6} for each.

entries.name: Destructive Summons (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Zohtraferna forcefully pulls its surroundings toward it. Each other creature within 120 feet must make a {@dc 22} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is pulled 30 feet directly toward the Zohtraferna.
The Zohtraferna then makes one Deceiver's Blade attack against a creature within its reach.

trait.name: Enraging Strikes
entries: Whenever the Morphelean deals damage to a creature with a Weapon Attack, that creature becomes enraged until the end of its next turn. An enraged creature gains a +1 bonus to Attack and Damage Rolls, and it feels an overwhelming urge to attack. If an enraged creature ends its next turn without having dealt damage to a creature with an attack, it is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 hour. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, a creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. The {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} spell, {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell, or similar magic can calm a creature and remove the enraged status.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Morphelean fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Morphelean's Weapon Attacks are magical.

trait.name: Reckless Attacks
entries: When the Morphelean makes an Attack Roll, it can choose to strike without caution, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Attack Rolls until the start of its next turn. If it does, all Attack Rolls made against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Imago Form
entries: When the Morphelean is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its dark exterior cracks, revealing a glowing golden light beneath. If the {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} or {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} spell is cast on it within 1 minute, the curse causing its rage is removed. After the minute passes, the Morphelean emerges as a golden, six-winged Celestial. Its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total resets to 287 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains any expended uses of Legendary Resistance.

If its curse was removed, the Morphelean produces a wave of rejuvenating energy. Each non-evil creature within 120 feet of the Morphelean regains 39 ({@damage 6d12}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and gains the benefits of a {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell.

If its curse was not removed, a wave of destructive light radiates out. Each creature within 120 feet must make a {@dc 21} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 26 ({@damage 4d12}) Radiant damage and is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Morphelean remains Hostile and aggressive. Additionally, the Morphelean can now use the options labelled "Imago Form Only." Award a party an additional 50,000 XP (100,000 XP total) for defeating the Morphelean after its Imago Form activates. Once the Morphelean's Imago Form has activated, it can't do so again until the Morphelean finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Morphelean makes four Frenzied Slash attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Shredding Charge. If the Morphelean is in its Imago Form, it can replace any of its attacks with Divine Beam attacks.

action.name: Frenzied Slash
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 16} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 1d10 + 9}) Slashing damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Necrotic damage. If the Morphelean is in its Imago Form, the Necrotic damage is replaced with 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Divine Beam (Imago Form Only)
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 13} to hit, range 90 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Radiant damage.

action.name: Shredding Charge
entries: The Morphelean moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} in a straight line, moving through other creature's spaces and shredding anything in its path. Each creature whose space the Morphelean enters must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 5 ({@damage 1d10}) Slashing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Necrotic damage and is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. If the Morphelean is in its Imago Form, the Necrotic damage is replaced with 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Radiant damage.

bonus.name: Disengage
entries: The Morphelean takes the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action.

legendary.name: Charge
entries: The Morphelean uses Shredding Charge.

legendary.name: Aura of Violence
entries: The Morphelean chooses up to two creatures it can see within 60 feet of it to afflict with its rage. Each target must make a {@dc 21} Charisma Saving Throw. On a failure, if the target is a Humanoid, it becomes enraged (see Enraging Strikes) until the end of its next turn. A non-Humanoid creature is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the Morphelean until the end of its next turn. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way, a creature spends its turn attacking the nearest creature.

legendary.name: Imago Actions
entries: If the Morphelean's Imago Form has activated, it can use the options below as Legendary Actions.

items.name: Divine Beam
entries: The Morphelean makes one Divine Beam attack.

items.name: Imprisoning Light (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: Beams of light rain down at a point the Morphelean can see within 60 feet of it, filling a 20-foot radius, 100-foot tall {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} with {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} until the end of the Morphelean's next turn. A creature other than the Morphelean that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 21} Charisma Saving Throw or be {@condition Restrained|XPHB} for the duration. While the Morphelean is in the light, it gains the benefits of the {@spell Bless|XPHB} spell.

monster.name: Explodobold Motherlode

trait.name: Explosive Weakness
entries: The Explodobold has these weaknesses:

items.name: Cold Fuse
entries: When the Explodobold takes 12 or more Cold damage in a single instance, it can replace one fewer attack with a use of Bombard when it takes the Multiattack action on its next turn, and its Thunder Volatility weakness doesn't function.

items.name: Thunder Volatility (1/Turn)
entries: When the Explodobold takes 12 or more Thunder damage in a single instance, it can replace one fewer attack with a use of Bombard when it takes the Multiattack action on its next turn, and it explodes violently. Each creature within 10 feet must make a {@dc 14} Constitution Saving Throw. The Explodobold also automatically fails this saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Thunder damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage. On a success, a creature takes half damage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Explodobold fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Eruption Form
entries: When the Explodobold is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the belief of its myriad kobold minions infuses it with almost-deific power. Any conditions on it end, it gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Fire damage, it gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 feet during its turn only, and its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total resets to 110 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

In addition, it immediately explodes. Each creature within 20 feet of the Explodobold must make a {@dc 14} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Thunder damage and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Fire damage, and is pushed 10 feet directly away from the Explodobold.

Award a party an additional 5,900 XP (9,600 XP total) for defeating the Explodobold after its Eruption Form activates. Until it finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it gains the traits in this section. Once the Explodobold's Eruption Form has activated, it can't do so again until the Explodobold finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Light of Belief
entries: The Explodobold sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.

items.name: Explodable
entries: When the Explodobold is hit by an attack that doesn't deal Cold damage, it explodes. It and each creature within 10 feet of it takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Thunder damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage. Creatures other than the Explodobold that take this damage are pushed 5 feet directly away from the Explodobold.

items.name: Fire Aura
entries: At the end of each of the Explodobold's turns, each creature of the Explodobold's choice in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Explodobold takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Explodobold makes two attacks, using Tail or Bowgun in any combination. It can replace up to two attacks with an equal number of uses of Bombard (see Explosive Weakness).

action.name: Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Thunder damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the Explodobold can push the target up to 10 feet directly away from itself.

action.name: Bowgun
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 6} to hit, range 120/480 ft. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d8 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Bombard
entries: The Explodobold detaches an explosive scale, which it fires at a point within 90 feet. Each creature within 10 feet of that point must make a {@dc 14} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Thunder damage and 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Fire damage. If a creature fails the save by 5 or more, it is {@condition Stunned|XPHB} until the start of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half damage.
After the Explodobold uses Bombard, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 10, an {@creature Explodoboldling|HFN2025} appears in the nearest unoccupied space to the chosen point.

reaction.name: Reposition
entries: {@actTrigger} An enemy moves within 10 feet of the Explodobold. {@actResponse} The Explodobold moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Explodobold makes one Tail or Bowgun attack.

legendary.name: Move
entries: The Explodobold moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

mythicHeader: For 1 hour after the explodobold's Eruption Form activates, it can take the following options as Legendary Actions.

mythic.name: Multishot (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Explodobold chooses up to three different targets in a 45-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} and makes one Bowgun attack against each target.

mythic.name: Cannonade (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Explodobold fires a rain of scales in a 60-foot-long, 20-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. At the end of the next turn, the scales explode. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 14} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Thunder damage and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Fire damage. Until the end of its next turn, the target is {@condition Deafened|XPHB} and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved.

trait.name: Avoidance
entries: If the Explodoboldling would take half damage as the result of succeeding on a Saving Throw, it instead takes no damage.

trait.name: Explosive Weakness
entries: The Explodoboldling has this weakness:

items.name: Thunder Volatility
entries: When the Explodoboldling takes 5 or more Thunder damage in a single instance, it loses all charges gained using its Gather {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and explodes. Each creature within 5 feet of the Explodoboldling must make a {@dc 11} Constitution Saving Throw. The Explodoboldling also automatically fails this saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Thunder damage and 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Fire damage. On a success, a creature takes half damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Explodoboldling makes two attacks, using Dagger or Shortbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Bomble.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/240 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Bomble
entries: The Explodoboldling moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} in a straight line. If it collides with a creature or object, its bomble explodes. Each creature within 10 feet of the Explodoboldling must make a {@dc 11} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Thunder damage and 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Fire damage for each charge the Explodobolding gained using its Gather {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and the creature is {@condition Deafened|XPHB} until the end of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half damage only. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} the Explodoboldling loses all charges.

bonus.name: Gather
entries: While within 40 feet of an {@creature Explodobold Motherlode|HFN2025|Explodobold}, the Explodoboldling can gather scales and other detritus shed by the Explodobold into a round object called a bomble. Each time it uses this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the Explodoboldling gains 1 charge (maximum 3 charges). For each charge the Explodoboldling has, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet, and it sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 5 feet.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: If the Chimerina is motionless at the start of combat, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll. Moreover, if a creature hasn't observed the Chimerina move or act, that creature must succeed on a {@dc 18} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check to discern that the Chimerina is animate.

trait.name: Floral Weakness
entries: The Chimerina has these weaknesses:

items.name: Necrotic
entries: If the Chimerina takes 13 or more Necrotic damage in a single instance, it can't take {@action Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Pyrophobia
entries: If the Chimerina starts its turn within 120 feet of a Small or larger open flame, it must make a {@dc 15} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, it can't willingly move closer to the flame until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Forest & Jungle Camouflage
entries: The Chimerina has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Stealth|XPHB} checks made to hide in forest and jungle terrain, as well as deep undergrowth.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Chimerina fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Stinky
entries: While not in its lair, creatures have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Ability Checks made to find or track the Chimerina that involve smell.

trait.name: Venus Trappers
entries: The first time a creature hits the Chimerina with a Melee Weapon Attack on its turn, the Chimerina's flytraps bite at the attacker. The attacking creature must make a {@dc 16} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, the Chimerina disarms the creature of the weapon with which it attacked. If the attack is made with a part of the attacker's body (such as a fist or mouth), the creature instead takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Piercing damage and is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 16}).
A creature within 5 feet of the Chimerina that has a free hand can use an action to make a {@dc 16} Strength check, grabbing a weapon that was disarmed in this way on a success.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Chimerina makes two attacks, using Tarsus Scythe or Acid Spit in any combination.

action.name: Tarsus Scythe
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Acid Spit
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 7} to hit, range 40/120 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d4 + 4}) Acid damage.

bonus.name: Awful Aroma
entries: The Chimerina releases a scent from its rafflesia flower, choosing from the following options. It can't choose the same scent two turns in a row. Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Chimerina is subjected to the scent's effect.

items.name: Serenity
entries: The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Constitution Saving Throw or lie down on the ground, falling {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

items.name: Terror
entries: The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Intelligence Saving Throw or perceive one random object it is holding as something it fears and throws it 30 feet in a random direction.

items.name: Wrath
entries: The target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom Saving Throw or immediately make one Weapon Attack against a randomly determined creature within its reach other than the Chimerina.

reaction.name: Acid Sap
entries: {@actTrigger} The Chimerina takes Piercing or Slashing damage. {@actResponse} Each creature within 5 feet of the Chimerina takes 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Acid damage.

legendary.name: Attack
entries: The Chimerina makes one Acid Spit or Tarsus Scythe attack.

legendary.name: Scent Bloom
entries: One creature within 15 feet of the Chimerina is subjected to one of the scents from the Awful Aroma action (Chimerina's choice when it takes this action). The Chimerina must use a different Legendary Action option before it can use this one again.

legendary.name: Webgility
entries: The Chimerina fires a sticky root at a point it can see within 30 feet of it and pulls itself to that point in a straight line without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. It can pass through the spaces of creatures smaller than it; when it does so, those creatures must make a {@dc 15} Strength Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Vampiacrus fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Lightning Absorption
entries: When the Vampiacrus is subjected to Lightning damage, it regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Lightning damage dealt.

trait.name: Lightning Body
entries: While the Vampiacrus is submerged in water, a creature that touches the Vampiacrus or hits it with a Melee Attack while within 5 feet of it takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Lightning damage. The Vampiacrus sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 100-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for another 100 feet.

trait.name: Lightning Reanimation
entries: A creature that is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by Lightning damage originating from the Vampiacrus, or a creature's corpse that is in the area of an effect originating from the Vampiacrus that deals Lightning damage, becomes a {@creature Vampire Spawn|XPHB} under the Vampiacrus' control. A creature that becomes a {@creature Vampire Spawn|XPHB} in this way has a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet, has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and shares the Vampiacrus' Lightning Absorption and Undead Nature traits.

trait.name: Piranha Panic
entries: If the Vampiacrus drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} outside its resting place, it uses Shape-Shift to become a ghostly {@creature Swarm of Piranhas|XPHB|Swarm of Piranhas} (no action required). If it can't use Shape-Shift, it is destroyed.

While it has 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in this form, it can't return to its true form, and it must reach its resting place within 2 hours or be destroyed. Once in its resting place, it returns to its true form and is {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} until it regains any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, and it regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} after spending 1 hour there.

trait.name: Vampire Weaknesses
entries: The Vampiacrus has these weaknesses:

items.name: Stake to the Heart
entries: If a Large or larger weapon made of wood that deals Piercing damage is driven into the Vampiacrus' heart while the Vampiacrus is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} in its resting place, the Vampiacrus is {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} until the weapon is removed.

items.name: Sunstruck
entries: The Vampiacrus takes 20 Radiant damage if it starts its turn in sunlight. While in sunlight, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Attack Rolls and Ability Checks.

action.name: Multiattack (True Form Only)
entries: The Vampiacrus makes one Bite attack and one Tail Whip attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}20 ({@damage 3d8 + 7}) Piercing damage plus 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Lightning damage. The target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the Lightning damage taken, and the Vampiacrus regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to that amount.

action.name: Tail Whip (True Form Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d4 + 7}) Bludgeoning damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Discharge ({@recharge 5}; True Form Only)
entries: The Vampiacrus erupts in a maelstrom of crimson lightning. Each creature within 30 feet must make a {@dc 20} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 66 ({@damage 12d10}) Lightning damage, and is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the start of its next turn. On a success, a creature takes half damage only.

bonus.name: Shape-Shift
entries: If the Vampiacrus isn't in sunlight, it shapeshifts into a {@creature Swarm of Piranhas|XMM|Swarm of Piranhas}, or it returns to its true form.

While in swarm of piranhas form, the Vampiacrus' game statistics are unchanged except as follows. The Vampiacrus' size is Medium, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0 feet, its {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} is 30 feet, and it can't take any actions, speak, or manipulate objects. It can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and it can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny piranha. It has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage except Radiant damage.

legendary.name: Surge
entries: The Vampiacrus swims up to half its {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

legendary.name: Tail Whip (True Form Only)
entries: The Vampiacrus makes one Tail Whip attack.

legendary.name: Hypnotising Luminescence (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Vampiacrus shimmers in a mesmerising pattern, choosing one of the options from the {@spell Command|XPHB} spell. Each creature within 30 feet must make a {@dc 20} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is subjected to the {@spell Command|XPHB} spell, following the command option chosen by the Vampiacrus.

variant.name: Variant: 2014 Rules
entries: If you are using the 2014 version of the 5e rules, you can make the following changes to the Vampiacrus.

entries.name: Modification: Creature Names
entries: Replace all mentions of {@creature Swarm of Piranhas|XMM|Swarm of Piranhas} with {@creature Swarm of Quippers|MM}.

entries.name: Additional Trait: Undead Nature
entries: The Vampiacrus doesn't need to breathe, eat, drink, or sleep.

trait.name: Dark Exchange
entries: When the Jinn damages a creature with its Leeching Claw attack for the first time on a turn, roll a {@dice d4}. The target gains the corresponding boon, which lasts until the end of the target's next turn. A creature that already has a boon from a Thanatophobia Jinn's Dark Exchange trait can't gain another.
The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its next Attack Roll, Ability Check, or Saving Throw.
The target gains {@dice 2d6} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, which disappear at the end of its next turn.
The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet.
The target gains a +1 bonus to its Damage Rolls.

trait.name: Magic Weapons
entries: The Jinn's Weapon Attacks are magical.

trait.name: Metaphysical Manifestation
entries: The Jinn uses its Charisma in place of its Strength or Dexterity for Attack Rolls.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Jinn makes two Leeching Claw attacks.

action.name: Leeching Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) Slashing damage plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. The Jinn regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage taken.

action.name: Essence Dispersal (1/Day)
entries: The Jinn separates a portion of its own essence into a cloud of bat-like energy. The Jinn takes 4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Force damage and creates a {@creature Swarm of Vitality Bats|HFN2025} for each point of damage taken. Each swarm appears in a space within 10 feet of the Jinn, shares the Jinn's {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, and takes its turn immediately after the Jinn's. Each swarm can move and use its {@action Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless the Jinn takes a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on its turn to command it (see below).

bonus.name: Command Swarm
entries: The Jinn commands all {@creature Swarm of Vitality Bats|HFN2025|Swarms of Vitality Bats} summoned by its Essence Dispersal action to take an action. The action can be in the swarm's statistics or some other action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or object interaction.

reaction.name: Smoke Cloak
entries: {@actTrigger} The Jinn takes damage from a Melee Weapon Attack. {@actResponse} The Jinn disappears into a cloud of ruby smoke, gaining {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the triggering damage and teleporting up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

trait.name: Neurosense
entries: The Mimeflayer knows the exact distance and direction to any creature with telepathy or using magical communication within 1 mile of it.

trait.name: Thought Entity
entries: Without a host, the Mimeflayer can move through objects as if they were {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, and the only action it can take is Subsume Will.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: Spells cast by the Mimeflayer don't require Verbal or Material components, and any effects are invisible except to the Mimeflayer, a creature with {@sense Truesight}, or a creature under the effect of the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Mimeflayer makes two attacks, using Psionic Slam or Terrorazor in any combination

action.name: Psionic Slam
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}9 ({@damage 1d8 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage and 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Psychic damage.

action.name: Terrorazor
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 9} to hit, range 60 ft., one target. {@h}23 ({@damage 4d8 + 5}) Psychic damage. The target must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} until the end of its next turn.

action.name: Subsume Will
entries: The Mimeflayer attempts to enter the mind of a creature with psychic abilities, such as telepathy, telekinesis, or others based on GM discretion. One creature with psychic abilities within 30 feet must make a {@dc 17} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a first failure, the target takes 17 ({@damage 5d6}) Psychic damage and is inhabited by the Mimeflayer, the Mimeflayer's previous host body is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} and {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}, the Mimeflayer disappears, the target is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} and loses control of its body, and it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns.

The Mimeflayer now controls the body, but the target retains awareness. The Mimeflayer can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect. If the target takes Psychic damage from any source other than Subsume Will, the Mimeflayer takes an equal amount of Psychic damage. The Mimeflayer's game statistics are the same, except it uses the possessed target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, as well as the target's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution modifiers.
If a creature is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while inhabited, it falls catatonic, becoming the new host body of the Mimeflayer and a creature under the GM's control.

On subsequent failures, the inhabited creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Psychic damage. On a success, the Mimeflayer is ejected from the target's mind and returns to its original host body, and the target is Immune to this Mimeflayer's Subsume Will for 24 hours.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Painted Lady fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Mask of Passions
