entries: The soulstitcher fills the air with a twisting spectral thread that cuts through the soul. {@actSave dex} {@dc 19} Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the soulstitcher. {@actSaveFail} 16 ({@damage 3d10}) Necrotic damage, and the target's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced by the amount equal to the damage taken. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

reaction.name: Garama!
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature the soulstitcher can see within 60 feet of it casts a level 1+ spell. {@actResponse} {@actSave dex} {@dc 19} The triggering creature. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Force damage, and the spell dissipates with no effect.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Growlbear can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Leap
entries: Growlbear's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 35 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 20 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Keen Sight and Smell
entries: Growlbear has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sight or smell.

trait.name: Toxic Mucus
entries: Growlbear is covered in a layer of poisonous slime. {@actSave con} {@dc 14} A creature that hits Growlbear with a melee attack or otherwise comes into direct contact with its skin. {@actSaveFail} The target is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, the target can't benefit from any {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Damage Immunities} and can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Growlbear makes two attacks, using Claw or Noxious Glob in any combination, and uses Inhale.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 3d8 + 5}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Noxious Glob
entries: Growlbear spits poisonous mucus. If it has a creature swallowed (see Inhale), Growlbear can launch that creature as part of the attack. {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 20/60 ft. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) Poison damage, plus 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Bludgeoning damage if Growlbear launched a creature. Hit or Miss: The launched creature takes 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Inhale {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 14} Each Medium or smaller creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} The target is swallowed by Growlbear (it can have up to four Medium creatures swallowed at a time). A swallowed creature is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside Growlbear, and it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Acid damage at the start of each of Growlbear's turns.

If Growlbear takes 15+ damage in a single instance from a creature inside him, Growlbear must succeed on a {@dc 14} Constitution saving throw or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which fall {@condition Prone|XPHB} in the nearest unoccupied space within 10 feet of Growlbear. If Growlbear dies, a swallowed creature is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by him and can escape from the corpse using 15 feet of movement, exiting {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The swallybog can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Gullible
entries: The swallybog has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks.

trait.name: Standing Leap
entries: The swallybog's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 20 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 10 feet with or without a running start.

trait.name: Swamp Camouflage
entries: The swallybog has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) checks made to hide in swampy terrain.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The swallybog makes two attacks, using Blowgun or Trident in any combination.

action.name: Trident
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d8 + 1}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Blowgun
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 3} to hit, range 25/100 ft. {@h}1 Piercing damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Poison damage.

monster.name: Fleadgling
languages: understands Common and Sylvan but can't speak

trait.name: Friendly
entries: The fleadgling has an easy time making friends. An {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} creature that spends 1 minute interacting with the fleadgling becomes {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} towards it.

trait.name: Poor Explorer
entries: The fleadgling lacks survival instincts. It has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks.

action.name: Headbutt
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage.

monster.name: Fluff of Fleas
languages: understands Common and Sylvan but can't speak

trait.name: Fluffbags
entries: Allies in the fluff's space have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The fluff can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the fluff can move through any opening large enough for a Small creature. The fluff can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fluff makes one Headbutts attack and uses Fluff Roll.

action.name: Headbutts
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, or 9 ({@damage 1d10 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage if the fluff is {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB}.

action.name: Fluff Roll
entries: The fluff moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. Each creature whose space the fluff enters for the first time during this move must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is stuck inside the fluff. A stuck target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the fluff and takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of the fluff's turns. When the fluff moves, the stuck target moves with it. On a success, a creature takes half damage, and the target moves to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the fluff. If there is no unoccupied space within range, the target fails the save instead.

A stuck target can try to escape by taking an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, automatically failing if there is no unoccupied space within range. On a successful check, the target escapes the fluff, exiting in the nearest unoccupied space.

trait.name: Death of Justice
entries: When the Arbiter is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it releases the built up energy created from housing both infernal and divine powers within one vessel. Each creature in a 30-foot-{@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Arbiter must make a {@dc 19} Dexterity Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 31 ({@damage 7d8}) damage (see Fires of Judgement trait). On a success, a creature takes half damage.

trait.name: Duality
entries: The Arbiter counts as both a Celestial and a Fiend in addition to being a Construct.

trait.name: Fires of Judgement
entries: Some damage dealt by the Arbiter changes depending on the damaged creature's alignment, as identified in its traits and actions. A good-aligned creature takes Necrotic damage, an evil-aligned creature takes Radiant damage, and any other target takes Force damage.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Arbiter fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Arbiter makes two Flail attacks.

action.name: Flail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}17 ({@damage 3d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage. {@hom} Each creature within 5 feet of the target takes 4 ({@damage 1d8}) damage (see Fires of Judgement trait).

action.name: Searing Smoke {@recharge 5}
entries: Each creature in a 60-foot-{@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Arbiter must make a {@dc 19} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, a creature takes 24 ({@damage 7d6}) damage (see Fires of Judgement trait). On a success, a creature takes half damage. Whether the save is a success or failure, the area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} for creatures other than the Arbiter until the start of the Arbiter's next turn.

bonus.name: Binding Gaze
entries: One creature the Arbiter can see within 30 feet must make a {@dc 19} Wisdom Saving Throw. On a failure, the target takes 11 ({@damage 2d10}) damage (see Fires of Judgement trait), and the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

reaction.name: Burning Soul
entries: The Arbiter extracts soul power to burn in its censer. {@actTrigger} A creature within 60 feet of the Arbiter is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. {@actResponse} If the triggering creature is alive, it fails a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB}. The Arbiter regains 9 ({@damage 2d8}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and its Searing Smoke action recharges if it is expended.

trait.name: Healing Salve
entries: Over the course of 1 minute, the Shermshroom can apply a restorative salve of mycelium to a creature within its reach. The creature regains 9 ({@damage 2d8}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or loses the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition (Shermshroom's choice). Once the Shermshroom has applied this salve, it can't do so again until it spends 1 hour preparing a new one.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Shermshroom can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an Ability Check.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the Shermshroom has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Ability Checks and Attack Rolls.

action.name: Mallet
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Spore Song
entries: The Shermshroom rings its bell and sings a song, releasing a cloud of faint spores in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Shermshroom, which lasts until the start of its next turn. When it uses this action, the Shermshroom chooses one of the following songs, which determine the effect of the spores:

items.name: Song of Clarity
entries: Allies within the area are Immune to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions. Any existing effect causing an ally to be {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} is suppressed on the ally while in the area.

items.name: Song of Energy
entries: Any ally that starts its turn within the area has its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increased by 5 feet until the end of its turn.

items.name: Song of Light
entries: The area is filled with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}.

items.name: Song of Vigor
entries: Any {@variantrule Bloodied|XPHB} ally that starts its turn within the area regains 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

trait.name: Chakram Specialist
entries: After a chakram thrown by the Poshengu hits or misses, and after any ricochets caused by its Ricochet {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, the chakram returns to the Poshengu.

trait.name: Leap
entries: The Poshengu's {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB} is up to 30 feet and its {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} is up to 20 feet, with or without a running start.

trait.name: Trace the Path
entries: The Poshengu can touch a creature and spend 1 minute focusing to see the path it has traveled, up to a decade into its past. This information only includes the geographical course and the time spent in each location. It doesn't include any information about what the creature said or did along that path. Once the Poshengu has traced a creature's path, it has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on History and {@skill Insight|XPHB} checks related to that creature until the Poshengu finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} or traces the path of a different creature.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Poshengu makes one {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB} attack and two {@item Chakram|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} attacks.

action.name: Quarterstaff
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} decreases by 10 feet until the start of the Poshengu's next turn.

action.name: Chakram
entries: {@atk mw,rw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 30/90 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d4 + 4}) Slashing damage for a Melee Attack, or 7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Slashing damage for a Ranged Attack.

bonus.name: Ricochet
entries: When the Poshengu hits a target with a Chakram attack, it causes the chakram to ricochet, making a Chakram attack against another target within the chakram's range of the original target.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Dragon can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an Ability Check.

trait.name: Stitching Prey
entries: Some of the Dragon's actions cause a hit creature to be threaded. A threaded creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet. A creature stops being threaded when it or a creature within 5 feet of it cuts the threads by hitting them (AC 5) with an attack that deals Slashing damage.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The Dragon ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Dragon makes one Bite attack and one Needleclaw attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage plus 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Needleclaw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait).

action.name: Silk Shot {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 12} One creature within 15 feet. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Poison damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait). While threaded in this way, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Thread Pull
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 12} One threaded creature within 30 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is moved up to 15 feet in a direction of the Dragon's choice and knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} only.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Dragon can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an Ability Check.

trait.name: Stitching Prey
entries: Some of the Dragon's actions cause a hit creature to be threaded. A threaded creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet. A creature stops being threaded when it or a creature within 5 feet of it cuts the threads by hitting them (AC 5) with an attack that deals Slashing damage.

entries: The Dragon ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Dragon makes one Bite attack and two Needleclaw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Needleclaw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait).

action.name: Silk Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 28 ({@damage 8d6}) Poison damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait). While threaded in this way, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Thread Pull
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 15} One threaded creature within 60 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is moved up to 30 feet in a direction of the Dragon's choice and knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} only

entries: If the Dragon fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

entries: The Dragon can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an Ability Check.

A creature stops being threaded when it or a creature within 5 feet of it cuts the threads by hitting them (AC 5) with an attack that deals Slashing damage.

entries: The Dragon ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Dragon makes one Bite attack and two Needleclaw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 10} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 2d10 + 5}) Piercing damage plus 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage.

action.name: Needleclaw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d8 + 6}) Piercing damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait).

action.name: Silk Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 18} Each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 35 ({@damage 10d6}) Poison damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait). While threaded in this way, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Thread Pull
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 18} One threaded creature within 60 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is moved up to 30 feet in a direction of the Dragon's choice and knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} only.

legendary.name: Pounce
entries: The Dragon moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Needleclaw attack.

legendary.name: Puppeteer
entries: The Dragon moves a threaded creature up to 30 feet in any direction. The creature then makes one Melee Weapon Attack against a target of the Dragon's choice within range. The Dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Strangling Silk
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 18} Each threaded creature within 60 feet of the Dragon. {@actSaveFail} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Slashing damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The Dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Dragon fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Spider Climb
entries: The Dragon can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an Ability Check.

trait.name: Stitching Prey
entries: Some of the Dragon's actions cause a hit creature to be threaded. A threaded creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet. A creature stops being threaded when it or a creature within 5 feet of it cuts the threads by hitting them (AC 5) with an attack that deals Slashing damage.

trait.name: Web Walker
entries: The Dragon ignores movement restrictions caused by webbing, and it knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Dragon makes one Bite attack and two Needleclaw attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 14} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}29 ({@damage 4d10 + 7}) Piercing damage plus 22 ({@damage 4d10}) Poison damage.

action.name: Needleclaw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 15} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}21 ({@damage 3d8 + 8}) Piercing damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait).

action.name: Silk Breath {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 21} Each creature in a 90-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 42 ({@damage 12d6}) Poison damage, and the target is threaded (see Stitching Prey trait). While threaded in this way, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Thread Pull
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 21} One threaded creature within 120 feet. {@actSaveFail} The target is moved up to 60 feet in a direction of the Dragon's choice and knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB} only.

legendary.name: Pounce
entries: The Dragon moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and it makes one Needleclaw attack.

legendary.name: Puppeteer
entries: The Dragon moves a threaded creature up to 30 feet in any direction. The creature then makes one Melee Weapon Attack against a target of the Dragon's choice within range. The Dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

legendary.name: Strangling Silk
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 21} Each threaded creature within 90 feet of the Dragon. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) Slashing damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The Dragon can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
entries: If the Chrysalich fails a Saving Throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

trait.name: Puppet Protection
entries: So long as the Chrysalich has an active puppet, it has {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} 21 and {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage types, and it reduces each instance of damage to its cocoon by 20.

trait.name: Reforming Cocoon
entries: If the Chrysalich's cocoon is destroyed, it can spend {@dice 1d10} days forming a new cocoon around an unharmed corpse it had wrapped before its cocoon was destroyed. The new cocoon regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and appears in the location of the transformed corpse.

trait.name: Silk Puppet
entries: The Chrysalich's puppet is an extension of the Chrysalich and isn't considered a separate creature, but has its own pool of 50 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} when it's created and can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. It uses the Chrysalich's statistics, except it has a Dexterity score of 20 and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 35 feet. The Chrysalich can take actions through the puppet and expend movement to move it. If the puppet is ever more than 10 miles from the Chrysalich, it immediately decays and is destroyed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Chrysalich makes four Needle attacks through its puppet.

action.name: Needle (Puppet Only)
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 11} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 2d8 + 5}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Necrotic Burst
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 19} Each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Chrysalich or its puppet (Chrysalich's choice). {@actSaveFail} 21 ({@damage 6d6}) Necrotic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Create Puppet
entries: If the Chrysalich doesn't have a puppet, it loses 25 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and creates a puppet which appears in an unoccupied space within 60 feet of it.

bonus.name: Unstitch (Puppet Only)
entries: When the Chrysalich's puppet hits with a Needle attack, the Chrysalich imbues the needle with arcane power, dealing an extra 13 ({@damage 2d12}) Necrotic damage to the target.

reaction.name: Escape Line
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature makes an attack targeting the Chrysalich's puppet. {@actResponse} The puppet moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, potentially moving out of the attack's range and causing the attack to miss.

legendary.name: Scatterstitch
entries: {@actSave str} {@dc 19} Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Chrysalich or its puppet (Chrysalich's choice). {@actSaveFail} The target is moved 15 feet directly away from the Emanation's source, and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Slipstitch
entries: The Chrysalich's puppet moves up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}, then makes one Needle attack.

legendary.name: Create Puppet (Costs 2 Uses)
entries: The Chrysalich uses Create Puppet.

trait.name: Flyby
entries: The Captain doesn't provoke an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

trait.name: Hive Commander
entries: At the start of each of the Captain's turns, it chooses either Attack Stance or Defence Stance. In Attack Stance, it scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. In Defence Stance, it has a +2 bonus to AC.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Captain makes two Stinging Rapier attacks.

action.name: Stinging Rapier
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage. {@actSave con} {@dc 15} One creature hit by this attack. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Poison damage, and the target is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Attack Order
entries: The Captain summons one {@creature Swarm of Insects|XMM} in an unoccupied space it can see within 5 feet. The swarm takes its turn immediately after the Captain on the same {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, and it obeys the Captain. A swarm disappears after 1 minute, if it's reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or if it's ever more than 300 feet away from the Captain.

bonus.name: Defend Order
entries: The Captain sprays out a cloud of swarmlings in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Captain that lasts until the start of its next turn. When an enemy of the Captain enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Piercing damage.

trait.name: Inviting Vines
entries: The plant life surrounding the Mosslax inhibits creatures moving away from it. Creatures of the Mosslax's choice within 60 feet of it must expend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot moved away from the Mosslax while on the ground.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Mosslax makes two Crushing Arm attacks.

action.name: Crushing Arm
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 12} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}26 ({@damage 3d12 + 7}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 20}) by one of two arms.

action.name: Body Slam
entries: The Mosslax leaps into the air and lands in a space within 20 feet of it, attempting to crush anything beneath it. {@actSave dex} {@dc 20} Each creature in the Mosslax's destination space. {@actSaveFail} 39 ({@damage 6d12}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}, is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, and unable to breathe until it no longer shares a space with the Mosslax. {@actSaveSuccess} The target escapes and moves to the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of the Mosslax.
A creature can take an action to make a {@dc 20} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check, freeing itself or another creature trapped under the Mosslax within its reach on a success.

reaction.name: Eat Anything
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature within 10 feet of the Mosslax attacks it with a melee weapon. {@actResponse} {@actSave str} {@dc 20} The triggering creature. {@actSaveFail} The target is disarmed, and the Mosslax eats its weapon. If the weapon is nonmagical, the Mosslax regains 11 ({@damage 1d8 + 7}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and the weapon is destroyed.

trait.name: Elements of Divinity
entries: The Jinn's form is built from four ceremonial jars. Each jar depicts the visage of a different god and is associated with a damage type:
Each jar is positioned at a different location on the Jinn: head, body, left arm, and right arm. The Jinn has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the damage type associated with its body's jar.

trait.name: Resonant Vessels (Except While in Shattered Form)
entries: The Jinn's jars vibrate violently when exposed to loud sounds, disrupting the flow of its power. If the Jinn takes 20+ Thunder damage on a single turn, the Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, and Radiant damage it deals is halved until it finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

trait.name: Shattered Form
entries: When the Jinn is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or it takes 35+ Bludgeoning damage on a single turn, the vessels containing it shatter, leaving their shards to swirl in a cloud of dust. The Jinn's current Hit Point total resets to 127 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, its creature type changes from Construct to Undead, its {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB|AC} becomes 15, it gains {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, it loses its Resonant Vessels trait, it can't use its Blast Off {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and it gains the Ceramic Whirlwind trait until the Jinn finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Award a party an additional 7,200 XP (14,400 XP total) for defeating the Jinn after its Shattered Form activates.
Once the Jinn's Shattered Form has activated, it can't do so again until the Jinn finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

trait.name: Ceramic Whirlwind
entries: The Jinn can move through a space as narrow as 1 foot without expending extra movement to do so, and it can move through the spaces of other creatures. When it moves through the space of another creature for the first time on a turn, that creature is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15}. {@actSaveFail} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Slashing damage and 13 ({@damage 3d8}) damage of the type associated with the Jinn's body (see Elements of Divinity trait).

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Jinn makes one Left Fist attack and one Right Fist attack.

action.name: Left Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Bludgeoning plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) damage of the type associated with its left arm (see Elements of Divinity trait).

action.name: Right Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Bludgeoning plus 9 ({@damage 2d8}) damage of the type associated with its right arm (see Elements of Divinity trait).

action.name: Wrathful Gaze
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}. {@actSaveFail} 18 ({@damage 4d8}) damage of the type associated with its head (see Elements of Divinity trait). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

bonus.name: Rearrange
entries: The Jinn rearranges its jars, assigning one to each part of its form.

bonus.name: Blast Off (Except While in Shattered Form)
entries: The Jinn gains a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet until the end of its turn. {@actSave dex} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Jinn when it takes this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) damage of the type associated with its body (see Elements of Divinity trait).

trait.name: Lost in Light
entries: The Skyslumbre is {@condition Invisible|XPHB} except when in moonlight or during a solar or lunar eclipse.

trait.name: Undetectable
entries: The Skyslumbre is {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to Divination magic and any spell or effect that would detect its thoughts, emotions, or location.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The Skyslumbre requires no spell components to cast the following spells.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Skyslumbre makes one Bite attack and one Constrict attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}16 ({@damage 3d8 + 3}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Constrict
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 14}). Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, and the Skyslumbre can't constrict another target.

action.name: Moonlit Glow {@recharge 5}
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Skyslumbre. {@actSaveFail} The target falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} for 10 minutes, until it takes damage, or until another creature within reach of it takes an action to wake it.

variant.name: Skyslumbre
entries: If you are using the 2014 version of the 5e rules, the skyslumbre has the following changes.

entries.name: Additional Trait: Magic Weapons
entries: The skyslumbre's weapon attacks are magical

trait.name: Frost Aversion
entries: If the Giblin takes 5+ Cold damage in a single instance, it can't use Rocket Charge until the end of its next turn.

trait.name: Lava Swimmers
entries: The Giblin can swim in lava using its {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB}.

trait.name: Rough Skin
entries: A creature that touches the Giblin with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Giblin makes two Punch attacks.

action.name: Chomp
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the Giblin chomps down on the target, attaches to it, and moves with it. The Giblin detaches from the target at the start of its next turn. The Giblin detaches early if it is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or moved more than 5 feet from the target. When the Giblin detaches, the target takes 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Piercing damage.

action.name: Punch
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Rocket Charge {@recharge 5}
entries: The Giblin launches itself 50 feet in a straight line. {@actSave dex} {@dc 13} Each creature in the Giblin's path. {@actSaveFail} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the creature is Medium or smaller, it is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

If no creatures fail the save or if the Giblin's movement ends early due to a wall or other obstruction, the Giblin crashes, taking 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage and knocking itself {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

trait.name: Lightning Body (Recharges after Using Thunderous Steps)
entries: When a creature hits the Matriarch for the first time on a turn with a melee weapon attack using a metal weapon, the Matriarch discharges, and the attacking creature takes 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Lightning damage and drops its weapon. A creature that is {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to Lightning damage does not drop its weapon.

trait.name: Thunderous Steps (1/Turn)
entries: When the Matriarch moves 30+ feet in a straight line, she creates a burst of thunder until she stops moving. {@actSave con} {@dc 15} Each creature within 20 feet of the Matriarch's path. {@actSaveFail} 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Thunder damage.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Matriarch makes one Charged Tail attack and two Storm Kick attacks.

action.name: Charged Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is wearing metal armour, it takes an extra 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Storm Kick
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}11 ({@damage 2d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage plus 4 ({@damage 1d8}) Lightning damage, and the target is pushed up to 10 feet straight away from the Matriarch.

action.name: Discharge (Recharges after Using Thunderous Steps)
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Lightning damage. {@actSaveFailBy 5} The target is {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} until the start of its next turn. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

trait.name: Shared Charge (1/Turn)
entries: A creature mounted on the Zaptor has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and when the rider hits another creature with a metal weapon, both it and the target take an extra 7 ({@damage 2d6}) Lightning damage as part of the attack.

trait.name: Thunderous Steps (1/Turn)
entries: When the Zaptor moves 30+ feet in a straight line, it creates a burst of thunder until it stops moving. {@actSave con} {@dc 12} Each creature within 20 feet of the Zaptor's path that isn't mounted on the Zaptor. {@actSaveFail} 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Thunder damage.

action.name: Charged Tail
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 2d4 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is wearing metal armour, it takes an extra 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Lightning damage.

action.name: Storm Kick
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Lightning damage, and the target is pushed 5 feet straight away from the Zaptor.

action.name: Discharge (Recharges after Using Thunderous Steps)
entries: {@actSave dex} {@dc 12} Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Lightning damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

trait.name: Biologic Targeting
entries: Damage from the Nucleye's Strand Rays ignores {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Form Change
entries: At the beginning of each of its turns, the Nucleye can choose to change its form to one of the following, gaining the benefits until it changes to a different form:

items.name: Attack
entries: The Nucleye gains a +1 bonus to the save DC of its Strand Rays and it rerolls any 1s on the damage dice of those effects.

items.name: Defense
entries: The Nucleye gains {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

items.name: Speed
entries: The Nucleye's {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases by 15 feet, and it doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.

trait.name: Sickening Growths
entries: A creature within 30 feet of the Nucleye is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} while it has a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to or greater than half its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point maximum}, and dies if it reaches a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to or greater than its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point maximum}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Nucleye uses Strand Rays twice.

action.name: Inject
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., an {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} creature or corpse that died within the last minute. {@h}4 ({@damage 1d4 + 2}) Piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}. If the target is a corpse, or if the target dies while {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, a new nucleye emerges from its corpse 1 week later. Destroying the corpse with fire prevents this.

action.name: Ram
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 2d6}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Strand Rays
entries: The Nucleye randomly shoots one of the following magical rays at a target it can see within 120 feet of itself (roll {@dice 1d6}; reroll if the Nucleye has already used that ray during this turn).

items.name: Radiation Ray
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Radiant damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

items.name: Psychic Ray
entries: {@actSave int} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 4d4}) Psychic damage, and the target immediately uses its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} in a direction of the Nucleye's choice. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

items.name: Mutating Ray
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} The target gains 16 ({@damage 4d6}) {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

items.name: Acceleration Ray
entries: {@actSave wis} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} The target is under the effects of the {@spell Haste|XPHB} spell until the end of its next turn, after which the spell ends.

items.name: Infection Ray
entries: The Nucleye casts the {@spell Pestilence|HFN2025} spell on the target ({@dc 16}).

items.name: Draining Ray
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 16}. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage and the Nucleye regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

bonus.name: Mutative Cone
entries: The central eye of the Nucleye radiates mutating energy in a 90-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Until the start of the Nucleye's next turn, a creature other than a nucleye that starts its turn in the area or enters it for the first time on a turn gains 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. While a creature is within the Nucleye's cone, {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} that it receives from any source are added to any that it already has, and its new total {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} last 1 minute regardless of any previous duration.

legendary.name: Strand Ray
entries: The Nucleye uses Strand Rays

trait.name: Flash Fire
entries: Whenever the Teneblaze is subjected to 5+ Fire damage, it recharges its Blaze Bright {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Teneblaze makes six Bitter Blade attacks.

action.name: Bitter Blade
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d6 + 4}) Fire damage plus 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Necrotic damage, and the Teneblaze gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage dealt. The attack deals an extra 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Necrotic damage for each time the target was hit by a Bitter Blade attack this turn, up to a maximum of 7 ({@damage 3d4}).

bonus.name: Phantom Step
entries: While in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, the Teneblaze teleports up to 20 feet to an unoccupied space it can see that is also in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. If it appears within 5 feet of a creature, the first attack roll it makes against that creature until the end of its turn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

reaction.name: Blaze Bright (Recharge: See Flash Fire Trait)
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature within 10 feet of the Teneblaze targets the Teneblaze with an attack. {@actResponse} {@actSave con} {@dc 14} The attacking creature. {@actSaveFail} The target is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Magical Empath
entries: The Empathrem is always under the effects of the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} and {@spell Detect Emotion|HFN2025} spells, both of which detect creatures out to a range of 120 feet.

trait.name: Telepathic Shroud
entries: The Empathrem's thoughts can't be read by any means, and magic can't detect its location or observe it remotely.

trait.name: Violent Suppression
entries: The Empathrem reacts aggressively to nearby creatures that don't keep their emotions in check. {@actSave wis} {@dc 15} Each creature in a 120-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Empathrem at the start of each of the Empathrem's turns. A creature that is unaware of the Empathrem's presence makes the saving throw with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and a creature under the effects of the {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} spell makes it with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. A creature in a {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian|XPHB|1|XPHB} loses {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to this effect and automatically fails saving throws against it. {@actSaveFail} The Empathrem has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against the target for 1 minute, and the target automatically fails the saving throws against other empathrems' Violent Suppression traits for 1 hour. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the effects of any Empathrem's Violent Suppression for 1 hour.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Empathrem makes two Pummel attacks.

action.name: Pummel
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 5} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d6 + 3}) Bludgeoning damage. A target hit by two Pummel attacks on the same turn has its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved and can't speak until the end of its next turn.

reaction.name: Panic
entries: {@actTrigger} The Empathrem takes Psychic damage. {@actResponse} The Empathrem makes one Pummel attack against a creature within reach and moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} away from the source of the damage.

monster.name: Kougaï
languages: the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Back for Seconds
entries: If the Kougaï is destroyed, it reforms and regains all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in {@dice 3d10} days unless it was fed a comfort food it loved in life within 24 hours before its death. The new body appears in a location within 1 mile of where it died.

trait.name: Endless Hunger
entries: If damage reduces the Kougaï to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it is reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. This trait doesn't activate if the Kougaï has taken Fire damage since the start of its last turn.

trait.name: Keen Senses
entries: The Kougaï has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on smell.

trait.name: Spirit Weapons
entries: The Kougaï can't be disarmed.

trait.name: Lantern Shell
entries: Fire burning inside the Kougaï's shell destabilizes its connection to its carapace and makes the spaces between the plates glow brightly. When the Kougaï takes 5+ Fire damage in a single instance, the next attack roll made against it before the end of its next turn has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Kougaï makes one Pincer attack and two Spirit Kama attacks.

action.name: Pincer
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}8 ({@damage 1d10 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 14}) by one of two pincers. Until the grapple ends, the target is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} and the Kougaï can't use that pincer to attack other targets.

action.name: Spirit Kama
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 6} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}7 ({@damage 1d8 + 3}) Necrotic damage.

action.name: Consume Vitality {@recharge 6}
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 14} One creature the Kougaï is grappling. {@actSaveFail} The target is reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

bonus.name: Hungry Eyes
entries: The Kougaï raises its pincers in an intimidating imitation of eyes. {@actSave wis} {@dc 14} Each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Kougaï that can see it. {@actSaveFail} The target is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of the Kougaï and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

monster.name: Shahoko

trait.name: Aqueous Affinity
entries: The Shahoko can breathe air and water.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Shahoko makes three attacks, using Hydrocannon or Leg in any combination.

action.name: Hydrocannon
entries: The Shahoko fires a blast of pressurized water out of its claw. {@atk rw} {@hit 9} to hit, range 90/360 ft. (180/720 ft. if the Shahoko is submerged in at least 5 feet of water), one target. {@h}14 ({@damage 3d6 + 4}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Leg
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 9} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Piercing damage.

bonus.name: Aqua Jet
entries: The Shahoko uses a blast of water to propel itself up to 20 feet in a direction of its choice without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. If the Shahoko is submerged in at least 5 feet of water, it moves up to 40 feet instead.

bonus.name: Shell Shield
entries: The Shahoko guards itself with its shielded claw. It gains a +3 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn or until it is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

legendaryHeader: Immediately after another creature's turn, the shahoko can expend a use to take one of the following actions. The shahoko regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns.
When the Shahoko takes aim with one of the following Legendary Actions, the point it targets glows with faint but noticeable magic until the effect occurs.

legendary.name: Dark Pulse
entries: The Shahoko takes aim at a point within 60 feet of it. At the end of the next enemy's turn, a blast of necrotic energy fills a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on that point. {@actSave con} {@dc 17} Each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Necrotic damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The area is filled with magical darkness until the end of the Shahoko's next turn.

legendary.name: Draconic Pulse
entries: The Shahoko takes aim at a point within 60 feet of it. At the end of the next enemy's turn, an elemental explosion fills a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on that point. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17} Each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 14 ({@damage 4d6}) Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison damage (Shahoko's choice when it takes this action). {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

legendary.name: Forceful Pulse
entries: The Shahoko takes aim at a point within 120 feet of it. At the end of the next enemy's turn, a concussive blast fills a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on that point. {@actSave str} {@dc 17} Each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Force damage, and the target is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage only.

trait.name: Blazing Rage
entries: Whenever the Enkon takes 20+ damage in a single instance, its rage level increases by one, up to a maximum of level 5. The die size of the Enkon's Fire damage increases with its rage level: d4s at level 1, d6s at level 2, d8s at level 3, d10s at level 4, and d12s at level 5. The Enkon's rage decreases by one level, to a minimum of level 1, if the {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} spell is cast on it or when it goes 24 hours without taking damage.

trait.name: Endless Fury
entries: If damage reduces the Enkon to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it makes a Charisma saving throw (DC 5 plus the damage taken) unless the damage is from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. The Enkon adds its rage level to the total of its roll. On a successful save, the Enkon drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

trait.name: Flame Body
entries: A creature that touches the Enkon or hits it with a melee attack while within 5 feet of it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4;1d6;1d8;1d10;1d12|}) Fire damage (see Blazing Rage trait).

trait.name: Illumination
entries: The Enkon sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Enkon makes two attacks, using Flaming Fist or Fiery Fling in any combination.

action.name: Flaming Fist
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 8} to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. {@h}12 ({@damage 2d6 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4;2d6;2d8;2d10;2d12}) Fire damage (see Blazing Rage trait).

action.name: Fiery Fling
entries: The Enkon ignites a piece of debris and throws it. {@atk rw} {@hit 8} to hit, range 40/120 ft., one target. {@h}10 ({@damage 2d4 + 5}) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4;2d6;2d8;2d10;2d12}) Fire damage (see Blazing Rage trait).

hp.special: 35 (Arboreal only) or 25 (Floral or Herbal only) + 5 for each spell slot level above 2

trait.name: Remedy (Herbal Only)
entries: Creatures within 5 feet of the spirit have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, and {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} conditions.

trait.name: Rooted Fortification (Arboreal Only)
entries: If the spirit ends its turn without using any movement, it gains a +2 bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The spirit makes a number of attacks equal to half this spell's level (rounded down).

action.name: Lash
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d8 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Slashing damage.

action.name: Poison Thorns (Floral Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hitYourSpellAttack Bonus equals your spell attack modifier}, reach 5 ft. {@h}{@damage 1d8 + 3 + summonSpellLevel} Piercing damage. {@actSave con} DC equals your spell save DC, a creature hit by this attack. {@actSaveFail}The target is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. {@actSaveSuccess} The target automatically succeeds on saves against this spirit's Poison Thorns for 24 hours.

hp.special: 25 + 5 for each spell slot level above 2
35 + 5 for each spell slot level above 2
25 + 5 for each spell slot level above 2

trait.name: Life Force
entries: When the swarm is created, it gains 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

trait.name: Swarm
entries: The swarm can occupy another creature's space and vice versa, and the swarm can move through any opening large enough for a Tiny bat.

trait.name: Will of the Master
entries: The swarm is connected to a master that controls it. If the swarm's master is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, the swarm immediately dies in a puff of smoke.

action.name: Latch On
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d4 + 3}) Piercing damage, and the swarm attaches to the target. While attached, the swarm moves with the target and can't be removed unless it chooses to detach itself by spending 5 feet of movement. The swarm has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls made against a creature it's attached to.

action.name: Leech
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft., one creature that the swarm is attached to. {@h}9 ({@damage 2d8}) Necrotic damage. If the target is a CR 1+ creature, the swarm's master gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage taken.

monsterFluff.name: Nergigoblin
entries: The Blackspine goblin tribes give all goblinkind a bad name... well, a worse name. Violent, cruel, and mercilessly sadistic, these goblins have forgone their ancestral pantheon to worship a legendary black dragon, whose power they claim rivals that of the very gods. This new faith commands its followers to indulge in their murderous impulses without restraint, rewarding the most ruthless among them by transforming them into nergigoblins: living vessels of the dragon's dark will

monsterFluff.name: Devil Jhoblin
entries: One word is often enough to capture the essence of the Brutescale goblin tribes: ravenous. Unlike their more territorial kin, the Brutescales don't settle—no land can sustain their endless hunger for long. Instead, they've adopted a nomadic lifestyle, roaming from one spot to the next in an unstoppable tideof gnashing teeth that devours everything in its path...whether or not it's still moving.

monsterFluff.name: Infernoguma

entries: "{@i We made it—the Hells. Like our benefactor said: getting in was easy. We'll set up camp here in case we need to make a quick getaway through the transplanar portal. If you go exploring, keep these massive stone statues in view. Wait... did they just mov—}"
entries.by: Last entry in the journal Calder of Bokram, found aside a charred corpse

entries: Monolithic sentries of the Hells, infernogumas keep silent vigil over the entrances, treasures, and secrets of infernal domains. These hulking stone sentries—identifiable by their stubby vestigial wings—are typically found guarding interplanar portals and archdevils' palaces.

entries.name: Cursed Guardians
entries: Infernogumas are cursed to exist as statues, becoming ambulant only when interlopers cross their thresholds. Reverting to flesh is little reprieve to them, however, as the scorching heat produced by their own bodies turns every waking second into agony. It is this burning desire that motivates infernogumas into intimidating or eliminating intruders—anything to permit them to return to the cold peace of their stone slumber.

entries.name: Infernal Engine
entries: When threatened, an infernoguma becomes a beacon of hellfire, setting anything it touches ablaze. Charred remains are a telltale sign of an infernoguma's presence and a warning of what awaits would-be interlopers. However, the propensity for infernogumas to throw these corpses as projectiles makes identifying one amidst the umpteen statues guarding an archdevil's treasure chamber a devilishly hazardous task.

monsterFluff.name: Be-Hudra

entries: {@i Ha! You unimaginative fools never considered water as a shield against a beast that wields lightning!}
entries.by: Aquonius the Tritonmage, from inside his water elemental, seconds before a be-hudra's electric breath boiled him alive

entries: Some behir parents, desperate to protect their young from predators, cram their pliable, leathery eggs into crevasses too tight for the clutch to fit comfortably. When the squirming hatchlings clamber over one another to escape the cramped nest, their tails wrap around each other and tie a permanent knot. The few that survive their painful adolescence grow into a tangle of six dragonkin radiating from a tail cluster that resembles a freakish head. The result is a titanic, reptilian rat king too clumsy and self-sabotaging to hunt or fight effectively.

entries.name: Hungry, Hungry Heads
entries: With five other siblings to compete with, no component behir eats enough to sate its hunger. Lifelong malnutrition stunts their growth and forces each to tear apart their prey rather than swallow it whole—giving each neighbour the opportunity to fight for scraps. A fresh meal transforms into a whirlwind feeding frenzy where scraps of meat and gobs of the be-hudra's natural ichor fly in all directions.

entries.name: Congealed Lightning
entries: The constant flailing of six uncooperative siblings irritates the behirs' skin with tiny friction burns and miniscule sores. From the irritated (if superficial) wounds oozes a river of viscous ichor comprised of equal parts blood, sweat, and bile that conducts the be-hudra's natural electricity better than copper or water. When the be-hudra's heads manage to set aside their differences and hunt as one, the secretions they hurl at the target amplify even tiny electrical sparks into a raging dynamo that brings their prey down in seconds.

entries.name: Washed Away
entries: Because their secretions dissolve in water, be-hudras avoid rainy and humid climates, which degrade their already-subpar hunting prowess. Younger ones may even balk at the sound of running water, which Aquonius the Tritonmage discovered shortly before his grisly death. He also discovered that even brief immersion in water can wash off a be-hudra's conductive fluid; his methods included the {@spell Create or Destroy Water|XPHB} spell, the {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} cantrip, and even {@spell Conjure Elemental|XPHB}, whose poorly-timed use led to his untimely death.

monsterFluff.name: Rompopowlbear

entries: {@i Heed my words, colleagues! I will take your owlbear and make it into something more monstrous, frightening... fowl! You will rue the day you ridiculed Dr. Francis N. Stein!}
entries.by: Dr. Francis N. Stein, after being mocked for his creation of the owlboar

entries: After a failed attempt to impress his colleagues by presenting them with the owlboar, Dr. Francis Stein came up with an infallible plan to cement his place as the greatest biomancer in the world. He augmented an inferior biomantic creation, the infamous owlbear, with the gular sack of turkeys and the tongue and of a poisonous frogs, the mad scientist created something entirely new and undeniably hideous.

entries.name: Territorial Turmoil
entries: Created from a desire to prove its superiority over the creatures that inspired it, the rompopowlbear was genetically imbued with an animosity for owlbears and the ability to instinctively seek out their territories. Once a rompopowlbear finds an owlbear lair, it will track down and challenge the resident over control of the area. These clashes are extremely violent, and, much to Dr. Stein's chagrin, generally a toss-up if both parties are equally experienced. Knowing this, rompopowlbears prefer to fight younger, less battle-hardened owlbears in order to claim victory more easily.

entries.name: Puff & Tough
entries: The most striking feature of a rompopowlbear is its multitude of inflatable air sacs, which it uses in a variety of ways: it can puff itself up to intimidate owlbears during a territorial challenge, eject bursts of air through its tail to throw its enemies off balance, or, as a last resort, slowly float away in shameful retreat if a fight against an owlbear takes a turn for the worst.

entries.name: Unexpected Employment
entries: Rompopowlbears' unique pneumatic abilities caught the interest of the entrepreneur extraordinaire Baron Bodrum, who theorised that the bursts of air generated by a rompopowlbear's tail could be profitably utilised in fracking. After a series of successful tests, rompopowlbears are now frequently used to surface oil and other valuables buried deep below the ground, requiring no form of payment other than a juvenile owlbear to do battle with every so often.

monsterFluff.name: Splatacabra

entries: {@i - What do you get when you cross a gelatinous cube, a few dragon bones, and half the dungeon!?}
-{@i  I told you—now's not the time for jokes. Save them for when we're out of here.}
{@i - ... It wasn't a joke. Please turn around before we all die.}

Of the thousands of mighty adventurers who blaze trails through lich-infested catacombs or gold-rich dragon lairs, a few dozen at most have met a splatacabra. These uncanny amalgams only appear in the unsettling quiet that follows the passage of victorious heroes, and so they are instead the bane of spelunkers and scavengers. After the adventurers' footprints fade in the dust and the monsters' blood dries, surviving gelatinous cubes sweep the dungeon clean and absorb the remains... and if the defeated monsters were formidable enough, the ooze begins to evolve.

entries.name: Neural Potpourri
entries: The undissolvable bones of dragons, giants, and other titans create a rudimentary limb structure and the beginnings of a consciousness within the cube. Clashing with the echoes of those unconquerable wills are the multitude of dissolved brains from cowardly vermin that died in the crossfire—bats, rats, imps, and goblins. Fearful instincts and mighty strength swirl within the splatacabra, creating a chaotic consciousness too scattered for mental assaults and telepathy to work (much to Chemburn the Xenobiologist's disappointment, for it stymied his favourite methods of research). The many minds' conflicting instincts cause the splatacabra to shift rapidly between fighting and fleeing, but its body always passively defends itself.

entries.name: Gooey Glue
entries: Low-level scavengers are all too happy to stay far away from a fleeing splatacabra, which happens to be the best way to avoid perishing at its hands. Engaging with the rubble-armoured beast at close range is a fool's errand—attackers find their swords ripped from their hands and their fists glued to the raging monster. Fortunately, precise attacks rip rubble and lost weapons from its translucent limbs, encouraging the wounded and vulnerable splatacabra to flee, interestingly enough, toward the surface of the dungeon, rather than its depths.

entries.name: Semiconscious Wanderlust
entries: With few remaining prospects within its shattered home, most splatacabras leave a few weeks after their creation. They amble around half-remembered traps and manoeuvre through subconsciously recalled passages, moving ever closer to the surface. Gregoire Chemburn and his party exploited this behaviour when lost in a destroyed dungeon by corralling a gelatinous cube into dragon bones and following the resulting splatacabra to the surface. The plan almost worked without a hitch—sticking a little too close to the creature when its mood became spontaneously and terrifyingly violent resulted in a vicious battle that gave the jocular xenobiologist his unfortunate moniker.

monsterFluff.name: Palicopy

entries: {@i Short, bipedal, feline? No, I'm afraid the creatures you described aren't half-gnome, half-catfolk—what you encountered were palicopies. Clever little beings who cook the most scrumptious food. Just make sure to drink lots of coffee after having dinner with them!}

Adorable though they may seem, a sinister secret lies in wait for those who fall asleep near this distant cousin of the mimic. Palicopies are patient hunters that seek out the camps of weary travellers and offer to cook for them, making their soon-to-be victims comfortable, well-fed, and drowsy. Only after their preys' bellies are filled and their minds float in innocent slumber do the palicopies make their move.

entries.name: Cute Cutthroats
entries: Palicopies travel in groups of three to five, using their (relatively) shrewd intelligence to deceive prey several times their own size. Their small size, cat-like disguises, and goofy gestures are all conscious efforts to lower a victim's guard, making them focus less on potential danger and more on the rumbling of their belly.

entries.name: Catnap
entries: The food palicopies prepare for their prey contains catnap, a special spice with a cinnamon aftertaste. The process of creating catnap is an unsavory one—a palicopy consumes grasses, regurgitates the bile, and then grinds it into a fine, narcotic powder. Whoever ingests catnap becomes drowsy within minutes and falls into a deep, peaceful slumber within the hour—making for an easy meal.

entries.name: Nightcap
entries: Palicopies' catnap is a difficult to procure, sought-after ingredient, and it is especially popular among insomniacs due to its somnifacient properties. Attempts to domesticate palicopies to harvest catnap have been met with limited success; the primary method of obtaining the rare spice remains to slay a palicopy and harvest the contents of its stomach.

monsterFluff.name: Darkveld Dracobane

entries: {@i I hunt dragons. That thing hunts dragons. That doesn't make it my prey; that makes us equals. I'm not going anywhere near it.}

With lightning alacrity, a quartet of articulating, scale-covered tentacles seize the darkveld's prey: a red dragon wyrmling. The drake's struggles fade to feeble twitches as its crimson scales pale and crack. Empowered with renewed vigour by the lifeforce of the slain wyrmling, the darkveld sets off to hunt its true quarry: a tyrannical adult red dragon.

entries.name: Fused Origins
entries: Based on the colouration, horn morphology, and immunity to acids, Humperdink posits that this draco-slayer is related to black dragons. Heliana's assertions that "this thing is never where you think it is" combined with its shoulder-positioned tentacles points to a second parental lineage: displacer beasts. The conclusion? Upon taming displacer beasts, biomancers within the Unseelie Court sought to combine two potent predators. While the biomantic fusion was clearly successful, the lack of any records describing domesticated darkvelds reveals a complete failure of the court's beastmasters to tame the dragons.

entries.name: Persistent Predators
entries: Engineered to hunt the strongest and most tenacious prey, darkvelds' digestive systems are vestigial, rendering them immune to poisons and able to pursue prey without stopping to eat and rest. Instead, they derive energy from creatures seized in their tentacles; laden with deer and bears, the darkveld's thundering footsteps can hurtle it across open ground fast enough to outpace a soaring dragon. When their draconic quarry grows tired enough to have to land, the darkveld is ready to pounce.

entries.name: Kinslayers
entries: The sudden death of a dragon indicates the potential presence of a darkveld. The dessicated corpse—surrounded by an untouched treasure hoard—confirms it. Creeping up mountainsides, darkvelds prey on slumbering dragons, using their prehensile tentacles to prevent escape. Despite their strength, they are unwilling to rely on brute force alone; Heliana has personally observed one shatter a clutch of eggs on three separate occasions. The parents, incited to reckless fury at the deaths of their unborn children, are all the more likely to make fatal mistakes in the ensuing savage brawl.

monsterFluff.name: Zohtraferna

entries: {@i Zohfernie was such a fun angel, really! We went out for lunch all the time. It wasn't until they decapitated Aggariel that we realized we'd been dining with a worg in sheep's clothing.}
entries.by: The angel Naivette, speaking with her therapist

entries: The mighty archangel Harrogan, a solar of the highest order, had single handedly destroyed as many Fiends as an entire legion. The pride of the Upper Planes, his soldiers whispered that he was as invincible as the gods themselves. With no hope of removing him in battle, his infernal opponents devised a more subtle approach, one that sent panic through the celestial ranks. Harrogan's body was found cleaved in twain behind the choir hall, his face frozen in a scream that no one ever heard. With nothing but scorch marks and the scent of sulphur, seven more commandant angels were torn to pieces before the zohtraferna was discovered and cast out.

entries.name: Artificial Angel
entries: Originally engineered by the archfiend of avarice, zohtraferna are perfect assassins. Many devils can disguise themselves, but zohtraferna are hidden from even the insightful eyes of Celestials. A cloak of white feathers and a perfectly tailored radiance give the look of divinity, allowing them to walk the Upper Planes openly without fear of retribution. Those who find themselves in the lands of mortals are often worshipped as deities, even as the civilizations that revere them slowly dwindle into ruin. Very few have lived to see a zohtraferna's true form, and those who have done so see their warnings dismissed as madness. After all, why would an angel burn people with hellfire?

entries.name: Fiendish Ambition
entries: After tearing through the heavenly ranks, the first zohtraferna returned to the Lower Planes expecting a hero's welcome. Their arrogance was crushed when their archfiend creator claimed the glory of the event. Filled with anger and betrayal, they made a play for the archfiend's domain but fell to the might of the greater devil. Ever since, zohtraferna have been locked out of their home plane, left to create their own dominions of white ruin wherever they find themselves.

monsterFluff.name: Morphelean

entries: {@i I know you're an angel trapped within a fiend's form, cursed to a life of nothing but violence and rage. If you were me, I think I'd be lashing out too. I understand you. Let me help!}
entries.by: Lady Hope Eterna, about to die trying to prove the power of friendship

entries: Even Celestials are capable of errors in their youth, especially before they grow into the divine wings of adulthood. Unfortunately, nothing pleases a demon more than tormenting the young, naive denizens of the Upper Planes. With a bit of demonic blood, a weak angel can be cursed with the mindless bloodlust and madness of the Abyss, stunting its growth and trapping it in an abyssal form. This cruel curse can be undone quickly but, if left untreated, leads to the angel's transformation into a morphelean.

entries.name: Contagious Frenzy
entries: The older the morphelean, the wider the influence of its curse. Entire regions have been wiped out by a morphelean's presence as its festering presence infuses the ground with every step. Hamlets become tragic ghost towns, peaceful songbirds wage war in the sky, and silent groves eviscerate woodland creatures to water the loam with their blood.

entries.name: Rebirth
entries: Whether as a noble quest to free an angel or a valiant effort to stop chaos and madness, morpheleans often become a target for heroes' adventures. If the outer shell of demonic carapace can be breached, there's a chance to remove the original curse. Those few morpheleans who have been freed from their mental prisons struggle to find a place in a world that they no longer recognize, and coming to terms with the tragedy they've caused can often take many mortal lifetimes.

monsterFluff.name: Explodobold Motherlode

entries: {@i IT REALLY IS ASTONISHING THAT THEY MANAGED TO EVOLVE AT ALL . THE SURVIVAL PERCENTAGE OF THEIR YOUNG IS IN THE SINGLE FIGURES. WHY DO YOU KEEP WINCING? AM I SHOUTING ?}

Large relatives of kobolds, explodobolds are most notable for the glowing scales that adorn their bodies. As time passes, they grow more volatile, occasionally spontaneously exploding. A cavern floor pockmarked by molten craters is a warning you have entered an explodobold's domain.

entries.name: Living Arsenal
entries: The glow of the rapidly-growing, volatile scales that form an explodobold's epidermis is proportional to its age; the more mature the explodobold, the greater the scales' brightness and instability. At breeding age, these scales can shake loose and explode, birthing Tiny explodoboldlings (provided that they survive the eruption). Thus, explodobold ecdysis—the process of shedding—is both a defensive measure and part of their explosive reproductive cycle.

entries.name: Kobold Belief-genuity
entries: As with their kobold cousins, explodobolds are creative problem solvers and artificers whose inventions rarely adhere to the rules of arcanomechanics. Instead, the more one believes in its contraption, the more successfully the contraption functions. As seen with the mechakoboldzilla, this belief can cause apotheosis—the genesis of a god—when concentrated in an individual. One should always tread carefully when battling an explodobold in the presence of its peers.

entries.name: Explodobold Motherlode
entries: Evasive and agile, the explodobold is a skirmisher, relying on its ‘bowgun' to rain destruction from afar. Between it and its enemies is a blanket of its young: explodoboldlings that gather its shed scales like dung beetles gather dung. The difference? This dung explodes and, when gathered in sufficient quantities, turns explodoboldlings into eruptive bowling balls. Defeating an explodobold is hard; reaching it is harder.

monsterFluff.name: Chimerina

entries: {@i Dancing has never been my forté... but a dance with death? I better start practicing my pliés.}

With stealthy stiletto steps, your best chance of spotting a chimerina is the stench of decay that wafts from the flower at its head, a rotcorpse lily. Unfortunately, the area around a chimerina's lair also stinks, which, combined with its natural, undergrowth-like camouflage, makes it almost impossible to find. More often than not, it is the chimerina that finds you.

entries.name: Floral Chimera
entries: Why limit biomancy to fauna? Flora has a whole lot going for it, and it practically feeds itself. Well, this chimerina definitely does. The progenitor of this plant-portmanteau is anyone's guess (ahem Stein ahem). Combining the nasal-nuking aroma of a rafflessia, the blade-biting maws of flytraps, and the acidic sap of a laceleaf mantid, the chimerina is a menace to pollinators and people alike.

entries.name: Mercy Bouquet
entries: The diet of this brutal bouquet is whatever crosses its path. The innate magic of a chimerina's lair creates alluring aromas that mesmerise potential prey. Beetles and bugs, drawn by the scent of rotting meat, buzz lazily into its rafflesia flower, where they are succinctly dissolved and absorbed. Adventurers and animals are coaxed to vulnerable locations where nigh-invisible, weblike strands fall onto them, causing partial paralysis. The chimerina promptly eviscerates incapacitated prey with its razor-sharp legs before feeding them piece by piece to the many mouths of the flytraps at its waist.

entries.name: Fire and Death
entries: A chimerina is not without weaknesses! Burning and decay are essential tools in besting these floral chimerae, and they have an innate fear of the former. A well-fed campfire can keep a chimerina at bay, even when the scent of death is thick in the air, and the chattering of ruby-tongued flytraps echoes from the dark between the trees.

monsterFluff.name: Vampiacrus

entries: {@i She bit WHAT!?}
entries.by: Humperdink, after receiving his vampiric mother's latest care package of "taster phials"

entries.name: Nigh-Invincible Nightstalker
entries: As Humperdink stalked the halls of the Loot Tavern, the schematics of his vampiric mother's latest reckless creation clenched in a white-knuckled hand, he reviewed his options. "Not a speck of sunlight down there... Too far from tritons or sea-elves for their holy symbols... Immobilised in running water? HA!... And there isn't a wooden stake big enough for that thing on the Material Plane!" He later threw a head of garlic across the kitchen, cursing himself for even considering the idea, and went back to darning his socks.

A lurid crimson glow in the distant abyss heralds the approach of the vampiacrus. It moves with the implacable force of a riptide, tearing whales apart in cataclysmic bursts of viscera and sucking the blood-saturated seawater into its iridescent flesh. It is a surreal, pelagic nightmare of blazing, electrified teeth and surging speed—a terror no terrestrial vampire could hope to match.

entries.name: Beyond the Seabottom
entries: The vampiacrus controls an ever-expanding territory marked by shredded whales, desecrated colossal squid, and exsanguinated dragon turtles. The few times the monster has been spotted, it was by sailors fortunate enough to survive the raging hurricane under which the creature appeared to be bathing. Surrounded by bioluminescent thralls of aqueous morphology, it was repeatedly struck by lightning, its blood-red glow growing brighter with each bolt. Scarlet arcs of electricity occasionally pulsed between its enormous form and that of its thrall attendants, who seemed to thrive on the scraps of its electrical discharge.

entries.name: Enthralling Carnivore
entries: The few creatures whose fragile forms survive the vampiacrus' initial blows find themselves spellbound by its shimmering flesh, then drained of life by its electrified teeth. Not long after, electricity surges through the corpse as it rises again as a twisted, twitching vampire spawn. The vampiacrus uses a school of these thralls (often unbreathing cetaceans, decayed sharks, and glowing plesiosaurs) to guard the entrance of its pelagic cave-lairs. There, their master grants them infrequent meals of blood and lightning, keeping their predator senses razor sharp—too sharp. They lash out, desperate and unthinking, at the slightest glimpse of a distant silhouette, and can spend hours gnawing on rusted shipwrecks, rock outcroppings, and even each other's necrotic flesh.

entries.name: Little Red Lights
