items.name: Starry Night
entries: The lair falls into complete {@spell Darkness|XPHB}. Each light source within the lair sheds a maximum of 10 feet of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}.

items.name: Water Lilies
entries: The ground in the lair becomes a 10-foot deep pool of water dotted with lily pads.

items.name: Windblown Field
entries: A strong wind blows through rows of golden grain. Flying creatures of the lady's choice fall {@condition Prone|XPHB}, and no creature can move towards the lady using a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}.

items.name: Disorienting Colours
entries: Painted doors and rooms rendered in perfect perspective make it hard to discern what's real. {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks and {@skill Survival|XPHB} checks made to navigate within the lair have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

items.name: Toxic Fumes
entries: Heavy fumes from oils and pigments fill the lair. A creature that spends 10 minutes breathing the air within the lair must make a {@dc 19} Constitution Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 hour and automatically fails Ability Checks to discern illusions until it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} outside of the lair.

items.name: Pierce the Earth
entries: A golden spectral sword pierces the ground in an unoccupied space of the Mantiroar's choice within the lair. The sword occupies a 5-foot space and lasts up to 1 minute. A creature that moves to a space within 10 feet of the sword for the first time on a turn is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} The target takes 10 Radiant damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage. The sword vanishes when it has dealt a total of 30 damage.

items.name: Ashen Blood
entries: The air within the lair causes wounds to persist. {@actSave con}{@dc 17} Each creature that first enters the lair. {@actSaveFail} Whenever a spell or other effect would restore {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to the target with a die roll, each die is treated as rolling a 1. This effect lasts until the creature finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} outside of the lair.

items.name: Field of Decay
entries: Ash in the air degrades weapons with constant wear and battle damage. A weapon degrades at each dawn while within 1 mile of the lair. When it degrades, a nonmagical weapon takes a -1 penalty to Attack and Damage Rolls, up to -3, which lasts until the weapon is repaired. When a non-Artifact magic weapon degrades, roll {@dice 1d8}. On a 1, the weapon becomes inert, losing all magical properties but remaining magical. These effects last until the magic weapon spends 24 hours more than 5 miles away from the lair.

items.name: Reign of Night
entries: The sky within 1 mile of the lair is perpetually dark regardless of time of day.

legendaryGroup.name: Reef Avatar
lairActions: The Shelf in Regrowth can't take the same action two turns in a row.

items.name: Changing Currents
entries: Water in the lair swirls with small but powerful currents, becoming {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for creatures of the Reef Avatar's choice.

items.name: Purification
entries: Each creature of the Reef Avatar's choice within the lair gains 13 ({@damage 3d8}) {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

items.name: Rapid Reconstruction
entries: A wall of venomous coral springs up from a solid surface within the lair. The wall is 5 feet wide, 10 feet tall, and 20 feet long. If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 17}. {@actSaveFail} 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Piercing damage and the target is {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Polyp Clouds
entries: Restorative clouds of coral prevent disease and decay within the lair. Diseases are suppressed while within the lair, and any spell that specifies that it withers nonmagical plants and vegetation, such as {@spell Blight|XPHB}, automatically fails.

items.name: Clear Water
entries: Though still saline, the water in the lair is extremely clean. A creature that can breathe water regains 1 extra {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} whenever it rolls a {@variantrule Hit Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while in the water.

items.name: Bones to Build
entries: The ground within the lair is covered in the bone-like spikes of old coral growth in the early stages of regrowth. {@actSave con} {@dc 17} A creature that moves 5 feet on the ground in the lair for the first time on a turn. {@actSaveFail} 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Piercing damage and the target is {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

legendaryGroup.name: Draconimbus
lairActions: In the Voidsea, there's only one thing deadlier than a draconimbus: a draconimbus within its lair. Fueled by the creature's primordial fury, the storms that surround its den rage without end— an ill omen to any fool who dares approach, and a small silver lining to all others. After all, a draconimbus in its lair is a draconimbus not chasing their vessel.

items.name: Downpour
entries: Torrential rain falls from the sky. Each creature that is outdoors within the lair becomes {@condition Wet|HFN2025}.

items.name: Gale Winds
entries: Powerful winds fill the lair. Until {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20 of the next round, ranged attacks with weapons made within the lair have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, and creatures other than the draconimbus that fly in the lair must spend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot they move.

items.name: Lightning Strike
entries: Electricity builds up in the clouds in preparation for a powerful discharge. The lair chooses a point on the ground within the lair, which is marked by crackling sparks. On {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 0 of this round, a massive lightning bolt strikes in a 15-foot-radius, 600-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centred on the point. {@actSave dex} {@dc 18}, each creature in the area. {@actSaveFail} 42 ({@damage 12d6}) Lightning damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

items.name: Mutagenic Waters
entries: The mutation level of ocean water within 5 miles of the lair is increased by 1.

items.name: Neverending Storm
entries: Winds within 5 miles of the lair are supernaturally strong. The area is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for Water Vehicles.

items.name: Wind Barrier
entries: A massive cyclone surrounds the lair. A creature that attempts to enter or exit the lair is subject to the following effect. {@actSave str} {@dc 18}. {@actSaveFail} The creature is pushed 60 feet in a straight line in a direction of the lair's choice.

legendaryGroup.name: Growlbear
lairActions: Within the foetid miasma of buzzing detritivores; betwixt curtains of hanging, slimy moss; drowned in a quagmire of stagnant water and improvised traps stands the Hungering Mire. Few would now recognize what was once the home of the esteemed wildshaper: in his final, paranoid weeks he razed the cottage to fashion traps from the dregs of the ruined building. These crude but deadly implements are scattered across the surrounding swamp, lurking just beneath the top layer of muck.

With his newfound cruelty and cunning, Growlbear brought the local swallybogs under his control. To avoid being eaten themselves, the swallybogs spend their days hunting for meat to appease their master's appetite and scouting for coming dangers, and trespassers may spot the corpses of scouts who weren't wary enough to avoid their master's traps.

lairActions.name: {@s GM} Heliana's Tip
entries: The Hungering Mire's Spring Trap Lair Action is intentionally vague to let you make up your own eviscerating effects. Here're some ideas:

items: A pit filled with sharpened bamboo
A bear-trap fashioned from the rent breastplate of a previous adventurer
Darts fletched with brightly coloured feathers that shoot from a nearby tree
Giant, sharpened pelvic bones that swing from branches above.

entries: All these and more are all suitable ways to puncture your player characters. Consider narrating various types of traps set off in the mire to keep your players on edge and make the gameplay varied and interesting.

lairActions: On its turn, the Hungering Mire takes an action to cause one of the following effects. The lair can't take the same action two rounds in a row.

items.name: Swallybogs
entries: Two {@creature Swallybog|HFN2025|Swallybogs} emerge from the muck in unoccupied spaces of the lair's choice within the lair. They are {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} to Growlbear and take their turns immediately after the lair.

items.name: Spring Trap
entries: A trap goes off in the mire. {@actSave dex} {@dc 14} One creature of the lair's choice within the lair. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Piercing damage.

items.name: Monster Mist
entries: A patch of sickening mist appears within the lair in a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} until the start of the lair's next turn. The area is {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}. {@actSave con} {@dc 14} Each creature that starts its turn in the area other than Growlbear or a swallybog. {@actSaveFail} The target is transformed into an amphibious hybrid. A transformed creature gains a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} of 15 feet and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks. The transformation lasts until the creature is targeted by a {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} spell or similar magic.

items.name: Infested Water
entries: Fumes coming off the grub-filled water prevent wounds from closing quickly. Creatures in the lair other than Growlbear can't use {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Shallow Traps
entries: Hazardous traps lie hidden in the muck. A creature that spends 1 hour traveling in the lair or that fails an ability check to navigate the lair is subject to the following effect. {@actSave dex} {@dc 14}. {@actSaveFail} 13 ({@damage 3d8}) Piercing damage.

baseitem.name: Blackpowder Rifle
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB|Smith} or {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}; 15 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 24 hours.

Rifles are long-barreled firearms boasting superior accuracy due to the grooves on the inside of their barrels, which confer spin to fired projectiles. Additionally, they have the {@itemMastery Piercing Shot|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Piercing Shot Superior Strike}{@sup *}.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Chakram
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}; 3 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 6 hours.

Chakrams consist of a circular blade with a sharp outer edge. They count as Monk weapons. Additionally, they have the {@itemMastery Battery Strike|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Battery Strike Superior Strike}{@sup *}.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Claw
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}; 2 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 3 hours.
Claws are Simple, bladed Melee weapons worn as gloves. They also have the {@itemMastery Skewer|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Skewer Superior Strike}{@sup *}.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Gunlance
entries: Gunlances take the form of one-handed polearms with built-in short-range firearms. They also have the Special: Point Blank property and the {@itemMastery Piercing Shot|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Piercing Shot Superior Strike}{@sup *}, with a range of 30 feet.

entries.name: Special: Point Blank
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wielding this weapon, you can prime it for shooting. The next time you hit with a melee attack using this weapon before the end of your turn, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Thunder damage.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Kusarigama
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}; 2 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 4 hours.

Kusarigama are composed of a sickle attached to a heavy counterweight by a metal chain. They count as Monk weapons. They also have the Special: Counterweight property and the {@itemMastery Trip|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Trip Superior Strike}{@sup *}.

entries.name: Special: Counterweight
entries: While wielding a Kusarigama with two hands, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to attack with its counterweight. This attack has a reach of 10 feet and deals {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage on a hit. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of this attack, unless that modifier is negative.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Magitech Rifle
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}; 330 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 120 hours.

Rifles are long-barreled firearms boasting superior accuracy due to the grooves on the inside of their barrels, which confer spin to fired projectiles. Additionally, they have the {@itemMastery Piercing Shot|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Piercing Shot Superior Strike}{@sup *}.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Spiked Cestus
entries: Manufacturing: {@item Smith's Tools|XPHB}; 1 {@item Gold|XDMG|GP} materials; 2 hours.

Spiked Cesti are thick leather straps worn around the knuckles of one hand, armed with sharp spikes to puncture targets. They also have the {@itemMastery Flurry|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Flurry Superior Strike}{@sup *}.
{@note {@sup *}See {@loader Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms|collection/Loot Tavern; Ryoko's Guide to Yokai Realms.json}}

baseitem.name: Magitech Cannon
entries: AC: 17 | DT: 16 | HP: 30 | IT: 18 | Con: +2 | Ability: Dex,Int
Damage Immunities: Poison, Psychic

entries.name: Ball Shot
entries: {@atk r} range 180/360/1,440 ft. {@h}18 ({@damage 4d8}) Bludgeoning damage. This shot has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against Hulls and kaiju at close range.

entries.name: Chain Shot
entries: {@atk r} range —/240/960 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d8}) Bludgeoning damage. This shot has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against masts and other tall, vertical structures (GM's discretion).

entries.name: Grape Shot
entries: {@actSave dex}, 90-foot Narrow Cone. {@actSaveFail} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) Piercing damage. {@actSaveSuccess} Half damage.

entries.name: Spirit Gauge
entries: When you hit an enemy that has a {@filter Challenge Rating of 1/2 or higher|bestiary|Challenge Rating=[&1/2;&Special]} with an attack using this weapon, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}. The weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}, and loses all charges whenever it goes 1 minute without gaining any charges. While it has any charges, the weapon is imbued with flame and sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a {@b 5-foot} radius.

entries.name: Searing Strike
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, roll {@dice 1d4} to determine how you can use the weapon's flames as indicated in the table. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 3 charges} to unleash this burst of fire. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d6} Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again for {@b 1 minute}.

rows: The blade releases a torrent of flame, akin to a dragon's breath, affecting each creature in a {@b 15-foot} {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}.
The sword's tip shoots a jet of fire, affecting each creature in a {@b 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long} {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}.
As you spin the sword, it creates a whirlwind of fire affecting each creature in a {@b 10-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you.

You shoot orbs of flame from the sword at two points within {@b 30 feet} of you, each of which explodes in a {@b 5-foot-radius} {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, affecting each creature in the area.

entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Wind-Up Strikes
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to charge up this sword. If you do, you can't make another attack this turn. The sword remains charged until the end of your next turn, until you drop it, or until you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

When you hit a target with this sword while it's charged, a shower of spikes bursts from its blade. The target and each creature other than you within {@b 5 feet} of the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage. If the original target is a creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw. The sword is then no longer charged.
On the second consecutive round you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 3d6}. On the third and subsequent consecutive rounds you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 4d6}.

item.name: Black Spine (very rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Wind-Up Strikes
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to charge up this sword. If you do, you can't make another attack this turn. The sword remains charged until the end of your next turn, until you drop it, or until you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

When you hit a target with this sword while it's charged, a shower of spikes bursts from its blade. The target and each creature other than you within {@b 5 feet} of the target must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage. If the original target is a creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw. The sword is then no longer charged.

On the second consecutive round you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 3d6}. On the third and subsequent consecutive rounds you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 4d6}. If you hit with a charged attack for the third consecutive round and subsequent ones, the spikes on the sword become metallic, granting you a +1 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Godplume Switchaxe (uncommon)
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to cause its feathers to glow with golden energy and its blade to unfold, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greatsword|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Radiant Build-Up
entries: When you hit an enemy with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Radiant Discharge
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a {@item Greataxe|XPHB} again.

item.name: Godplume Switchaxe (rare)
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to cause its feathers to glow with golden energy and its blade to unfold, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greatsword|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Radiant Discharge

item.name: Godplume Switchaxe (very rare)
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to cause its feathers to glow with golden energy and its blade to unfold, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greatsword|XPHB|Greataxes}. If you turn the {@item Greataxe|XPHB} into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB} while it has 3 charges, you gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of {@b 30 feet} until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Radiant Build-Up
entries: When you hit an enemy with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Radiant Discharge
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Radiant damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a {@item Greataxe|XPHB} again.

entries.name: Wyrmstake
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to make a special melee attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage, and the weapon conjures a large stake that it embeds in the target.

At the end of the target's next turn, the stake explodes in a burst of necrotic energy, forcing the target to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target becomes incapable of dealing Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Brutescale Gunlance (rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Wyrmstake
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to make a special melee attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage, and the weapon conjures a large stake that it embeds in the target.

At the end of the target's next turn, the stake explodes in a burst of necrotic energy, forcing the target to make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Necrotic damage and becomes incapable of dealing Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Brutescale Gunlance (very rare)
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Wyrmstake
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to make a special melee attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage, and the weapon conjures a large stake that it embeds in the target.

At the end of the target's next turn, the stake explodes in a burst of necrotic energy, forcing the target to make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d6} Necrotic damage and becomes incapable of dealing Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage for {@b 1 minute}. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Psionic Link
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon, you can allow its electrified tentacles to pierce your skin and establish a psionic connection with you until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or pull the tentacles out as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. While this connection lasts, you take {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your jump distance is doubled, and attacks you make with this weapon deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: You can enhance your attunement to this weapon by enduring the pain of electrocution. In order to do so, you must take a total of 100 Lightning damage from hostile creatures while attuned to the sword. While enhanced, the weapon gains the Overload property:

entries.name: EA: Overload
entries: When you use the Psionic Link property, you can choose to intensify the link. If you do, you take {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns while the link lasts, instead of {@damage 1d6}. In addition to the benefits of the Psionic Link property, the extra Psychic damage dealt with the weapon increases to {@damage 2d6}.

entries.name: Psionic Link
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon, you can allow its electrified tentacles to pierce your skin and establish a psionic connection with you until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or pull the tentacles out as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. While this connection lasts, you take {@damage 1d8} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage, your jump distance is doubled, and attacks you make with this weapon deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: You can enhance your attunement to this weapon by enduring the pain of electrocution. In order to do so, you must take a total of 100 Lightning damage from hostile creatures while attuned to the sword. While enhanced, the weapon gains the Overload property:

entries.name: EA: Overload
entries: When you use the Psionic Link property, you can choose to intensify the link. If you do, you take {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns while the link lasts, instead of {@damage 1d8}. In addition to the benefits of the Psionic Link property, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence saving throws, and the extra Psychic damage dealt with the weapon increases to {@damage 2d8}.

item.name: Bubblebasher (uncommon)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spin to Win
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to spin it around in a deadly whirlwind, making a melee attack with the maul against each creature within {@b 5 feet} of you. As the weapon is spun, it creates bubbles of oil that slowly fall to the ground. After you make all the attacks, the area within {@b 5 feet} of you other than the space you occupy is covered in a layer of slippery oil as per the {@spell Grease|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 13}) until the end of your next turn.

If the oil or a creature standing on the oil takes Fire damage, the oil ignites. Each creature standing in the area must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 1d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. After the oil ignites, it disappears.

entries.name: Slip and Slide
entries: While holding this weapon, you expend only 1 foot of movement for each 2 feet you move when moving into a space covered in a slippery substance like oil or ice, and you automatically succeed on saving throws and ability checks made not to slip on it.

item.name: Begrappler (uncommon)
entries: The glowing "eyes" set into this greatsword's decorative reptilian skulls are actually fluid-producing glands cut from a be-hudra's skin. One goes dark every time the blade discharges electricity, and all ignite again when its power is replenished.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Conductive Grasp
entries: While holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to launch ichor-coated tentacles toward a creature you can see within {@b 5 feet} of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 13}) until you release the weapon or use it to attempt to grapple or attack a different target.

A creature grappled by this weapon is also covered in ichor for {@b 1 hour} or until it takes Lightning damage. If a creature covered in ichor takes Lightning damage, it rolls an additional 3 Lightning damage dice and adds them to the total damage dealt.

entries.name: Writhing Discharge
entries: You can expend up to {@b 2 charges} when you hit a creature with this weapon to deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage for each charge expended.

item.name: Begrappler (rare)
entries: The glowing "eyes" set into this greatsword's decorative reptilian skulls are actually fluid-producing glands cut from a be-hudra's skin. One goes dark every time the blade discharges electricity, and all ignite again when its power is replenished.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.
While holding or carrying this weapon, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage.

entries.name: Writhing Discharge
entries: You can expend up to {@b 2 charges} when you hit a creature with this weapon to deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage for each charge expended.

item.name: Pin (uncommon)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 4 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Pinprick
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause it to emit a burst of air. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to {@b 10 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Ejector Seat
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to forego one of your attacks and press this weapon into the ground. One Large or smaller creature of your choice on the ground within {@b 30 feet} of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning and be launched 10 feet in a direction of your choice, falling {@condition Prone|XPHB} when it lands.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

item.name: Cushion (uncommon)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. This item has {@b 3 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bouncy Pad
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this shield and on the ground, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to point the shield's air bags toward the ground and inflate it, launching yourself up to {@b 30 feet} vertically into the air, and up to {@b 15 feet} horizontally. If a creature is within 5 feet of where you land, you can immediately make a melee attack with a weapon or Unarmed Strike against that creature as part of this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, and any damage you take from the fall is reduced by an amount equal to twice your level. That amount is added onto the attack's damage.

entries.name: Airbag
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Bludgeoning damage, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inflate Cushion, granting yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that instance of Bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

item.name: Cushion (rare)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. This item has {@b 5 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d4+1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bouncy Pad
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this shield and on the ground, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to point the shield's air bags toward the ground and inflate it, launching yourself up to {@b 30 feet} vertically into the air, and up to {@b 15 feet} horizontally. entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

item.name: Cushion (very rare)
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. This item has {@b 7 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d6+1} expended charges daily at dawn.

If a creature is within 5 feet of where you land, you can immediately make a melee attack with a weapon or Unarmed Strike against that creature as part of this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, and any damage you take from the fall is reduced by an amount equal to twice your level. That amount is added onto the attack's damage.

entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Bludgeoning damage, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inflate Cushion, granting yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that instance of Bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

item.name: Stickaxe (rare)
entries: This weapon has a maximum of {@b 5 charges}, and deals Acid damage instead of its normal type. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to unfold the blade and spew potent glue from its sacs, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Adhesive Buildup
entries: When you hit a hostile creature with an attack with this weapon in {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Slimy Slam
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Acid damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a greataxe again.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the weapon, you can attempt to knock a weapon or other item from a target's grasp. Once on your turn after you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the target to make a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check (its choice). On a failure, the target drops one item it is holding.

item.name: Stickaxe (very rare)
entries: This weapon has a maximum of {@b 5 charges}, and deals Acid damage instead of its normal type. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to unfold the blade and spew potent glue from its sacs, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Adhesive Buildup
entries: When you hit a hostile creature with an attack with this weapon in {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Slimy Slam
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Acid damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a greataxe again.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the weapon, you can attempt to knock a weapon or other item from a target's grasp. Once on your turn after you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the target to make a {@dc 16} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check (its choice). On a failure, the target drops one item it is holding. When a creature fails its check against the Optional {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} property, you can expend 1 charge to {@condition Grappled|XPHB} it instead (escape {@dc 16}). Until the grapple ends, you can't attack another creature with this weapon.

item.name: Life-eater Charge Blade (rare)
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 5 charges}. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to detach the axehead from the blade, turning it into a {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and a {@item Shield|XPHB}, or to recombine the {@item Shield|XPHB} and {@item Shortsword|XPHB} back into a greataxe. You are proficient with this {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}. If the {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} are more than {@b 15 feet} apart from each other for {@b 1 minute}, your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to them ends, and they become magically inert until reunited.

entries.name: Lifeforce Recycler (Shortsword Form Only)
entries: When you hit a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, it gains {@b 1 charge}. This item loses all gained charges 1 minute after it gains its last charge.

entries.name: Life-Devouring Discharge (Greataxe Form Only)
entries: When you hit with an attack with this {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, you can expend any number of charges to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Force damage to the target per charge expended. You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Force damage dealt.

item.name: Life-eater Charge Blade (very rare)
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 5 charges}. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to detach the axehead from the blade, turning it into a {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and a {@item Shield|XPHB}, or to recombine the {@item Shield|XPHB} and {@item Shortsword|XPHB} back into a greataxe. You are proficient with this {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}. If the {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} are more than {@b 15 feet} apart from each other for {@b 1 minute}, your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to them ends, and they become magically inert until reunited.

entries.name: Lifeforce Recycler (Shortsword Form Only)
entries: When you hit a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, it gains {@b 1 charge}. This item loses all gained charges 1 minute after it gains its last charge.

entries.name: Life-Devouring Discharge (Greataxe Form Only)
entries: When you hit with an attack with this {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, you can expend any number of charges to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage to the target per charge expended. You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Force damage dealt.

item.name: Life-eater Charge Blade (legendary)
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with this {@item Greataxe|XPHB} (in either form). You gain a +1 bonus to AC while holding the weapon's {@item Shield|XPHB}, in addition to the {@item Shield|XPHB}'s normal bonus.

This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 5 charges}. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to detach the axehead from the blade, turning it into a {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and a {@item Shield|XPHB}, or to recombine the {@item Shield|XPHB} and {@item Shortsword|XPHB} back into a {@item Greataxe|XPHB}. You are proficient with this {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}. If the {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} are more than {@b 15 feet} apart from each other for {@b 1 minute}, your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to them ends, and they become magically inert until reunited.

entries: When you hit a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, it gains {@b 1 charge}. This item loses all gained charges 1 minute after it gains its last charge.

entries.name: Life-Devouring Discharge (Greataxe Form Only)
entries: When you hit with an attack with this {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, you can expend any number of charges to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage to the target per charge expended. You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Force damage dealt.
In addition, while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 3 charges} to cast the {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} spell.

item.name: Divine Lie (rare)
entries: This weapon has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you deal damage with this weapon, you can choose to deal Force damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 5d8} Force damage.

entries.name: Divine Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself.

entries.name: Heavenly Cloak
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cast the {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 15}). When you cast a spell with this property, you can choose to appear as a Celestial to Divination magic and similar effects for the duration.

entries.name: Consume Light
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Radiant damage while holding this weapon, you can absorb it into the blade, gaining {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage (including against the triggering damage) until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} and {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, both weapons have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. Additionally, when you use the Consume Light property of {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025}, {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge, and when you use the Consume Shadow property of {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge. Once a weapon has regained a charge in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

item.name: Divine Lie (very rare)
entries: This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you deal damage with this weapon, you can choose to deal Force damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 5d8} Force damage.

entries: This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you deal damage with this weapon, you can choose to deal Force damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 6d8} Force damage.

entries.name: Divine Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself. When you use the Divine Step property, you can choose a creature you can see within 10 feet of you immediately before or after you teleport. It must succeed on a {@dc 17} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Heavenly Cloak
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cast the {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} or {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 17}). When you cast a spell with this property, you can choose to appear as a Celestial to Divination magic and similar effects for the duration.

entries: This weapon has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you hit a target and deal damage to it with this weapon, you can choose to deal Fire or Lightning damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 5d8} Fire or Lightning damage.

entries.name: Fiend Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself.

entries.name: Hellish Wave
entries: As an action, you can stab this weapon into the ground and expend {@b 1 charge} to create a radiating burst of flames. Each creature within {@b 20 feet} of you must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Fiend Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself. When you use the Fiend Step property, you can create a {@b 10-foot-radius} Sphere of silence centered on either the space you left or your destination space until the end of your next turn. Any creature or object entirely inside the Sphere is {@condition Deafened|XPHB} and has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Thunder damage, and casting a spell with a Verbal component is impossible in the area.

entries.name: Hellish Wave
entries: As an action, you can stab this weapon into the ground and expend {@b 1 charge} to create a radiating burst of flames. Each creature within {@b 20 feet} of you must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 10d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Necrotic damage while holding this weapon, you can absorb it into the blade, gaining {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage (including against the triggering damage) until the start of your next turn.

Once a weapon has regained a charge in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

item.name: Shadowsheath (rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon can have a maximum of {@b 7 charges}.

entries.name: Sheathed Menace
entries: While this weapon is sheathed and the sheath is visible, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intimidation checks.

entries.name: Divine Fury
entries: Whenever you hit a creature with a weapon attack using the unsheathed sword, it deals extra Radiant damage equal to half the number of charges it has, and then it gains {@b 1 charge} for every 5 damage dealt by the attack. Sheathing the sword resets the charges to 0.

entries.name: Halo of Rage
entries: As an action, you can place the guard above your own head and cast the {@spell Blood Frenzy|HFN2025} spell on yourself. Once you cast a spell using this property, that spell can't be cast with it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This sword is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the sword, keeping it within reach at all times. If the weapon reaches its maximum number of charges, you go into an uncontrollable frenzy for {@b 1 minute}, becoming a creature under the GM's control, during which you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and spend each turn attacking the nearest creature. After the minute has elapsed, the sword loses all charges.

Another creature can end the frenzy early by making a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check (DC equals 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus), sheathing the blade and ending the frenzy on a success.

item.name: Shadowsheath (very rare)

entries.name: Halo of Rage
entries: As an action, you can send the guard of the blade to hover above a willing creature you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you, casting the {@spell Enrage|HFN2025} spell on it (save {@dc 16}). Alternatively, you can use an action to place the guard above your own head and cast the {@spell Blood Frenzy|HFN2025} spell on yourself. item.name: Sanguine Shockshot (rare)
entries: While carrying this weapon, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and when you hit with an attack with it, you can choose to deal Lightning damage instead of Piercing.

entries.name: Bio-Electric Railgun
entries: You can make ranged spell attacks with this weapon, in addition to its normal melee weapon attacks. Each spell attack has a normal range of 100 feet, a long range of 300 feet, {@hit +7} to hit, and deals {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage on a hit. You do not add an ability modifier to this damage.

entries.name: Crimson Flash
entries: You can use an action to release a flash of bioluminescence from this weapon. Each other creature within a {@b 300-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the weapon must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Sanguine Shockshot (very rare)
entries: While carrying this weapon, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and when you hit with an attack with it, you can choose to deal Lightning damage instead of Piercing.

entries.name: Bio-Electric Railgun
entries: You can make ranged spell attacks with this weapon, in addition to its normal melee weapon attacks. Each spell attack has a normal range of 100 feet, a long range of 300 feet, {@hit +8} to hit, and deals {@damage 3d8} Lightning damage on a hit. You do not add an ability modifier to this damage.

entries.name: Crimson Flash
entries: You can use an action to release a flash of bioluminescence from this weapon. Each other creature within a {@b 300-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the weapon must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Sanguine Shockshot (legendary)
entries: While carrying this weapon, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and when you hit with an attack with it, you can choose to deal Lightning damage instead of Piercing.

entries.name: Bio-Electric Railgun
entries: You can make ranged spell attacks with this weapon, in addition to its normal melee weapon attacks. Each spell attack has a normal range of 100 feet, a long range of 300 feet, {@hit +9} to hit, and deals {@damage 3d8} Lightning damage on a hit. You do not add an ability modifier to this damage.

entries.name: Crimson Flash
entries: You can use an action to release a flash of bioluminescence from this weapon. Each other creature within a {@b 300-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the weapon must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Storm Devourer
entries: When a creature within {@b 30 feet} of you takes Lightning damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause it to take no Lightning damage instead. The next time you deal Lightning damage with this weapon, use the highest number possible for each die instead of rolling. This property can be used three times, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

item.name: Graviturgic Warp-hilt (uncommon)
entries: This distorted, unnatural sword hilt can be socketed onto any magical melee weapon, adapting its bizarre geometry to the hilt or handle of its host.

entries.name: Gravity Well
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll using the socketed weapon, you can attack a creature {@b 5 feet} farther than your normal reach with the weapon. On a hit, the creature is pulled {@b 5 feet} directly towards you.

entries.name: Consuming Force
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can flourish the socketed weapon and create a miniscule black hole at a point you can see within {@b 20 feet} of you. Each creature within 15 feet of the point must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pulled to the unoccupied space nearest the point. When this property is used, roll {@dice 1d4}. On a 1, the black hole consumes the magic of the socketed weapon; the hilt and weapon lose their magical properties until the next dawn.

On a 1, the black hole consumes the magic of the socketed weapon; the hilt and weapon lose their magical properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sandswim
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing this weapon, you can grant yourself a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} of {@b 15 feet} that allows you to burrow through sand and loose earth only. Burrowing in this way leaves no trace or tunnel.

entries.name: Ambush From Below
entries: When you emerge from burrowing within {@b 5 feet} of a creature for the first time on a turn, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first attack roll you make against that creature using these claws.

entries.name: Sandswim
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing this weapon, you can grant yourself a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} of {@b 20 feet} that allows you to burrow through sand and loose earth only. Burrowing in this way leaves no trace or tunnel.

entries.name: Ambush From Below

entries.name: Sandswim

entries.name: Ambush From Below
entries: When you emerge from burrowing within {@b 5 feet} of a creature for the first time on a turn, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first attack roll you make against that creature using these claws and, on a hit, the creature is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

item.name: Tails of Glory (uncommon)
entries: This prosthesis has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can replace or enhance a tail. It has the Melee ({@damage 1d6} Piercing, {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse}) property and the {@itemMastery Hail of Ammunition|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} Superior Strike, which requires no ammunition to perform with this prosthesis.

entries.name: Blur of Blades
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can expend {@b 1 charge} and take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to fling two blades from the tail, each at a different target. Each blade is a ranged weapon attack with a range of 20/60 ft. and a damage die of {@damage 1d8}. The blades vanish at the end of your turn, reappearing attached to the tail.

entries.name: Hero's Presence
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to fling an enchanted blade to an unoccupied space within {@b 30 feet} of you. When an enemy enters a space within {@b 10 feet} of the blade for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 10} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The blade vanishes after {@b 1 minute} or when it has dealt a total of 30 damage.

item.name: Tails of Glory (rare)
entries: This prosthesis has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can replace or enhance a tail. It has the Melee ({@damage 1d6} Piercing, {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse}) property and the {@itemMastery Hail of Ammunition|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} Superior Strike, which requires no ammunition to perform with this prosthesis.

When an enemy enters a space within {@b 10 feet} of the blade for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it makes a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 10} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The blade vanishes after {@b 1 minute} or when it has dealt a total of 30 damage. The area within {@b 10 feet} of an enchanted blade is {@quickref Difficult Terrain|XPHB|3}.

The blade vanishes after {@b 1 minute} or when it has dealt a total of 40 damage. The area within {@b 10 feet} of an enchanted blade is {@quickref Difficult Terrain|XPHB|3}.

item.name: Comfort of the Void (uncommon)
entries: This cloak has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spacial Bending
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to create an aura of starlight that bends space in a {@b 10-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around you for {@b 1 minute}. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved until the start of its next turn. When a creature fails this saving throw, you can let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 1d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you do, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 instead.

entries.name: Eldritch Resilience
entries: When you make a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 1d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. You then add the damage you took to the result of the triggering saving throw.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This cloak is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the cloak, keeping it within reach at all times. When the cloak has fed on its owner sufficiently (GM's discretion), it transforms into an {@creature Embroidered Voidspawn Cloak|HFN2025}, which is {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to the party.

item.name: Comfort of the Void (rare)
entries: While wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
This cloak has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 3d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, and cast the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This cloak is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the cloak, keeping it within reach at all times. When the cloak has fed on its owner sufficiently (GM's discretion), it transforms into an {@creature Embroidered Voidspawn Cloak|HFN2025}, which is {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to the party.

entries.name: Sanguine Insight
entries: When you take the Attack action while wearing this helmet, you can replace any number of attacks with special attacks made using the tentacles on the helmet. These attacks have a {@hit +5} attack bonus and a reach of {@b 5 feet}, and they deal {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage on a hit. When you hit a creature with an attack using the helmet, you can choose to sink the tentacles into it more deeply, dealing an extra {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage and learning a piece of information about its past (GM's discretion). Once this property has been used to sink the tentacles into a creature more deeply, it can't be used in that way again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Prophetic Necrosis
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while wearing the helmet, you can inject a willing creature within {@b 5 feet} of you with necrotic blood. The creature takes {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, and adds the result to the next ability check it makes within {@b 1 minute}. Only one creature can be affected by this property at a time.

entries.name: Eldritch Filtration
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against poisons and mutagens to which you're exposed as a result of breathing water.

entries.name: Sanguine Insight
entries: When you take the Attack action while wearing this helmet, you can replace any number of attacks with special attacks made using the tentacles on the helmet. These attacks have a {@hit +7} attack bonus and a reach of {@b 10 feet}, and they deal {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage on a hit. When you hit a creature with an attack using the helmet, you can choose to sink the tentacles into it more deeply, dealing an extra {@damage 4d6} Necrotic damage and learning a piece of information about its past (GM's discretion). Once this property has been used to sink the tentacles into a creature more deeply, it can't be used in that way again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can enhance or replace an arm. It has the Melee ({@damage 1d6} Slashing, {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light}) and Mobility ({@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB|Swim} 30 ft.) properties and the {@itemMastery Trip|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} Superior Strike.

entries.name: Luring Grasp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing this prosthesis, you can extend tendrils to pull in a Large or smaller creature you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be moved to the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of you.

entries.name: Forbidden Gaze
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while wearing this prosthesis, you can take {@damage 2d6} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, to open its eye. Each creature that can see you within a {@b 20-foot-radius} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 for {@b 1 minute} or until it takes damage. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Shakwraps (uncommon)
entries: This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Energy Chakram
entries: While wearing these vambraces, you can conjure a {@item Chakram|HFN2025} formed from golden energy into your free hand (no action required). Chakrams conjured this way deal Radiant damage and disappear 6 seconds after you cease holding them.

entries.name: Poshanka!
entries: You can cross your forearms and clash the vambraces together in a warrior's salute to gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} check you make within the next {@b 1 minute}.

entries.name: Ricochet
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} when you hit a target with a {@item Chakram|HFN2025} conjured by the vambraces, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause it to ricochet. Make a ranged weapon attack using the {@item Chakram|HFN2025} against a different target within the {@item Chakram|HFN2025}'s range of the original target.

item.name: Shakwraps (rare)
entries: This item has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

If the second attack hits, the {@item Chakram|HFN2025} charges with lifeforce, expanding into a protective golden energy field when it returns. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

entries: This item has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

If the second attack hits, the {@item Chakram|HFN2025} charges with lifeforce, expanding into a protective golden energy field when it returns. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. When your second attack from Ricochet hits, you can make a third attack from the location of the second target. If the third attack hits, the bonus to your AC increases to +3.

item.name: Seam Ripper (uncommon)
entries: This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Silk Spear
entries: The sword's spool of magical silk allows you to throw and retract it a short distance. During your turn, this weapon has the {@itemProperty SC|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Scourge} property.

entries.name: Pallid Pins
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to conjure and suspend three ethereal pins in the air on magical silken threads. Make three attack rolls using this weapon, each against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, a pin deals Necrotic damage instead of Piercing. Hit or miss, the pins disappear at the end of your turn.

item.name: Seam Ripper (rare)
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Pallid Pins
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to conjure and suspend three ethereal pins in the air on magical silken threads. Make three attack rolls using this weapon, each against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, a pin deals Necrotic damage instead of Piercing and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. Hit or miss, the pins disappear at the end of your turn.

item.name: Seam Ripper (very rare)
entries: You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Silk Spear
entries: The sword's spool of magical silk allows you to throw and retract it a short distance. During your turn, this weapon has the {@itemProperty SC|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Scourge} property.

entries.name: Pallid Pins
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to conjure and suspend three ethereal pins in the air on magical silken threads. Make three attack rolls using this weapon, each against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, a pin deals Necrotic damage instead of Piercing and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. Hit or miss, the pins disappear at the end of your turn.

entries.name: Soothing Melody
entries: If you play the flute as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can create a {@b 20-foot}-{@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of soothing magic originating from you, which lasts for as long as you continue to play the pipes each round as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. A Beast or Plant of CR 0 that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 10} Charisma saving throw or be magically calmed. If it was {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you or your allies, it becomes {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent}. If a calmed target leaves the area and reenters it before the melody stops, it automatically fails its save against this effect.

entries.name: Liven Up!
entries: You can play this flute to cast the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} cantrip. Additionally, you can play it to cast the level 2 version of {@spell Summon Plant|HFN2025}, summoning a new plant or animating an existing one within {@b 30 feet}. Once this property has been used to cast {@spell Summon Plant|HFN2025}, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Soothing Melody
entries: If you play the flute as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can create a {@b 20-foot}-{@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of soothing magic originating from you, which lasts for as long as you continue to play the pipes each round as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. If it was {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you or your allies, it becomes {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent}. If a calmed target leaves the area and reenters it before the melody stops, it automatically fails its save against this effect.

Once this property has been used to cast {@spell Summon Plant|HFN2025}, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn. You can play the flute to cast {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB}; once the flute is used to cast {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB}, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn.

item.name: Toothy Brawl (uncommon)
entries: These weapons have {@b 6 teeth} and regrow {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended teeth daily at dawn. When all teeth have been expended, the weapons' damage type changes to Bludgeoning.

entries.name: Rough Handling
entries: Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to resist being grappled by you and on ability checks made to escape your grapple. Whenever a creature attempts to escape your grapple, it takes {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage.

entries.name: Dental Damage
entries: When you hit with an attack using these cesti, you can expend {@b 1 tooth} to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

entries.name: Rocket Tooth
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can expend {@b 1 tooth} and replace one of your attacks with firing the tooth at a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack using a {@hit +5} bonus instead of your attack modifier. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Piercing damage.

item.name: Nucleic DNArm (uncommon)
entries: This prosthesis has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can enhance or replace an arm. It has the {@itemProperty Integrated|HFN2025} ({@item Whip|XPHB}) property.

entries.name: Form Shift
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inject a willing creature within {@b 5 feet} of you with one of the following mutations of your choice until the end of its next turn:

items.name: Assault Forme
entries: The target's weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit.

items.name: Carapaced Forme
entries: The target gains a +3 bonus to Armor Class.

items.name: Nimble Forme
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 20 feet.

entries: This prosthesis has {@b 7 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can enhance or replace an arm. It has the {@itemProperty Integrated|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} ({@item Whip|XPHB}) property.

items.name: Carapaced Forme
entries: The target gains a +4 bonus to Armor Class.

items.name: Nimble Forme
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 25 feet.

entries.name: Form Shift
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inject a willing creature within {@b 5 feet} of you with one of the following mutations of your choice until the end of its next turn. You can choose to inject a creature with two mutations instead of one. If you do, the creature takes {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way.

items.name: Assault Forme
entries: The target's weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 3d6} damage on a hit.

items.name: Carapaced Forme
entries: The target gains a +5 bonus to Armor Class.

items.name: Nimble Forme
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 35 feet.

item.name: Emotional Dis-Dress (uncommon)
entries: This item has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Heart On My Sleeve
entries: This magical clothing adapts to fit your thoughts and mood. While touching it, you can cause it to change into any article of clothing, though it always retains its organic texture and tendrils. While wearing it, it subtly shifts its colour to match your current emotional state.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this item, you can cast the spells on the following table from it (save {@dc 13}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.
When you cast {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} in this way, each unwilling creature that succeeds on the saving throw takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage.

entries.name: Furious Fabric
entries: The clothing's tendrils lash out independently based on your feelings of stress and danger. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are below your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB|Hit Point maximum}, you can use the clothing to make a melee weapon attack against a target within {@b 5 feet} of you. The attack has {@hit +5} attack modifier and deals {@damage 2d4} Bludgeoning damage.

item.name: Mantis Menace (uncommon)
entries: This Kusarigama has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn. Whenever you deal damage with this weapon, it deals Necrotic damage instead of its normal damage types.

entries.name: Clawing Fear
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the counterweight of this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to attempt to frighten it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you for {@b 1 minute}. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

entries.name: Draining Fear
entries: Whenever you deal damage to a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature with this weapon, its {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB|Hit Point maximum} is reduced by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken. A creature's {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB|Hit Point maximum} returns to normal when it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hungering Scythe
entries: When you make an attack with the blade of this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to extend it into a scythe of ghostly flame (no action required). Until the end of the turn, it deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage on a hit.

item.name: Mantis Menace (rare)
entries: This Kusarigama has {@b 7 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. Whenever you deal damage with this weapon, it deals Necrotic damage instead of its normal damage types.

entries.name: Clawing Fear
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the counterweight of this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to attempt to frighten it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you for {@b 1 minute}. While {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Crablaster (uncommon)
entries: This Rifle has the {@itemProperty RDX|HFN2025|Reload} (6) property and doesn't require ammunition. Instead, it fires high pressure water blasts, and requires 1 gallon of water to reload. While you are underwater, reloading the Rifle doesn't require an action, as long as you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

entries.name: Aqua Jet
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can propel yourself {@b 10 feet} backwards without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}. If you're submerged in at least 5 feet of water, you move backwards {@b 15 feet} instead. Using this property expends a shot, as if firing a bullet.

entries.name: Rain of Ammunition
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while the Rifle has at least one shot remaining, you can empty the tank of the Rifle to rain down on your enemies. The Rifle loses any remaining ammunition. Choose a point within the Rifle's normal range. Each creature in a {@b 10-foot-radius, 30-foot-high} {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on that point makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. If the Rifle had at least three shots remaining, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

item.name: Crablaster (rare)
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic Rifle.

This Rifle has the {@itemProperty RDX|HFN2025|Reload} (6) property and doesn't require ammunition. Instead, it fires high pressure water blasts, and requires 1 gallon of water to reload. While you are underwater, reloading the Rifle doesn't require an action, as long as you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.
