item.name: Magical Prostheses
entries: Magical prostheses make use of intricate enchantments to enhance their functionality and gain new properties. A magical prosthesis with no additional properties is a Common magic item that doesn't require {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, but more powerful prostheses with additional properties may require {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}.

entries.name: Replacing or Enhancing a Limb
entries: A magical prosthesis can be used to replace a missing limb or be placed around an existing limb to enhance it. Unless stated otherwise, a prosthesis can't be used to add a new limb that a member of the species wouldn't normally have.

entries.name: Prosthesis Functions
entries: A magical prosthesis can replicate all functions of the limb it emulates. For example, a magical prosthetic tongue is able to taste, a magical prosthetic eye can see, and a magical prosthetic nose is able to smell.

entries.name: Attaching or Removing a Magical Prosthesis
entries: A magical prosthesis can't be attached to or removed from an unwilling creature, even if the creature is {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}.

item.name: The Steamer (rare)
entries: This item can be a Magitech Cannon of any tier, and uses the corresponding statistics.

entries.name: Steam Vent
entries: This cannon's Grape Shot shoots a cone of steam instead of shot. It uses no ammunition and deals Fire damage instead of Bludgeoning damage. Being underwater doesn't grant {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to this Fire damage.

entries.name: Eggsplosion
entries: When you hit with an attack using the cannon's Ball Shot, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 6, the cannonball is actually an egg, from which a {@creature Chuul|XMM} emerges after the attack, appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the attack's target. The chuul rolls {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} to join combat and is Hostile to all creatures. After 1 minute, the chuul flees towards the nearest body of water. Once this property has created a chuul, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

itemFluff.name: Tyrant's Talon
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Dragon (Ophthalos) Claws}

"Who here wants a mighty blade forged from the talons of an ophthalos?" the guildmaster bellowed, and all intrepid adventurers across the taproom of the hunters' guild eagerly raised their hands. "And who wants to go kill one for the materials?" All of a sudden, there were no hands in sight.

itemFluff.name: Black Spine
entries: {@i Component: Monstrosity (Nergigoblin) Horn}

You don't see many creatures walking around with weapons crafted from their own kind. This— alongside their unwavering zealotry, complete disregard for their own safety, and perpetually prickly disposition— is just another way in which the Blackspine nergigoblins stand out from the pack.

itemFluff.name: Godplume Switchaxe
entries: {@i Component: Monstrosity (Godmane) Horn}

When asked to measure the radiant energy within a Godplume Switchaxe, L'Arsène Upin said, in astonishment, that its “power level” was “over nine thousand”. No one is quite certain of what this metric means, and L'Arsène has refused to elaborate.

itemFluff.name: Brutescale Gunlance
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Monstrosity (Devil Jhoblin) Teeth}

Shamans of the Brutescale tribe discovered that some devil jhoblins who gorged themselves on their own kin contracted a strange curse that severed their connection to the elements. As this rot, dubbed wyrm blight, spread to other members of the tribe, they were faced with the choice to eradicate it… or weaponise it. No prizes for correctly guessing what happened next.

itemFluff.name: Mindshock
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Brainielle) Tentacle}
No mind-numbing, thousand-volts-coursing-through-your-body pain, no mind-expanding, unbelievably powerful psionic gain!
entries.by: L'Arsène Upin, Coming Around on Auto-Electrocution

itemFluff.name: Bubblebasher
entries: {@i Component: Fiend (Demon) Bone}

Between blowing streams of iridescent bubbles and sliding at risky speeds on a trail of slippery oil, this hefty hammer lets you bring some of the innocent fun of childhood into brutal, bloody combat. Is it something anyone asked for? No. Can you do it anyway? Yes!

itemFluff.name: Pandrumonium
entries: {@i Components: Monstrosity (Infernoguma) Pelt}

First-time use of this unusual piece of weaponry is accompanied by an unpleasant brimstone odour, which seems to originate from the monster components used in its creation. To get rid of this aroma, one must use it to deliver a few good whacks, a process known as ‘beating the devil out of it'.

itemFluff.name: Begrappler
entries: {@i Component: Monstrosity (Be-hudra) Liver}

itemFluff.name: Pin
entries: {@i Components: Monstrosity (Rompopowlbear) Stinger}

There's no doubt about it: this impressive piece of weaponry is sure to catch any bystander's attention. It's a good thing then that—should your adoring fans get too close—all it takes is a small poke from this blade to reclaim that priceless personal space.

itemFluff.name: Cushion
entries: {@i Components: Monstrosity (Rompopowlbear) Pelt}
Listen, it's child's play; just point the shield towards the ground when you're about to hit it, and whoosh! You'll land soft as a feather. You'll see; give it a try on that cliff over there!
entries.by: Bree the Barbarian to her Wizard friend, forgetting to tell him this item requires attunement

itemFluff.name: Stickaxe
entries: {@i Component: Phial of Ooze (Splatacabra) Mucus}

No matter how many times the sludge oozing from this weapon is scraped off or washed away, the blade and haft are quickly covered again. When its inventor, Thomumin Underjaw, was asked what to do with the excess, he said, “I've tried burning it, eating it, freezing it, and making bouncy balls for small children. None of that worked, so it's your problem now.”

itemFluff.name: Life-eater Charge Blade
entries: {@i Component: Dragon (darkveld) horn}

After forging the first version of this mighty blade, its creator took a well-deserved break outside his forge. When he returned, he found the prototype's ghostly image floating next to his workbench, his hard work nowhere to be seen. Eventually, he found the invisible weapon right where he'd left it and spent the ensuing month trying—and failing— to convince buyers that the invisibility wasn't a bug, but a feature.

itemFluff.name: Palipolearm
entries: {@i Components: Pouch of Monstrosity (Palicopy) Teeth}

While harvesting catnap—a dangerous undertaking— L'Arsene noticed that, even after death, the slightest contact with a palicopy's jaw can cause it to clamp down. The infamous craftsman proceeded to make excellent use of this trait by fashioning the palicopy's jaws into a projectile with bite.

itemFluff.name: Kablowbold Suit
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Dragon (Explodobold) Scales}
“I guarantee this will make you fly. You will also eventually stop flying. When is anyone's guess. Good luck!”
— L'Arsène Upin, Honestly Imprecise

itemFluff.name: Laceblade
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Plant (Chimerina or Laceleaf) Leaves}
First thing's first: your grip shouldn't be too tight, nor too loose. Never drop your blade. You can't be a swordsman without a swo–. Well. That doesn't seem fair.
— Cyrio Morel, Mycelian Swordmaster

itemFluff.name: Bloodmist Bow
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Thanatophobia) Phial of Blood}

Leeching the life force away from your enemies? That's kind of hot. Being an emotional leech? That's worse than a bite to the neck! Come to my dark and spooky mansion alone if you want to improve your relationships!
—Advertisement next to several unrelated missing person posters

itemFluff.name: Divine Lie
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Fiend (Zohtraferna) Feathers}
Of course you can trust me; look how pure and celestial my sword is! An evil person wouldn't have a sword like this! This is probably the most trustworthy sword anyone's ever owned!

itemFluff.name: Dark Truth
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Celestial (Zohtraferna) Claws}
There's a special kind of exhaustion that comes from presenting a facade to the world. Some days, you just want to take the mask off and let the world see the real you: pure, unadulterated evil.

itemFluff.name: Shadowsheath
entries: {@i Component: Celestial (Morphelean) Skin}

This sword is like an onion; it has layers. Specifically, it has a terrifying demonic sheath layer, a glimmering celestial blade layer, and then a juicy core of uncontrollable, frenzied rage, all wrapped in a sheath of devilish smoke.

itemFluff.name: Sanguine Shockshot
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Undead (Vampiacrus) Teeth}

The forging of this lightning-spewing railgun-lance was fraught with avoidable deaths. Though its supercharged material necessitated the use of nonmetal equipment for the safety of its creators, multiple stubborn dwarves died during its creation… the Fighter who commissioned it later said, when asked, “I'm glad they gave up after the fourth one; I couldn't afford to pay for another funeral.”

itemFluff.name: Graviturgic Warp-hilt
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Aberration (bicephal wyrmhole) Claws}

Running around from foe to foe, hunting them like prey, is such a waste of energy. Being a truly formidable opponent, one with gravitas, means bringing your enemies to you. Let them waste their strength running towards their own slaughter.

itemFluff.name: Sandripple Spikes
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Monstrosity (Stonemaw) Teeth}

Sand and water aren't all that different, both flowing, shifting, and curving into rolling waves. Desert dunes are an ocean, and a dash of magic is all you need to start swimming. The only question left is whether or not that desert counts as a soup.

itemFluff.name: Tails of Glory
entries: {@i Component: Monstrosity (Mantiroar) Stinger}

Originally called "Sit on Your Grass," this prosthesis was designed with tensioned blades to easily mow stretches of lawn. Poor initial sales and multiple complaints of the blades' rapid, spontaneous deconstruction led to a recall, redesign, and rebranding, targeting the adventurer demographic instead.

itemFluff.name: Comfort of the Void
entries: {@i Component: Phial of Aberration (Voidspawn Cloak) Blood}

No one ever said fashion was painless. So what if your magical cloak takes a little nibble now and then? It's a vintage look that comes with magical powers; it's worth a bit of blood, just don't let it work up too much of an appetite.

itemFluff.name: Bloodprophet's Eyes
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Necropod) Eye}
You can tell a lot about a person by magically ingesting their blood: past experiences, future potential, favourite colour… Try starting a collection of your most interesting samples!

itemFluff.name: Lurelimb
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Deep Gazer) Tentacle}
My eye is over here, buddy. Nope. Further. On the shoulder, the shoulder! There you go.

itemFluff.name: Shakwraps
entries: {@i Component: Beast Chitin}

A true warrior will always welcome the respectful salute of a fellow combatant. Those who flee the salute are probably dishonourable. They should be chakram'd and their life force absorbed the moment they turn their backs.

itemFluff.name: Seam Ripper
entries: {@i Component: Undead (Chrysalich) Ethereal Ichor}
As I've always said, any skilled and precise seamstress will pin her enemies down before sowing their destruction.

itemFluff.name: Hivecomb Plate
entries: {@i Component: Phial of Ooze (Hivebound) Mucus}

Lightweight, sturdy, and stylish, Hivecomb Plate is the ideal suit of armour for daily use. The only reason it hasn't spiked in popularity is the minor downside of perpetual stickiness. Seriously, no amount of magic has been able to keep it from sticking to everything it touches.

itemFluff.name: Vinecharmer
entries: {@i Component: Plant (Mosslax) Bark}
Snake charming is boring and cliché. Everyone's seen a dancing snake before. Now, making the petunias hop up and dance the tango? That's a novelty.

itemFluff.name: Toothy Brawl
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Fey (Giblin) Teeth}
Everyone's heard the classic threat "I'll punch your teeth in," but few have heard the more upsetting threat of "I'll punch my teeth into you."

itemFluff.name: Nucleic DNArm
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Nucleye) Tentacle}
I assure you, rapid modification of my own genes has had no repercussions on me whatsoever. It's perfectly safe to use on any healthy Humanoid.

itemFluff.name: Emotional Dis-Dress
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Empathrem) Antenna}

Do you know what the red means, Brad? It means I'm angry. I'm literally wearing clothes that change colour to fit my mood, and you're still too dense to figure it out! I swear if you take one step closer, we'll see which one punches you first, my fist or my outfit!

itemFluff.name: Mantis Menace
entries: {@i Component: Undead (Kougaï) Ethereal Ichor}
The fastest way to cause an enemy's heart to falter is with overwhelming fear and dread. The second fastest is a scythe between the fourth and fifth ribs.

itemFluff.name: Crablaster
entries: {@i Component: Pouch of Fiend (Shahoko) Claws}
Your silly little peashooter is nice, but what happens when you run out of bullets, huh? If you were calm, collected, and moisturised like me, you'd use mother nature's bullets: water.

itemFluff.name: Darkflame's Embrace
entries: {@i Component: Undead (Teneblaze) Ethereal Ichor}

Show off your inner darkness. It's like, super angular armour in really dark colours to, y'know, blend in with the shadows. It's also like, on fire, but the fire is shadowy ghost armour. You can like, absorb fireballs and use your foes' powers against them, like a dark mirror of their sins. It's pretty cool.

itemFluff.name: The Steamer (rare)
entries: {@i Component: Aberration (Chuulverin) Chitin}
You call chuuls steamed lobsters?
Yes! It's a… regional dialect.
Uh-huh. What region?
Uh… Upper Voidsea.
entries.by: Conversation between Admiral Chalmers and Captain Skinner

entries.name: Spirit Gauge
entries: When you hit an enemy that has a {@filter Challenge Rating of 1/2 or higher|bestiary|Challenge Rating=[&1/2;&Special]} with an attack using this weapon, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}. The weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}, and loses all charges whenever it goes 1 minute without gaining any charges. While it has any charges, the weapon is imbued with flame and sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a {@b 5-foot} radius.

entries.name: Searing Strike
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, roll {@dice 1d4} to determine how you can use the weapon's flames as indicated in the table. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 3 charges} to unleash this burst of fire. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d6} Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once this property of the weapon has been used, it can't be used again for {@b 1 minute}.

rows: The blade releases a torrent of flame, akin to a dragon's breath, affecting each creature in a {@b 15-foot} {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}.
The sword's tip shoots a jet of fire, affecting each creature in a {@b 5-foot-wide, 30-foot-long} {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line}.
As you spin the sword, it creates a whirlwind of fire affecting each creature in a {@b 10-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you.

You shoot orbs of flame from the sword at two points within {@b 30 feet} of you, each of which explodes in a {@b 5-foot-radius} {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, affecting each creature in the area.

entries.name: Wind-Up Strikes
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks to charge up this sword. If you do, you can't make another attack this turn. The sword remains charged until the end of your next turn, until you drop it, or until you become {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

When you hit a target with this sword while it's charged, a shower of spikes bursts from its blade. The target and each creature other than you within {@b 5 feet} of the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage. If the original target is a creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on this saving throw. The sword is then no longer charged.
On the second consecutive round you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 3d6}. On the third and subsequent consecutive rounds you deal this damage, it increases to {@damage 4d6}.

itemGroup.name: Godplume Switchaxe
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 3 charges}. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to cause its feathers to glow with golden energy and its blade to unfold, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greatsword|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Radiant Build-Up
entries: When you hit an enemy with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Radiant Discharge
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a {@item Greataxe|XPHB} again.

entries.name: Wyrmstake
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can use it to make a special melee attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage, and the weapon conjures a large stake that it embeds in the target.

At the end of the target's next turn, the stake explodes in a burst of necrotic energy, forcing the target to make a {@dc 13} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target becomes incapable of dealing Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Psionic Link
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon, you can allow its electrified tentacles to pierce your skin and establish a psionic connection with you until you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or pull the tentacles out as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. While this connection lasts, you take {@damage 1d6} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. In addition, your jump distance is doubled, and attacks you make with this weapon deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit.

entries.name: Enhanced Attunement
entries: You can enhance your attunement to this weapon by enduring the pain of electrocution. In order to do so, you must take a total of 100 Lightning damage from hostile creatures while attuned to the sword. While enhanced, the weapon gains the Overload property:

entries.name: EA: Overload
entries: When you use the Psionic Link property, you can choose to intensify the link. If you do, you take {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage at the start of each of your turns while the link lasts, instead of {@damage 1d6}. In addition to the benefits of the Psionic Link property, the extra Psychic damage dealt with the weapon increases to {@damage 2d6}.

itemGroup.name: Bubblebasher
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The weapon has {@b 4 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spin to Win
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to spin it around in a deadly whirlwind, making a melee attack with the maul against each creature within {@b 5 feet} of you. As the weapon is spun, it creates bubbles of oil that slowly fall to the ground. After you make all the attacks, the area within {@b 5 feet} of you other than the space you occupy is covered in a layer of slippery oil as per the {@spell Grease|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 16}) until the end of your next turn.

If the oil or a creature standing on the oil takes Fire damage, the oil ignites. Each creature standing in the area must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. After the oil ignites, it disappears.

entries.name: Slip and Slide
entries: While holding this weapon, you expend only 1 foot of movement for each 2 feet you move when moving into a space covered in a slippery substance like oil or ice, and you automatically succeed on saving throws and ability checks made not to slip on it.

itemGroup.name: Pandrumonium
entries: You gain a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon, and you can use it as if it were a drum. This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Percussion
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and tap a rhythm on this weapon while holding it with both hands. Choose up to two other creatures that can hear you. Until the start of your next turn, each creature deals extra Fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) the next time it hits with an attack.

entries.name: Concussion
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack made with this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to cause a blaze of hellfire to erupt from it (no action required). The target and each creature other than yourself within {@b 5 feet} of it must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one.

itemGroup.name: Begrappler
entries: The glowing “eyes” set into this greatsword's decorative reptilian skulls are actually fluid-producing glands cut from a be-hudra's skin. One goes dark every time the blade discharges electricity, and all ignite again when its power is replenished.
This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Conductive Grasp
entries: While holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to launch ichor-coated tentacles toward a creature you can see within {@b 5 feet} of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or be {@condition Grappled|XPHB} (escape {@dc 13}) until you release the weapon or use it to attempt to grapple or attack a different target.

A creature grappled by this weapon is also covered in ichor for {@b 1 hour} or until it takes Lightning damage. If a creature covered in ichor takes Lightning damage, it rolls an additional 3 Lightning damage dice and adds them to the total damage dealt.

entries.name: Writhing Discharge
entries: You can expend up to {@b 2 charges} when you hit a creature with this weapon to deal an extra {@damage 2d6} Lightning damage for each charge expended.

itemGroup.name: Pin
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 4 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Pinprick
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause it to emit a burst of air. The creature must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or be pushed up to {@b 10 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Ejector Seat
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to forego one of your attacks and press this weapon into the ground. One Large or smaller creature of your choice on the ground within {@b 30 feet} of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Bludgeoning and be launched 10 feet in a direction of your choice, falling {@condition Prone|XPHB} when it lands.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

itemGroup.name: Cushion
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC. This item has {@b 5 charges}, and regains {@dice 1d4+1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bouncy Pad
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this shield and on the ground, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to point the shield's air bags toward the ground and inflate it, launching yourself up to {@b 30 feet} vertically into the air, and up to {@b 15 feet} horizontally. If a creature is within 5 feet of where you land, you can immediately make a melee attack with a weapon or Unarmed Strike against that creature as part of this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, and any damage you take from the fall is reduced by an amount equal to twice your level. That amount is added onto the attack's damage.

entries.name: Airbag
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Bludgeoning damage, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inflate Cushion, granting yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that instance of Bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Pin|HFN2025} and {@item Cushion|HFN2025}, you can use charges from either item to fuel the other's properties.

itemGroup.name: Stickaxe
entries: This weapon has a maximum of {@b 5 charges}, and deals Acid damage instead of its normal type. While the weapon has at least 1 charge, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to unfold the blade and spew potent glue from its sacs, turning the weapon into a {@item Greatsword|XPHB}. You are considered proficient with this {@item Greatsword|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}.

entries.name: Adhesive Buildup
entries: When you hit a hostile creature with an attack with this weapon in {@item Greataxe|XPHB} form, the weapon gains {@b 1 charge}.

entries.name: Slimy Slam
entries: When you hit with an attack using this weapon in its {@item Greatsword|XPHB} form, the weapon loses {@b 1 charge} and deals an extra {@damage 2d6} Acid damage to the target. When the weapon loses its last charge, it becomes a greataxe again.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you attune to the weapon, you can attempt to knock a weapon or other item from a target's grasp. Once on your turn after you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the target to make a {@dc 15} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check (its choice). On a failure, the target drops one item it is holding.

itemGroup.name: Life-eater Charge Blade
entries: This weapon can hold a maximum of {@b 5 charges}. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding it to detach the axehead from the blade, turning it into a {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and a {@item Shield|XPHB}, or to recombine the {@item Shield|XPHB} and {@item Shortsword|XPHB} back into a greataxe. You are proficient with this {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're proficient with {@item Greataxe|XPHB|Greataxes}. If the {@item Shortsword|XPHB} and {@item Shield|XPHB} are more than {@b 15 feet} apart from each other for {@b 1 minute}, your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to them ends, and they become magically inert until reunited.

entries.name: Lifeforce Recycler (Shortsword Form Only)
entries: When you hit a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creature of CR 1 or higher with an attack using this {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, it gains {@b 1 charge}. This item loses all gained charges 1 minute after it gains its last charge.

entries.name: Life-Devouring Discharge (Greataxe Form Only)
entries: When you hit with an attack with this {@item Greataxe|XPHB}, you can expend any number of charges to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage to the target per charge expended. You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Force damage dealt.

itemGroup.name: Palipolearm
entries: This item has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Finesse
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

entries.name: Whirling Strikes
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to whirl this weapon around you. Each creature within {@b 10 feet} of you must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Slashing damage and be pushed {@b 5 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Chuck-a-Chomp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to launch a chuck-a-chomp toward a Small or larger creature within {@b 30 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage, and the chuck-a-chomp sticks to it. The chuck-a-chomp remains attached for {@b 1 minute}. It detaches early if you use this property again or a creature uses an action to remove the chuck-a-chomp from itself or a creature within its reach. While the chuck-a-chomp is attached, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns to deal {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage to the target.

itemGroup.name: Kablowbold Suit
entries: As with all things kobold-derived, the Kablowbold Suit can be a little… inconsistent. Part squirrel suit, part artillery, and partly reliable, its tiny rocket engine can guarantee escape from any well, canyon, or tarrasque's stomach, provided the escape is guaranteed to take six seconds or less.

entries.name: Glide
entries: When you fall at least {@b 30 feet}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deploy the suit's wings. If you do, subtract up to {@b 100 feet} from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend.

entries.name: Flight
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing the armour, you can pull a toggle, activating a small rocket and gaining a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of {@b 30 feet} for {@b 1 minute}. At the end of each of your turns, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, you immediately lose the {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} and fall {@b 200 feet}. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

itemGroup.name: Laceblade
entries: You have a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with an attack using this weapon, it deals your choice of Piercing or Slashing damage.

entries.name: Vine Hilt
entries: When you grasp the weapon's hilt, the blades' vines wrap around your forearm. You can't be disarmed of this weapon. If you choose to drop it, the vines hold it within easy grasp of your hand. On your turn, you can take hold of the item again (no action required). A creature can take {@b 1 minute} to gently detach a Laceblade from itself or the forearm of a creature within its reach.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Quickvine property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Quickvine
entries: When a willing creature within {@b 20 feet} of you is targeted by an attack from a creature you can see, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause a vine to whip out and pull the creature up to {@b 15 feet} directly towards you, causing the attack to miss if the creature is pulled out of the attack's reach or range. As an action, you can pull a willing creature within {@b 20 feet} of you {@b 15 feet} directly towards you. This property can be used twice, and regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

itemGroup.name: Bloodmist Bow
entries: This bow doesn't require ammunition; an arrow of red smoke forms whenever it's drawn. It regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bat Bolt
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to fire a special arrow at a point you can see within the bow's normal range. Where the arrow lands, it swirls into ruby smoke, creating a {@creature Swarm of Vitality Bats|HFN2025} under your control.

The swarm shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}s on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to command it to take a different action. The action can be one in the swarm's stat block or some other action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to command the swarm, you can command all swarms you've made with this weapon using the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The swarm lasts for {@b 1 minute}, until it dies, or until you dismiss it (no action required).

itemGroup.name: Divine Lie
entries: This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you deal damage with this weapon, you can choose to deal Force damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 5d8} Force damage.

entries.name: Divine Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself.

entries.name: Heavenly Cloak
entries: As an action while holding this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cast the {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} spell (save {@dc 16}). When you cast a spell with this property, you can choose to appear as a Celestial to Divination magic and similar effects for the duration.

entries.name: Consume Light
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Radiant damage while holding this weapon, you can absorb it into the blade, gaining {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage (including against the triggering damage) until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Zohtraferna's Duality
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} and {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, both weapons have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. Additionally, when you use the Consume Light property of {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025}, {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge, and when you use the Consume Shadow property of {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge. Once a weapon has regained a charge in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Dark Truth
entries: This weapon has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

When you hit a target and deal damage to it with this weapon, you can choose to deal Fire or Lightning damage instead of Slashing damage. Additionally, when you deal damage with this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to deal an extra {@damage 5d8} Fire or Lightning damage.

entries.name: Fiend Step
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while holding this weapon in an area of {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to teleport to an unoccupied space in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} you can see within {@b 30 feet} of yourself.

entries.name: Hellish Wave
entries: As an action, you can stab this weapon into the ground and expend {@b 1 charge} to create a radiating burst of flames. Each creature within {@b 20 feet} of you must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d6} Fire damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

entries.name: Consume Shadow
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you take Necrotic damage while holding this weapon, you can absorb it into the blade, gaining {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage (including against the triggering damage) until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Item Set
entries: While you are attuned to both {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} and {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, both weapons have the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property. Additionally, when you use the Consume Light property of {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025}, {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge, and when you use the Consume Shadow property of {@item Dark Truth|HFN2025}, {@item Divine Lie|HFN2025} regains 1 expended charge. Once a weapon has regained a charge in this way, it can't do so again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Shadowsheath
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon can have a maximum of {@b 7 charges}.

entries.name: Sheathed Menace
entries: While this weapon is sheathed and the sheath is visible, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intimidation checks.

entries.name: Divine Fury
entries: Whenever you hit a creature with a weapon attack using the unsheathed sword, it deals extra Radiant damage equal to half the number of charges it has, and then it gains {@b 1 charge} for every 5 damage dealt by the attack. Sheathing the sword resets the charges to 0.

entries.name: Halo of Rage
entries: As an action, you can send the guard of the blade to hover above a willing creature you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you, casting the {@spell Enrage|HFN2025} spell on it (save {@dc 16}). Alternatively, you can use an action to place the guard above your own head and cast the {@spell Blood Frenzy|HFN2025} spell on yourself. Once you cast a spell using this property, that spell can't be cast with it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This sword is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the sword, keeping it within reach at all times. If the weapon reaches its maximum number of charges, you go into an uncontrollable frenzy for {@b 1 minute}, becoming a creature under the GM's control, during which you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and spend each turn attacking the nearest creature. After the minute has elapsed, the sword loses all charges.

Another creature can end the frenzy early by making a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check (DC equals 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus), sheathing the blade and ending the frenzy on a success.

itemGroup.name: Sanguine Shockshot
entries: While carrying this weapon, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage, and when you hit with an attack with it, you can choose to deal Lightning damage instead of Piercing.

entries.name: Bio-Electric Railgun
entries: You can make ranged spell attacks with this weapon, in addition to its normal melee weapon attacks. Each spell attack has a normal range of 100 feet, a long range of 300 feet, {@hit +7} to hit, and deals {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage on a hit. You do not add an ability modifier to this damage.

entries.name: Crimson Flash
entries: You can use an action to release a flash of bioluminescence from this weapon. Each other creature within a {@b 300-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the weapon must make a {@dc 16} Constitution saving throw. A creature that fails its saving throw is {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Graviturgic Warp-hilt
entries: This distorted, unnatural sword hilt can be socketed onto any magical melee weapon, adapting its bizarre geometry to the hilt or handle of its host.

entries.name: Gravity Well
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll using the socketed weapon, you can attack a creature {@b 5 feet} farther than your normal reach with the weapon. On a hit, the creature is pulled {@b 5 feet} directly towards you.

entries.name: Consuming Force
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can flourish the socketed weapon and create a miniscule black hole at a point you can see within {@b 20 feet} of you. Each creature within 15 feet of the point must succeed on a {@dc 13} Strength saving throw or be pulled to the unoccupied space nearest the point. When this property is used, roll {@dice 1d4}. On a 1, the black hole consumes the magic of the socketed weapon; the hilt and weapon lose their magical properties until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sandswim
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing this weapon, you can grant yourself a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} of {@b 20 feet} that allows you to burrow through sand and loose earth only. Burrowing in this way leaves no trace or tunnel.

entries.name: Ambush From Below
entries: When you emerge from burrowing within {@b 5 feet} of a creature for the first time on a turn, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the first attack roll you make against that creature using these claws and, on a hit, the creature is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

itemGroup.name: Tails of Glory
entries: This prosthesis has {@b 8 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can replace or enhance a tail. It has the Melee ({@damage 1d6} Piercing, {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse}) property and the {@itemMastery Hail of Ammunition|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} Superior Strike, which requires no ammunition to perform with this prosthesis.

entries.name: Blur of Blades
entries: When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can expend {@b 1 charge} and take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to fling three blades from the tail, each at a different target. Each blade is a ranged weapon attack with a range of 20/60 ft. and a damage die of {@damage 1d8}. The blades vanish at the end of your turn, reappearing attached to the tail.

entries.name: Hero's Presence
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to fling an enchanted blade to an unoccupied space within {@b 30 feet} of you. When an enemy enters a space within {@b 10 feet} of the blade for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it makes a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 10} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. The blade vanishes after {@b 1 minute} or when it has dealt a total of 40 damage. The area within {@b 10 feet} of an enchanted blade is {@quickref Difficult Terrain|XPHB|3}.

itemGroup.name: Comfort of the Void
entries: While wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.
This cloak has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spacial Bending
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to create an aura of starlight that bends space in a {@b 10-foot} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} around you for {@b 1 minute}. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved until the start of its next turn. When a creature fails this saving throw, you can let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 1d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. If you do, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 instead.

entries.name: Eldritch Resilience
entries: When you make a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 1d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way. You then add the damage you took to the result of the triggering saving throw.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to let the cloak bite you; you take {@damage 3d4} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, and cast the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This cloak is cursed, and becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you are unwilling to part with the cloak, keeping it within reach at all times. When the cloak has fed on its owner sufficiently (GM's discretion), it transforms into an {@creature Embroidered Voidspawn Cloak|HFN2025}, which is {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to the party.

entries.name: Sanguine Insight
entries: When you take the Attack action while wearing this helmet, you can replace any number of attacks with special attacks made using the tentacles on the helmet. These attacks have a {@hit +8} attack bonus and a reach of {@b 15 feet}, and they deal {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage on a hit. When you hit a creature with an attack using the helmet, you can choose to sink the tentacles into it more deeply, dealing an extra {@damage 7d6} Necrotic damage and learning a piece of information about its past (GM's discretion). Once this property has been used to sink the tentacles into a creature more deeply, it can't be used in that way again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Prophetic Necrosis
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while wearing the helmet, you can inject a willing creature within {@b 5 feet} of you with necrotic blood. The creature takes {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, and adds the result to the next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes within {@b 1 minute}. Only one creature can be affected by this property at a time.

entries.name: Eldritch Filtration
entries: While wearing this helmet, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against poisons and mutagens to which you're exposed as a result of breathing water.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can enhance or replace an arm. It has the Melee ({@damage 1d6} Slashing, {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light}) and Mobility ({@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB|Swim} 30 ft.) properties and the {@itemMastery Trip|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} Superior Strike.

entries.name: Luring Grasp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing this prosthesis, you can extend tendrils to pull in a Large or smaller creature you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. That creature must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength saving throw or be moved to the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. A creature that fails the saving throw against the Luring Grasp property also takes {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage.

entries.name: Forbidden Gaze
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while wearing this prosthesis, you can take {@damage 2d6} Force damage, which can't be reduced or prevented in any way, to open its eye. Each creature that can see you within a {@b 20-foot-radius} {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Wisdom saving throw or have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 for {@b 1 minute} or until it takes damage. A creature that fails the saving throw against the Forbidden Gaze property is also {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} while its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

itemGroup.name: Shakwraps
entries: This item has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Energy Chakram
entries: While wearing these vambraces, you can conjure a {@item Chakram|HFN2025} formed from golden energy into your free hand (no action required). Chakrams conjured this way deal Radiant damage and disappear 6 seconds after you cease holding them.

entries.name: Poshanka!
entries: You can cross your forearms and clash the vambraces together in a warrior's salute to gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} check you make within the next {@b 1 minute}.

entries.name: Ricochet
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} when you hit a target with a {@item Chakram|HFN2025} conjured by the vambraces, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause it to ricochet. Make a ranged weapon attack using the {@item Chakram|HFN2025} against a different target within the {@item Chakram|HFN2025}'s range of the original target. If the second attack hits, the {@item Chakram|HFN2025} charges with lifeforce, expanding into a protective golden energy field when it returns. You gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. When your second attack from Ricochet hits, you can make a third attack from the location of the second target. If the third attack hits, the bonus to your AC increases to +3.

itemGroup.name: Seam Ripper
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Silk Spear
entries: The sword's spool of magical silk allows you to throw and retract it a short distance. During your turn, this weapon has the {@itemProperty SC|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Scourge} property.

entries.name: Pallid Pins
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to conjure and suspend three ethereal pins in the air on magical silken threads. Make three attack rolls using this weapon, each against a target within {@b 30 feet} of you. On a hit, a pin deals Necrotic damage instead of Piercing and the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the end of its next turn. Hit or miss, the pins disappear at the end of your turn.

itemGroup.name: Hivecomb Plate
entries: This armor has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sticky Situation
entries: Whenever a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, its Speed is reduced by {@b 5 feet} until the start of its next turn. If a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 by this property, it's {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Swarm
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause a swarm of bees to surround you until the start of your next turn. When you activate this property, choose attack or defence. Your choice determines the effect created while the swarm lasts:

items.name: Attack
entries: When any creature of your choice enters a space within {@b 5 feet} of you for the first time on a turn, it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Piercing damage.

items.name: Defence
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to AC.

entries.name: Liven Up!
entries: You can play this flute to cast the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} cantrip. Additionally, you can play it to cast the level 2 version of {@spell Summon Plant|HFN2025}, summoning a new plant or animating an existing one within {@b 30 feet}. Once this property has been used to cast {@spell Summon Plant|HFN2025}, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn. You can play the flute to cast {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB}; once the flute is used to cast {@spell Plant Growth|XPHB}, it can't be used to cast it again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Soothing Melody
entries: If you play the flute as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can create a {@b 20-foot}-{@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of soothing magic originating from you, which lasts for as long as you continue to play the pipes each round as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action. A Beast or Plant that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw or be magically calmed. If it was {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you or your allies, it becomes {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent}. If a calmed target leaves the area and reenters it before the melody stops, it automatically fails its save against this effect.

itemGroup.name: Toothy Brawl
entries: These weapons have {@b 6 teeth} and regrow {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended teeth daily at dawn. When all teeth have been expended, the weapons' damage type changes to Bludgeoning.

entries.name: Rough Handling
entries: Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to resist being grappled by you and on ability checks made to escape your grapple. Whenever a creature attempts to escape your grapple, it takes {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage.

entries.name: Dental Damage
entries: When you hit with an attack using these cesti, you can expend {@b 1 tooth} to deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage.

entries.name: Rocket Tooth
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can expend {@b 1 tooth} and replace one of your attacks with firing the tooth at a target within {@b 120 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack using a {@hit +5} bonus instead of your attack modifier. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Piercing damage.

entries.name: Toothy Brawl (2014)
entries: If you are using the 2014 version of the 5e rules, the Toothy Brawl has the following changes.
{@b Modification: Rough Handling.} Creatures have disadvantage on ability checks made to resist being grappled by you or to escape your grapple.

itemGroup.name: Nucleic DNArm
entries: This prosthesis has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Prosthesis Properties
entries: This prosthesis can enhance or replace an arm. It has the {@itemProperty Integrated|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms} ({@item Whip|XPHB}) property.

entries.name: Form Shift
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to inject a willing creature within {@b 5 feet} of you with one of the following mutations of your choice until the end of its next turn:

items.name: Assault Forme
entries: The target's weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 2d6} damage on a hit.

items.name: Carapaced Forme
entries: The target gains a +3 bonus to Armor Class.

items.name: Nimble Forme
entries: The target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 20 feet.

itemGroup.name: Emotional Dis-Dress
entries: This item has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Heart On My Sleeve
entries: This magical clothing adapts to fit your thoughts and mood. While touching it, you can cause it to change into any article of clothing, though it always retains its organic texture and tendrils. While wearing it, it subtly shifts its colour to match your current emotional state.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this item, you can cast the spells on the following table from it (save {@dc 13}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.
When you cast {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} in this way, each unwilling creature that succeeds on the saving throw takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage.

entries.name: Furious Fabric
entries: The clothing's tendrils lash out independently based on your feelings of stress and danger. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are below your Hit Point maximum, you can use the clothing to make a melee weapon attack against a target within {@b 5 feet} of you. The attack has {@hit +5} attack modifier and deals {@damage 2d4} Bludgeoning damage.

itemGroup.name: Mantis Menace
entries: This Kusarigama has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn. Whenever you deal damage with this weapon, it deals Necrotic damage instead of its normal damage types.

entries.name: Clawing Fear
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack using the counterweight of this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to attempt to frighten it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 13} Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of you for {@b 1 minute}. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.

entries.name: Draining Fear
entries: Whenever you deal damage to a {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature with this weapon, its Hit Point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the Necrotic damage taken. A creature's Hit Point maximum returns to normal when it finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hungering Scythe
entries: When you make an attack with the blade of this weapon, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to extend it into a scythe of ghostly flame (no action required). Until the end of the turn, it deals an extra {@damage 1d8} damage on a hit.

itemGroup.name: Crablaster
entries: This Rifle has the {@itemProperty RDX|HFN2025|Reload} (6) property and doesn't require ammunition. Instead, it fires high pressure water blasts, and requires 1 gallon of water to reload. While you are underwater, reloading the Rifle doesn't require an action, as long as you aren't {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}.

entries.name: Aqua Jet
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can propel yourself {@b 10 feet} backwards without provoking {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}. If you're submerged in at least 5 feet of water, you move backwards {@b 15 feet} instead. Using this property expends a shot, as if firing a bullet.

entries.name: Rain of Ammunition
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while the Rifle has at least one shot remaining, you can empty the tank of the Rifle to rain down on your enemies. The Rifle loses any remaining ammunition. Choose a point within the Rifle's normal range. Each creature in a {@b 10-foot-radius, 30-foot-high} {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on that point makes a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. If the Rifle had at least three shots remaining, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

itemGroup.name: Darkflame's Embrace
entries: This armour has {@b 5 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this armour, you can cast spells on the Darkflame's Embrace Spells table from the armour (save {@dc 15}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

entries.name: Phantom Blade
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to envelop a weapon you're holding in ghostly flame until the start of your next turn or until you let go of it. While enveloped in these flames, the weapon's damage type changes to Fire or Necrotic (your choice), and it deals an extra {@damage 3d6} damage of the chosen type on a hit.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon, and you can use it as if it were a drum. This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Percussion
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and tap a rhythm on this weapon while holding it with both hands. Choose up to two other creatures that can hear you. Until the start of your next turn, each creature deals extra Fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) the next time it hits with an attack.

entries.name: Concussion
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack made with this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to cause a blaze of hellfire to erupt from it (no action required). The target and each creature other than yourself within {@b 5 feet} of it must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon, and you can use it as if it were a drum. This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Percussion
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and tap a rhythm on this weapon while holding it with both hands. Choose up to two other creatures that can hear you. Until the start of your next turn, each creature deals extra Fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) the next time it hits with an attack.

entries.name: Concussion
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack made with this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to cause a blaze of hellfire to erupt from it (no action required). The target and each creature other than yourself within {@b 5 feet} of it must make a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made using this magic weapon, and you can use it as if it were a drum. This item has {@b 4 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Percussion
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to expend {@b 1 charge} and tap a rhythm on this weapon while holding it with both hands. Choose up to two other creatures that can hear you. Until the start of your next turn, each creature deals extra Fire damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1) the next time it hits with an attack.

entries.name: Concussion
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack made with this weapon, you can expend {@b 2 charges} to cause a blaze of hellfire to erupt from it (no action required). The target and each creature other than yourself within {@b 5 feet} of it must make a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a failed saving throw, or half as much damage on a successful one.
This item has {@b 3 charges} and regains {@dice 1d3} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Finesse
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

entries.name: Whirling Strikes
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to whirl this weapon around you. Each creature within {@b 10 feet} of you must succeed on a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d6} Slashing damage and be pushed {@b 5 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Chuck-a-Chomp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to launch a chuck-a-chomp toward a Small or larger creature within {@b 30 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage, and the chuck-a-chomp sticks to it. The chuck-a-chomp remains attached for {@b 1 minute}. It detaches early if you use this property again or a creature uses an action to remove the chuck-a-chomp from itself or a creature within its reach. While the chuck-a-chomp is attached, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns to deal {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage to the target.
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
This item has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Finesse
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

entries.name: Whirling Strikes
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to whirl this weapon around you. Each creature within {@b 10 feet} of you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Slashing damage and be pushed {@b 5 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Chuck-a-Chomp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to launch a chuck-a-chomp toward a Small or larger creature within {@b 30 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage, and the chuck-a-chomp sticks to it. The chuck-a-chomp remains attached for {@b 1 minute}. It detaches early if you use this property again or a creature uses an action to remove the chuck-a-chomp from itself or a creature within its reach. While the chuck-a-chomp is attached, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns to deal {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage to the target.
You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls made with this magic weapon.
This item has {@b 7 charges} and regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Finesse
entries: This weapon has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} property.

entries.name: Whirling Strikes
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to whirl this weapon around you. Each creature within {@b 10 feet} of you must succeed on a {@dc 16} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d10} Slashing damage and be pushed {@b 5 feet} away from you.

entries.name: Chuck-a-Chomp
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to launch a chuck-a-chomp toward a Small or larger creature within {@b 30 feet} of you. Make a ranged weapon attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage, and the chuck-a-chomp sticks to it. The chuck-a-chomp remains attached for {@b 1 minute}. It detaches early if you use this property again or a creature uses an action to remove the chuck-a-chomp from itself or a creature within its reach. While the chuck-a-chomp is attached, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns to deal {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} Acid damage to the target.

As with all things kobold-derived, the Kablowbold Suit can be a little... inconsistent. Part squirrel suit, part artillery, and partly reliable, its tiny rocket engine can guarantee escape from any well, canyon, or tarrasque's stomach, provided the escape is guaranteed to take six seconds or less.

entries.name: Glide
entries: When you fall at least {@b 30 feet}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deploy the suit's wings. If you do, subtract up to {@b 100 feet} from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you can move up to 2 feet horizontally for every 1 foot you descend.

entries.name: Flight
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing the armour, you can pull a toggle, activating a small rocket and gaining a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of {@b 30 feet} for {@b 1 minute}. At the end of each of your turns, roll a {@dice d6}. On a 1-2, you immediately lose the {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} and fall {@b 200 feet}. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while wearing the armour, you can pull a toggle, activating a small rocket and gaining a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of {@b 30 feet} for {@b 1 minute}. This property can be used twice, regaining all expended uses daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bombs Away
entries: There are two incendiary bombs attached to the chest of the armour. While wearing the armour and flying, you can use an action to drop one bomb directly below you, which immediately falls {@b 200 feet}, and another {@b 200 feet} at the start of each of your turns, until it hits a surface or creature. When it does, the bomb explodes in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. Each creature in the area must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} Fire damage plus {@damage 2d8} Thunder damage and falls {@condition Prone|XPHB}. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. Dropped bombs reappear on the suit daily at dawn.

You have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with an attack using this weapon, it deals your choice of Piercing or Slashing damage.

entries.name: Vine Hilt
entries: When you grasp the weapon's hilt, the blades' vines wrap around your forearm. You can't be disarmed of this weapon. If you choose to drop it, the vines hold it within easy grasp of your hand. On your turn, you can take hold of the item again (no action required). A creature can take {@b 1 minute} to gently detach a Laceblade from itself or the forearm of a creature within its reach.

entries.name: Optional Attunement
entries: If you choose to attune to this item, you can use the Quickvine property while you remain attuned to it.

entries.name: OA: Quickvine
entries: When a willing creature within {@b 20 feet} of you is targeted by an attack from a creature you can see, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause a vine to whip out and pull the creature up to {@b 15 feet} directly towards you, causing the attack to miss if the creature is pulled out of the attack's reach or range. As an action, you can pull a willing creature within {@b 20 feet} of you {@b 15 feet} directly towards you. This property can be used twice, and regains all expended uses daily at dawn.

This bow doesn't require ammunition; an arrow of red smoke forms whenever it's drawn. It has {@b 2 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Bat Bolt
entries: As an action, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to fire a special arrow at a point you can see within the bow's normal range. Where the arrow lands, it swirls into ruby smoke, creating a {@creature Swarm of Vitality Bats|HFN2025} under your control.

The swarm shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to command it to take a different action. The action can be one in the swarm's stat block or some other action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to command the swarm, you can command all swarms you've made with this weapon using the same {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The swarm lasts for {@b 1 minute}, until it dies, or until you dismiss it (no action required).

entries.name: Smoky Escape
entries: As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are hit by an attack, you can disappear in a swirl of red smoke, teleporting to an unoccupied space you can see within {@b 30 feet} of you. Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn unless 1 charge is expended as part of the {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to use it again.
This armor has {@b 5 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sticky Situation
entries: Whenever a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, its Speed is reduced by {@b 5 feet} until the start of its next turn. If a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 by this property, it's {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Swarm
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause a swarm of bees to surround you until the start of your next turn. When you activate this property, choose attack or defence. Your choice determines the effect created while the swarm lasts:

items.name: Attack
entries: When any creature of your choice enters a space within {@b 5 feet} of you for the first time on a turn, it takes 2 ({@damage 1d4}) Piercing damage.

items.name: Defence
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to AC.
This armor has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

items.name: Attack
entries: When any creature of your choice enters a space within {@b 5 feet} of you for the first time on a turn, it takes 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Piercing damage.

items.name: Defence
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to AC.
This armor has {@b 6 charges} and regains {@dice 1d4 + 2} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Sticky Situation
entries: Whenever a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, its Speed is reduced by {@b 10 feet} until the start of its next turn. If a creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 by this property, it's {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to cause a swarm of bees to surround you until the start of your next turn. When you activate this property, choose attack or defence. Your choice determines the effect created while the swarm lasts:

items.name: Defence
