This armour has {@b 3 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this armour, you can cast spells on the Darkflame's Embrace Spells table from the armour (save {@dc 13}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

entries.name: Phantom Blade
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to envelop a weapon you're holding in ghostly flame until the start of your next turn or until you let go of it. While enveloped in these flames, the weapon's damage type changes to Fire or Necrotic (your choice), and it deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage of the chosen type on a hit.
This armour has {@b 5 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this armour, you can cast spells on the Darkflame's Embrace Spells table from the armour (save {@dc 15}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

entries.name: Phantom Blade
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to envelop a weapon you're holding in ghostly flame until the start of your next turn or until you let go of it. While enveloped in these flames, the weapon's damage type changes to Fire or Necrotic (your choice), and it deals an extra {@damage 3d6} damage of the chosen type on a hit.
This armour has {@b 6 charges} and regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Spells
entries: While wearing this armour, you can cast spells on the Darkflame's Embrace Spells table from the armour (save {@dc 16}). The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.

entries.name: Phantom Blade
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend {@b 1 charge} to envelop a weapon you're holding in ghostly flame until the start of your next turn or until you let go of it. While enveloped in these flames, the weapon's damage type changes to Fire or Necrotic (your choice), and it deals an extra {@damage 3d6} damage of the chosen type on a hit.

As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} whenever you take Fire damage while wearing this armour, you can gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage (including the triggering damage) until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Adaptable
entries: This weapon can be used with differing techniques, allowing it to deal different types of damage. A damage type in parentheses appears with the property— when a creature makes a weapon attack with this weapon, it can choose to deal the weapon's alternative damage type.

entries.name: Attached
entries: You can't be disarmed of this weapon, but donning or doffing the weapon takes an action. You can use a hand equipped with this weapon to hold items, but can't attack with the weapon while doing so. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls using other weapons held in a hand equipped with this weapon and on {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} checks using the hand that is equipped with this weapon.

entries.name: Loud (X)
entries: The sound of the weapon discharging alerts all hearing creatures within a number of feet as specified by the number in parentheses after the Loud property. This range is doubled in echoey locations, such as cave systems, and where sound travels faster, such as underwater.

entries.name: Reload (X)
entries: A limited number of shots, specified by the number in parentheses after the Reload property, can be made with this weapon. A character must then reload it using an action.

entries.name: Pugilist
entries: When rolling a die for the damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} using a limb equipped with a Pugilist weapon, the size of your damage die is increased by one (to a maximum of 1d12).

entries.name: Thrown Versatility
entries: This weapon is better used for thrown ranged weapon attacks than for melee weapon attacks. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property— the damage when the weapon is used to make a ranged weapon attack.

entries.name: Unwieldy
entries: This weapon is large and difficult to wield. Drawing or stowing the weapon requires a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet while you wield it. In addition, weapons with this property can't be used for two-weapon fighting.

The property information for each prosthesis with this property notes an object. The object is integrated into the prosthesis, and can't be separated from it, exchanged for another integrated object, or used to make ranged attacks by throwing it.

You can extend or retract the integrated object as your free object interaction. If the integrated object is a weapon, you can either extend or retract the weapon to equip or unequip it as you would other weapons. While an object is extended from an arm or hand prosthesis, you can manipulate it as if you were holding it, and your hand is not considered free.
The integrated object can be one of the following:

items: A weapon that lacks the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} and Superheavy properties
A handheld item that can conceivably fit within the prosthesis (GM's discretion)

A stash: a concealed storage space within the prosthesis, up to 35 inches cubed in volume, that can be used to store Tiny objects, such as rings, phials of liquid (e.g. potions), smoke bombs, pieces of ammunition, or a bag of {@cite Caltrops|XPHB} or {@item Ball Bearings|XPHB}. The user of the prosthesis can stow or retrieve an item from the stash as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. A creature can find this secret compartment with a successful {@dc 11} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check.

entries.name: Melee
entries: The prosthesis is a Martial Melee weapon. If you're proficient with a Martial Melee weapon, you're considered proficient with this weapon. The information in parentheses notes the weapon's damage dice, damage type, and any additional weapon properties it may have, respectively.

entries.name: Mobility
entries: While wearing this prosthesis, you gain a special {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, the type and amount of which are both indicated in parenthesis.

entries.name: General Statistics
entries: Every component has five core statistics:

entries.name: Armour Class (AC)
entries: This is how hard the component is to hit, a combination of its size and the material from which it's made.

entries.name: Damage Threshold (DT)
entries: This is the component's capacity to ignore damage, based on the hardiness of its material. Damage dealt to a component that does not exceed the DT is ignored. Resistances and Vulnerabilities are always calculated before determining if the damage exceeds the component's DT.

entries.name: Hit Points (HP)
entries: This is how much damage a component can sustain before it becomes Broken and nonfunctional.

entries.name: Broken
entries: When a component is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it becomes Broken. A component remains Broken until its current Hit Points are equal to or higher than its IT, at which point it becomes Impaired.
Broken components are generally inoperable, resulting in the generic effects in the Broken Component Effect table.

entries.caption: Broken Component Effect
rows: The component can't be used to make attacks.
A creature can no longer take a Ship Action to Manoeuvre, Accelerate, or Decelerate the vessel.
The component's primary and secondary speeds are 0.

entries.name: Impairment Threshold (IT)
entries: This is the number of Hit Points at which a component becomes Impaired

entries.name: Impaired
entries: Every component has an Impairment  (IT) indicated in its statistics. When the component is reduced to a number of Hit Points equal to or lower than its IT, it becomes Impaired. A component remains Impaired until its current Hit Points are restored to its Hit Point maximum (see Repairing Components).
Every component type has a generic effect that occurs when it is Impaired, as listed in the Impaired Component Effect table, though some specific components also have unique impairments.

entries.caption: Impaired Component Effect
rows: The component's long range is reduced to its normal range, and its normal range is reduced to its close range. The component loses its close range.
The component's Turn score is reduced by 1.
The ship takes on water, potentially damaging stores and cargo (GM's discretion). Its top speed is reduced by 30 feet (minimum 0 feet).
The component's primary and secondary speeds are reduced by 30 feet (minimum 0 feet).

entries.name: Constitution Saving Throw (Con)
entries: This is the component's Constitution saving throw modifier. This is a measure of the robustness of its construction; how well its joints are sealed against the ingress of acid or its ability to absorb the shock of a thunderclap.

entries.name: Artillery Components
entries: Artillery are the components that fire projectiles and energy to impair enemy vessels or attack hostile crew members.

entries.name: Attack Modifier
entries: When making an attack roll with an Artillery component, the modifier for the attack roll equals the Artillerist's ability modifier (the ability depends on the type of artillery) plus any additional bonus the Artillerist may have from Artillery perks.

entries.name: Artillery attack modifier
entries.text: artillerist's ability modifier + artillerists' Artillery perk bonuses

entries: Each nonplayer ship (NPS) has a crew artillery attack modifier. This is used in place of the previous calculation.

entries.name: Critical Hits
entries: As usual, a roll of a 20 on the d20 for an attack roll is a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. Like normal {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB|Critical Hits}, the attack hits regardless of AC and the damage dice are rolled twice to determine how much damage is dealt by that attack.

entries.name: Arcs of Fire
entries: Each ship has specific emplacements reinforced for use with Artillery components. The horizontal angles at which an artillery can be fired from that emplacement are shown in the ship's Deck Layout. Unless otherwise stated (such as by the Anti-Air trait, coming soon), an Artillery component's maximum vertical angle is 45 degrees. This means that if a flying creature is 95 feet away from the Artillery component and 100 feet above the deck on which the Artillery component is installed, the component can't target that creature; it can't point upwards enough.

entries.name: Range
entries: Artillery weapons have three ranges: close, normal, and long. These are indicated in the Artillery component's shot selection's statistics after the word "range". Eyepieces attached to magitech artillery always allow for accurate ranging; an Artillerist never has to guess a range.

Normal and long range work like regular ranged weapons: attack rolls against targets beyond an artillery's normal range are made at {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, while the long range indicates the maximum possible distance that the artillery can attack a target.

The shots of certain artillery components have unique effects—typically gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll—against specific targets within their close range. This is detailed in the artillery's shot selection. Close range also allows Artillery to attempt a Rake.

entries.caption: Range & Attack Rolls

colLabelRows.entry: Target Closer or Equal to this Range

footnotes: {@sup *} Creatures receive a bonus to AC against Artillery based on size (see the Bonus to Creature AC vs Artillery Attacks table).
{@sup **} Or other tall vertical structure, at the GM's discretion.
{@sup ***} See Shot Selection & Ammunition, on this page.

entries.name: Targeting Creatures
entries: Ships move predictably and are enormous targets. This makes them much easier to hit than the crew that man them. Accordingly, unless stated otherwise, creatures receive a bonus to their AC against attacks from Artillery components according to the creature's size (see Bonus to Creature AC vs Artillery Attack table). Artillery components with the Anti-Personel trait ignore this bonus to AC.

entries.caption: Bonus to Creature AC vs Artillery Attacks
colLabels: Bonus to AC vs Artillery Attacks

entries.name: Shot Selection & Ammunition
entries: Shot selection and ammunition choice are two different things. Shot is the form the ammunition takes, whereas ammunition is a special variant of that form. For example, cannons have three choices of shot: ball, chain, and grape.

entries.name: Ball Shot
entries: This is a single spherical ball, usually made of metal—the type of shot you would expect a cannon to fire. It deals the most damage and is great for punching through thick Hulls. This shot confers {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against Hulls and kaiju at close range.

entries.name: Chain Shot
entries: This consists of two smaller balls connected by a chain. When fired, the chain stretches horizontally, making it more likely to hit tall vertical structures like masts. This shot has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against masts and other tall vertical structures. The GM decides if a component is tall enough for an attack against it to gain this {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}, and an Artillerist always knows this information before they select their shot.

entries.name: Grape Shot
entries: A dozen or more smaller balls tightly wrapped and bound with cloth and rope forms a grape shot. When fired, the cloth rips, causing the shot to spread out in a Narrow Cone. Any creature in this area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equals 8 + the artillerist's ability modifier + the artillerist's perk bonuses). A creature takes damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one (the damage dice are detailed in the artillery's statistics).

Other weapons, like ballista, have a similar selection of shots; its javelin shot and splinter shot are the equivalent of ball shot and grape shot, respectively. Read the Shot Selection section of an Artillery component's statistics for details.

entries.name: Shot Selection
entries: An Artillerist chooses which shot to load when it makes an attack roll with an Artillery component. It makes this decision and loads this shot as part of the Ship Action it takes to use the component.

entries.name: Ammunition
entries: Imaginative artificers can augment a type of shot with magical or mechanical alterations, causing them to unleash incapacitating gases, destructive explosions, or arcanomagnetic pulses that can disable components. These variations are referred to as ammunition. A full list of ammunition will be presented in future playtests.

feat.name: Psychosomatic Reality
entries: A brush with the unknowable entities from beyond the physical planes has imbued you with a vivid, psionic imagination. You develop the power to manifest your thoughts in the physical world. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Manifest Thoughts
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and one or more free hands to mime a Small or Tiny imaginary object, such as a {@item Dagger|XPHB} or {@item Rope|XPHB}. So long as you are miming, the object interacts with the world as if it were real, and it disappears as soon as you are no longer using a free hand to mime it. Objects mimed in this way are only visible to creatures with Truesight or creatures under the effect of the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell.

entries.name: Mental Block
entries: You can cast the {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB} spell using only a Somatic component. When you do, you can only create 1 panel instead of 10, but the panel can be up to 30 feet long and 10 feet tall. Once you have used this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Impossible Wielder
entries: Training in advanced, unorthodox techniques has accelerated your mastery in the martial arts, granting you the following benefits:

entries.name: Weapon Training
entries: You gain proficiency with a Martial weapon of your choice.

entries.name: Mastery
entries: You learn one {@variantrule Advanced Weapon Masteries|RyokoGuideToYokaiRealms|Advanced Technique} of your choice from any {@filter Technique Tree|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=AWM;FWM;HWM;IWM;LWM;LRWM;PWM;RWM;SCWM;SWM;TWM;VWM}{@sup *}. You must meet the prerequisites for the technique to learn it.
{@note *See Ryoko's Guide to the Yokai Realms}

entries.name: Armoury of One
entries: While you are wielding no weapons with the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can wield a {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} weapon using your mouth or tail (if applicable) for 1 minute. When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action while wielding a weapon in this way, you can make one additional attack using this weapon. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of this extra attack unless that modifier is negative. Once you have wielded a weapon in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Last Stand
entries: Power lies latent in your blood—power that can only be released in moments of true desperation. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

entries.name: Out with a Bang
entries: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, energy explodes from your body in a destructive wave. Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus). Creatures of your choice have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes a number of d10s of Force damage equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. Once the explosion happens, it can't happen again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

feat.name: Flaming Fury
entries: By infusing your soul with energy from the Plane of Fire, flames flow through your blood and can ignite when enough of it is spilled.

entries.name: Catch These Hands on Fire
entries: While you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, your melee weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Fire damage on a hit. While you have only 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, the Fire damage increases to {@damage 1d8}.

entries.name: Blaze of Glory
entries: Your inner flame keeps your body moving even at the point of collapse. If damage would reduce you to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can make a Constitution saving throw with a DC of 5 plus the damage taken. On a success, you're reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. Once you've succeeded on this saving throw, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Painted Lady Patron
entries: Those who gain the eye and favour of a painted lady are burdened with the role of a hero. A bitter oil painter who seeks to paint their rival's hometown as it burns, a student of watercolour trying desperately to recapture the figure of their lost lover, a sketch artist trying to produce the face of their mother's killer—all of these are prime candidates for the fiendish muse. Nothing pleases a painted lady more than myths of tragic heroes with artistic flair.

A Warlock who wins the aid of one of these devils always has something to prove and the potential to become a magnificent work of art. Most come pre-traumatized with at least one agonizing memory. If they don't, the lady is always happy to provide one along with its power. Tragedy is key to creativity, after all.

Armed with imagination, magic, and trauma, Warlocks of the Painted Lady are expected to show excellence and nothing less. Each battle inspires a new artwork, and each artwork is a step towards greater arcane power. The infernal cycle of art is accelerated by conflict, and many Warlocks share their patron's competitive spirit. Contests of artistry, tactics, and magic are common among the followers of the painted ladies, occasionally escalating into full-scale tournaments. Winners are adorned with accolades, while losers are subject to more… character development.

subclassFeature.name: Painted Lady Spells
entries: {@i 1st-level Painted Lady feature}

The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Painted Lady Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Palette
entries: {@i 1st-level Painted Lady feature}

Spells, like paint, should come in a wide variety of hues and values. Whenever you cast a Warlock cantrip that deals damage, you can change the colour and damage type to green (Acid), blue (Cold), red (Fire), or yellow (Lightning).
When you reach higher levels in this class, you have access to additional colours: black (Necrotic) and white (Radiant) at level 6, and violet (Psychic) at level 10.

subclassFeature.name: Fume Acclimation
entries: {@i 1st-level Painted Lady feature}
Long exposure to paint fumes has left you resilient to the substances used to make them. You have resistance to Acid and Poison damage.

subclassFeature.name: Competitive Skill Set
entries: {@i 6th-level Painted Lady feature}

Your patron grants you an edge over the competition in a skill of your choice. When you finish a short or long rest, choose a skill. You have double your proficiency bonus for ability checks you make that use that skill until you finish a short or long rest. If you fail an ability check that uses that skill proficiency, you take Psychic damage equal to three times your proficiency bonus as punishment for your failure.
When you reach level 14 in this class, you can choose two skills to gain the effects of this feature each time you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Colourful Strikes
entries: {@i 10th-level Painted Lady feature}

Specialized paints crafted with the aid of your patron can imbue weapons with new capabilities. As an action, you can touch a weapon and paint it with one of the following colours, which determines its effect. The paint job lasts for 1 hour or until you paint another weapon.

items.name: Cyan
items.entry: The first time a creature is hit by this weapon on a turn, its wielder can teleport to an unoccupied space it can see within 10 feet of it.

items.name: Magenta
items.entry: Attacks made using this weapon ignore resistances, temporary hit points, and spells that raise the target's AC.

items.name: Yellow
items.entry: When a creature is hit by this weapon, its wielder chooses another creature within 10 feet of it. That creature takes Lightning damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

items.name: Black
items.entry: The first time a creature is hit by this weapon on a turn, that creature subtracts {@dice 1d4} from the next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes before the end of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Character Development
entries: {@i 14th-level Painted Lady feature}

Overcoming your darkest moments grants you the power of a legendary hero, transforming you into a masterpiece version of yourself. When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can choose to drop to 1 hit point instead (no action required). Your body transforms into a work of art; you regain 1 hit point, and you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your Warlock level + twice your Charisma modifier.
Your AC can't be lower than 20.
You gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Warlock spells.
You have immunity to the {@condition charmed|PHB}, {@condition exhaustion|PHB}, {@condition frightened|PHB}, and {@condition paralyzed|PHB|paralysed} conditions.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Painted Lady Patron
entries: Those who gain the eye and favour of a painted lady are burdened with the role of a hero. A bitter oil painter who seeks to paint their rival's hometown as it burns, a student of watercolour trying desperately to recapture the figure of their lost lover, a sketch artist trying to produce the face of their mother's killer—all of these are prime candidates for the fiendish muse. Nothing pleases a painted lady more than myths of tragic heroes with artistic flair.

A Warlock who wins the aid of one of these devils always has something to prove and the potential to become a magnificent work of art. Most come pre-traumatized with at least one agonizing memory. If they don't, the lady is always happy to provide one along with its power. Tragedy is key to creativity, after all.

The infernal cycle of art is accelerated by conflict, and many Warlocks share their patron's competitive spirit. Contests of artistry, tactics, and magic are common among the followers of the painted ladies, occasionally escalating into full-scale tournaments. Winners are adorned with accolades, while losers are subject to more… character development.

The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Painted Lady Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Painted Lady Spells
rows: {@spell Chameleon Skin|HFN2025}, {@spell Color Spray|XPHB}, {@spell Hallucinogenic Cloud|HFN2025}, {@spell Inequality|HFN2025}

You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If you fail an ability check that uses that skill proficiency, you take Psychic damage equal to three times your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} as punishment for your failure.
When you reach level 14 in this class, you can choose two skills to gain the effects of this feature each time you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries: {@i 10th-level Painted Lady feature}

Specialized paints crafted with the aid of your patron can imbue weapons with new capabilities. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can touch a weapon and paint it with one of the following colours, which determines its effect. The paint job lasts for 1 hour or until you paint another weapon.

items.name: Cyan

items: You gain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Temporary Hit Points} equal to twice your Warlock level + twice your Charisma modifier.
Your AC can't be lower than 20.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Wield Weapons While Wild Shaped
entries: Druids of the Circle of the Blade carry a reputation all their own amongst their fellow sages, favouring strength not through serene communion with nature, but martial prowess. Though they are skilled fighters in their own right, it's when they take on a wild shape that they become truly unparalleled— combining the feral instincts of their animal forms and the sharpness of their steel to dispose of foes as swiftly as a falcon snatches a rabbit.

Druids of this circle make their homes where civilisation and nature intersect, building relationships with nearby settlements through the trade of rare goods, such as the magical xyxlwood, to the chagrin of their more reclusive peers. Still, they prefer to spend their days honing their combat skills by sparring with one another—a graceful, intricate waltz where steel meets claw in a whirlwind of shifting feral forms.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Blade Spells
entries: {@i 2nd-level Circle of the Blade feature}

When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Blade Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Once you gain access to a spell from this feature, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare, but those spells otherwise count as Druid spells for you.
In addition, you can cast the spells from this feature while you are in a Wild Shape form.

subclassFeature.name: Feral Technique
entries: {@i 2nd-level Circle of the Blade feature}
Your martial prowess shines, granting you the benefits below while you are in a Wild Shape form. In addition, you can assume a Wild Shape form as a bonus action.

items.name: Martial Training
items.entry: You can wield weapons you're proficient with regardless of your Beast form's anatomy, such as by holding them in your mouth or tail.

items.name: Temporary Hit Points
items.entry: When you assume the form, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to three times your Druid level.

items.name: Bestial Techniques
items.entry: You can use one technique of your choice from the "Bestial Techniques" section later in this subclass's description; your chosen form must have at least one of the pieces of anatomy associated with that technique. If a technique has a level requirement, you must be at least that Druid level to use it. When you reach Druid level 10, you can use two techniques instead.

entries.name: Bestial Techniques
entries: The following techniques are presented by level prerequisite, and then in alphabetical order.

entries.caption: Bestial Techniques
rows: If you move at least 15 feet straight toward a target immediately before hitting it with an attack using a Melee weapon, you can knock the target {@condition prone}.
Digitigrade or ungulate legs built for running at high speeds
You can take the Dash or Disengage action as a bonus action if you've made an attack with a Melee weapon that turn.
Opposable thumbs, articulated tail, tentacles, or similar anatomy that allows for a precise grip
You can use the mastery property of one kind of Melee weapon with which you are proficient. Choose the weapon when you assume Beast form.
Claws, hooves, pincers, talons, or spurs
Immediately after you reduce a creature to 0 hit points using an attack using a Melee weapon, you can use a bonus action to make one attack using your Beast form's natural weapons.
Fangs, sharp beak, or blood-sucking proboscis
You have advantage on attack rolls made with Melee weapons against Bloodied creatures.
When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can take a reaction to make one attack using a Melee weapon against the creature.
When you hit a creature with an attack using a Melee weapon, it can't make opportunity attacks until the start of its next turn.
Pincers, tentacles, or a long limbless body capable of coiling around a target

When you hit a creature with an attack using a Melee weapon as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can attempt to grapple the target as part of the same action. You can use this technique only once per turn.
Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using a Melee weapon, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Poison damage to the target.

subclassFeature.name: Savage Blade
entries: {@i 2nd-level Circle of the Blade feature}
When you gain this feature, you also gain proficiency with martial weapons and training with medium armour.
You can cast {@spell Shillelagh|PHB} as part of the bonus action used to enter a Wild Shape form.

When you cast the spell in this way, you can imbue any one Melee weapon you're proficient with and that you're holding, not just a Club or Quarterstaff. Alternatively, you can magically conjure a mundane Melee weapon you're proficient with, which gains the benefits of the spell and lasts for as long as you remain in a Wild Shape form.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: {@i 6th-level Circle of the Blade feature}
You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn while in a Wild Shape form.

subclassFeature.name: Blade Parry
entries: {@i 10th-level Circle of the Blade feature}

When another creature hits you with a melee attack, you can take a reaction to try to deflect the attack. Make an attack roll with a Melee weapon you're wielding. If the result of your attack roll is higher than the triggering attack's, that attack automatically misses.

subclassFeature.name: Shifting Form
entries: {@i 14th-level Circle of the Blade feature}

Your form is as swift as your blade. As a bonus action while in a Wild Shape form, you can shift into a different Beast form you know. When you do so, you gain temporary hit points equal to twice your Druid level, and you gain two techniques of your choice that match your new form's anatomy, as per your Feral Technique feature.

entries.name: Wield Weapons While Wild Shaped
entries: Druids of the Circle of the Blade carry a reputation all their own amongst their fellow sages, favouring strength not through serene communion with nature, but martial prowess. Though they are skilled fighters in their own right, it's when they take on a wild shape that they become truly unparalleled— combining the feral instincts of their animal forms and the sharpness of their steel to dispose of foes as swiftly as a falcon snatches a rabbit.

Druids of this circle make their homes where civilisation and nature intersect, building relationships with nearby settlements through the trade of rare goods, such as the magical xyxlwood, to the chagrin of their more reclusive peers. Still, they prefer to spend their days honing their combat skills by sparring with one another—a graceful, intricate waltz where steel meets claw in a whirlwind of shifting feral forms.

entries: {@i 3rd-level Circle of the Blade feature}
When you reach a Druid level specified in the Circle of the Blade Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.
In addition, you can cast the spells from this feature while you are in a Wild Shape form.

entries.caption: Circle of the Blade Spells
rows: {@spell Barkskin|XPHB}, {@spell Expeditious Retreat|XPHB}, {@spell Searing Smite|XPHB}, {@spell Shillelagh|XPHB}, {@spell Shining Smite|XPHB}
