subclassFeature.name: Shifting Form
entries: {@i 14th-level Circle of the Blade feature}

Your form is as swift as your blade. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while in a Wild Shape form, you can shift into a different Beast form you know. When you do so, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Druid level, and you gain two techniques of your choice that match your new form's anatomy, as per your Feral Technique feature.
That portrait is absolutely hideous! You'll never impress Her Ladyship with something so atrocious. Go stash it in an attic somewhere before it ruins your good looks!
entries.by: Doreen Wilde, Art-canist and Tutor Extraordinaire

entries: That portrait is absolutely hideous! You'll never impress Her Ladyship with something so atrocious. Go stash it in an attic somewhere before it ruins your good looks!
entries.by: Doreen Wilde, Art-canist and Tutor Extraordinaire

spell.name: Arcanomagnetic Pulse
entries: You create a powerful arcanomagnetic charge at a point you can see within range, pulling in nearby creatures before discharging violently. Each creature within a 30-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on that point must make a Strength saving throw.

On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d8} Lightning damage and is pulled straight towards the point. On a successful save, a creature within 20 feet of the point takes half as much damage only, and a creature further away suffers no effect.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Magnetise
entries: You magnetically shift a ferrous object that weighs no more than 100 pounds and is within range, moving it 5 feet straight towards or away from you (your choice). If a creature is wearing or carrying the object, that creature must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, if a creature is wearing the object, the creature is moved 5 feet straight towards or away from you (your choice). If it's carrying the object, the object is moved 5 feet straight towards or away from you (your choice), disarming the creature.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The spell pushes objects farther when you reach levels 5 (up to 10 feet), 11 (up to 15 feet), and 17 (up to 20 feet).

spell.name: Freezing Fog
entries: You create a 15-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of icy fog centred on a point within range. The Sphere is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as the one created by {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}) disperses it. Whenever a creature enters the Sphere or starts its turn there, it becomes {@condition Wet|HFN2025}. A {@condition Wet|HFN2025} creature that takes Cold damage while in the area is {@condition Restrained|XPHB} until the end of its next turn or until it takes Fire damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The fog's radius increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Lingering Image
entries: You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within range, and you either leave an illusory copy of yourself in the space you left, or you create an illusory copy of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within range (your choice). The illusory copy lasts for the duration, or until it is hit by an attack.
As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is within range.

A creature that takes a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine the image can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the creature can see through the image, and its other sensory qualities become faint to the creature.

The illusion has an AC equal to 10 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. If a creature hits the illusion with an attack, the illusion dissipates and the creature takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Anchor Bones
entries: You touch a willing creature and imbue it with the steadiness of an anchor. For the duration, the target's weight is doubled, and as long as the target is submerged in at least 1 foot of liquid, it is {@condition Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and can't be moved against its will.

spell.name: Uplifting Chime
entries: You ring a sequence of clear, beautiful notes on a bell, bringing life to those who hear it. Up to six creatures of your choice within range regain 1 {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB}. If a creature isn't missing any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it gains {@dice 2d4} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} instead. A {@condition Deafened|XPHB} creature is unaffected by this spell.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} gained increase by {@dice 1d4} for every spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Living Marionette
entries: You attempt to attach magical threads to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have 5 threads attached to it. While the threads are attached, the target can't willingly move more than 60 feet away from you, and the spell ends early if you are ever more than 60 feet away from the target. For the duration, you can pull on one or more threads in one of the following ways:

items: When the target moves, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to snap one thread to halve its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} until the end of the turn.
When the target makes an attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to snap one thread to give it {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.
You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to snap one thread to move the target up to half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} in any direction.

You can take a {@variantrule Action|XPHB} to snap two threads to force the target to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and make one Melee Weapon Attack against a target of your choice within its reach.

entries: Once a thread has been snapped, it's expended and can't be used again. When the last thread is snapped, the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The number of threads increases by 1 for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Inhabit Object
entries: You touch a Small or Tiny object and transfer your consciousness into it. While your consciousness is in the object, you can see and hear using your own senses as if you were in its space, and your body is considered {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}.

The object has 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, can't attack, and has no {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} unless it has the anatomy to move, such as legs or wheels, in which case it has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 15 feet. While the object is motionless, a creature without {@sense Truesight|XPHB} can't distinguish it from an ordinary object of its type. If the object is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or your body takes damage, the spell ends and you wake up in your body.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The object's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} increase by 5 for each spell slot level above 2 and the maximum size of object you can target increases with a spell slot of level 4–5 (Medium), 6–7 (Large), 8 (Huge), and 9 (Gargantuan).

spell.name: Terraconstruct
entries: You create a hard ceramic shell around yourself for the duration. You gain {@dice 4d8} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, and the spell ends early when you lose all of them.

For the duration, your size becomes Large unless it's already bigger, and you have resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. The {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} take double damage from Bludgeoning and Thunder damage.
Additionally, when you deal damage with an Unarmed Strike, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Strength modifier, instead of the strike's normal damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} increase by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Blood Moon
entries: A menacing beam of blood-red moonlight shines down in a 15-foot-radius, 60-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point within range. Until the spell ends, {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} fills the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder}, and creatures within the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

When a creature enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, its blood begins to roil, causing excruciating pain, and it makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 5d10} Necrotic damage and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. Creatures without blood automatically succeed on this saving throw and take no damage on a success.
On each of your turns after you cast this spell, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to move the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} 60 feet in any direction.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Eclipse

m.text: a chunk of extraterrestrial stone worth 1,500+ GP

entries: You block out the light of the sun, filling a 10-mile {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you with magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}. {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} can't see through it, all light sources within the area are extinguished, and no light can illuminate it. Illusion spells of level 8 or lower within the area are dispelled.

When you cast this spell, you can specify up to ten creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. For the duration, the specified creatures gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Radiant damage and can see through the {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} created by this spell as if it were {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}.

spell.name: Moonveil
entries: You cause pale moonlight to fill a 25-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you with {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. For the duration, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, and {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks made to study anything within the moonlight. Creatures can't willingly shape-shift while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}.

spell.name: Watchful Eye
entries: You touch a willing creature and place an invisible sigil of protection over it. Until the spell ends, you are magically alerted whenever the creature is the target of Divination magic.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Acid Rain
entries: With a gentle patter, corrosive raindrops build to an acidic deluge in a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-tall {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder}, centred on a point within range. When the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} appears, each creature in it makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Acid damage on failure, or half as much damage on a success. A creature also makes this save when it enters the spell's area or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Acid damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Blood Frenzy

components.m: a drop of blood, less than 24 hours old

entries: You touch a willing creature, sharpening its focus with the instincts of a killer. Until the spell ends, the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls it makes against Bloodied creatures. In addition, if there is at least one Bloodied creature within 30 feet of the target, and the target considers that creature Hostile, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls it makes against creatures above half their {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Brainfreeze
entries: You cause a sudden, freezing pain in the brain of a creature you can see within range, which must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Cold damage and {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage, and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws it makes to maintain Concentration for the duration. In addition, on each of your subsequent turns for the spell's duration, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to deal {@damage 2d6} Cold damage to it. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and suffers no additional effects; the spell then ends.
At the end of each of its turns, an affected target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Cold damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Calm Waters
entries: With a peaceful gesture and murmuring word, you calm the water in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} centred on a point within range for the duration. In the area, a tumultuous stream becomes swimmable, a stormy ocean gains a moment of reprieve, and the effects of water-based spells of level 2 or lower, such as the {@spell Riptide|RGTYR} spell, are prevented and suppressed.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You suppress the effects of a water-based spell if its level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you use.

spell.name: Chameleon Skin
entries: You imbue a creature you touch with pigment so that its skin, and anything it wears or carries, slowly shifts hues to match its surroundings. For the duration, the target can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of its turns, even when only {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}.

If the target did not move during its last turn, creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks made to see the target. Creatures who do not rely on sight, such as those with {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB}, are {@condition Immunity|XPHB|Immune} to this effect.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Coldsnap
entries: You choose a point you can see within range, causing temperatures within a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on that point to plummet. Water in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} instantly freezes solid, thawing 10 minutes later at room temperature, 1 hour later in cold conditions, and 1 minute later in hot ones. Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} must immediately make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 4d8} Cold damage and has its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage and suffers no other effects.

In addition, a {@condition Wet|HFN2025} creature in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become {@condition Restrained|XPHB} for 1 minute. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} target can take an action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC, freeing itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Coralskin
entries: You touch a willing creature, turning their skin into a rapidly growing landscape of varicoloured coral for the spell's duration.

When you cast the spell, and at the start of each of the target's turns for the spell's duration, the creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d4} plus your spellcasting ability modifier. These {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} vanish if any remain when the spell ends.
In addition, when the target uses its Unarmed Strike to deal damage, it deals an extra {@damage 1d4} Slashing damage.

spell.name: Corrupting Ichor
entries: You spit a globule of phlegm at one creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage. In addition, until it takes an action to wipe off the phlegm, it takes {@damage 1d4} Necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns and any {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regained are reduced by half.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage at the start of the target's next turn increases by {@dice 1d4} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Detect Emotion
entries: You gain a preternatural ability to detect the most prominent emotion of creatures near you. As part of the action you use to cast this spell and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns, you can focus on one creature you can see within 15 feet of you. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or you learn which emotion it feels most strongly at that moment: Anger, Disgust, Joy, Fear, Sadness, or Surprise.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The range at which you can detect a creature's emotion doubles when you reach levels 5 (30 feet), 11 (60 feet), and 17 (120 feet).

spell.name: Enrage
entries: You attempt to create violent emotions in a group of people. Each Humanoid in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on a point you choose within range must make a Charisma saving throw; a creature can choose to fail this saving throw if it wishes. A creature that fails this saving throw becomes enraged for the duration.

While enraged, a target becomes Hostile towards all creatures. An enraged creature that is concentrating on a spell must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw at the start of each of its turns or lose Concentration on the spell.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can force an enraged creature to use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against one randomly determined creature within the enraged creature's reach. If the enraged creature was Friendly towards the target of its attack before this spell was cast, the enraged creature can repeat the saving throw immediately after making the attack, ending the effect on itself on a success.

An enraged creature can repeat the saving throw whenever it takes damage, ending the effect on itself on a success. When the spell ends, the creature reverts to its previous disposition again, unless the GM rules otherwise.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: When you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to force an enraged creature to make a melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike, you can force one additional enraged creature to make a melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Enthusing Cheer
entries: You jollify up to four willing creatures of your choice (other than yourself) within range. Any effect causing a target to be {@condition Frightened|XPHB} ends on it. Additionally, each creature gains {@dice 1d4} {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and can immediately make a saving throw against any effect that is causing it to be {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}. The creature suffers no additional effect on a failed saving throw.

spell.name: Firther's Shadow
entries: You warp and distort the memories of a creature you can see within range, bringing forth hideous and disturbing mental images. The creature must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 4d10} Psychic damage and you learn something about the creature (roll a d6 and consult the following table):

rows: The creature's damage {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerabilities}, {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances}, and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB|Immunities}
The creature's skill and saving throw proficiencies

entries: On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and you don't learn anything. A creature with an Intelligence score of 4 or lower automatically succeeds on the saving throw.

If a creature fails the initial saving throw then, on each of your turns for the duration, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to force the creature to make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage and you learn something about the creature (roll a d6 and consult the table above). On a success, the creature takes half as much damage, you don't learn anything, and the spell ends. The spell also ends if the target is ever outside the spell's range.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The initial and ongoing damage increases by {@dice 1d10} and {@dice 1d6}, respectively, for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Flash

time.condition: which you take when a creature within 10 feet of you that you can see targets you with an attack

entries: You exploit a creature's focus on you to detonate a blinding flash of light. The attacking creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the end of its next turn.

spell.name: Hallucinogenic Cloud
entries: You create a burst of brightly coloured, hallucinogenic pollen in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centred on a point within range. Each creature in the area must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature sees hallucinations of horrifying monsters all around it, and is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of those visions for the duration. A creature that ends its turn {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way takes {@damage 2d6} Psychic damage and must immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to uselessly attack one of the hallucinations, after which it can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Inequality

components.m: a drop of blood and water, mixed

entries: You twist the fates of two creatures you can see, both of whom must be within range. Choose one target to favour and one to disfavour. The disfavoured target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or the following effects occur:

items: Whenever the favoured target makes an attack roll or saving throw before the spell ends, the favoured target can roll a {@dice d4} and add the number rolled to the attack or saving throw.

Whenever the disfavoured target makes an attack roll or saving throw before the spell ends, the disfavoured target must roll a {@dice d4} and subtract the number rolled from the attack or saving throw.
The disfavoured target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved, whilst the favoured target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by half.

entries: These effects end for the pair if either of the target pair is knocked {@condition Unconscious|XPHB}, killed, or the spell ends. While under the influence of this spell, a favoured target can't benefit from the {@spell Bless|XPHB} spell, and a disfavoured target can't be afflicted by the {@spell Bane|XPHB} spell.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can link two other creatures, favouring one, and disfavouring the other, for every two spell slot levels above 2.

spell.name: Inner Flame
entries: Flames wreathe your body. Creatures you are touching must make a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 2d6} Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. Creatures you are {@condition Grappled|XPHB|grappling} and creatures {@condition Grappled|XPHB|grappling} you automatically fail this saving throw. If you are {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by a creature that fails this saving throw, the creature releases you and the grapple ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Nomi's Adamantine Carapace

m.text: a hunk of adamantine worth at least 300 GP

entries: You touch a willing creature, causing blue-green veins of adamantine to thread through its skin, making it tougher than steel and as flexible as silk. For the duration, the target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid, Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and its base AC is 20 (a Shield's benefits apply as normal).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The base AC increases by 1 for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Pestilence
entries: Choose a magical contagion from the list below; you direct a pathogen that carries this contagion towards a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage and become {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for the duration.

While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way, the creature takes {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. In addition, it makes another Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. If it successfully saves against this spell three times after becoming {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the spell ends. If it fails its saves three times after becoming {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target is no longer {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, but it is subjected to the chosen contagion for the spell's duration:

items: Bardthritis. The creature's joints lock in place, hampering its movement. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity-based {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}, it has a -2 penalty to AC, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet.

Brain Rot. The creature is plagued with a splitting headache. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws, and it takes {@damage 1d8} Psychic damage at the end of each of its turns while it is Concentrating on a spell.

Dragonorrhea. The creature contracts a fever that clouds its judgement. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws, and it is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by all creatures it can see that haven't dealt damage to it yet.

Fey Fever. The creature becomes paranoid as it hallucinates visions of allies turning on them. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom checks and Wisdom saving throws and is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} of Friendly creatures it can see.

Ghoulgrene. The creature's flesh decays. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution checks and Constitution saving throws and, if the creature would regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it regains half as many instead. In addition, whenever the creature finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it must roll 1d20; on a 1–10, it gains no benefits from the rest.

Shadowpox. The creature's muscles become atrophied. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength-based {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}, and Strength-based weapon attacks and Unarmed Strikes it makes deal 5 less damage on a hit (this can't reduce the damage below 1).

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Purple Rain
entries: An alcoholic rainstorm rapidly builds in a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-tall {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centred on a point within range, releasing intoxicating vapours. When the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} appears, each creature in it makes a Constitution saving throw, gaining 2 {@condition Drunkenness|HFN2025} levels on a failed save.
A creature also makes this save when it enters the spell's area or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

A creature with levels of {@condition Drunkenness|HFN2025} inflicted by this spell takes {@damage 1d10} Poison damage per level of {@condition Drunkenness|HFN2025} it has at the start of each of its turns. When the spell ends, affected creatures immediately lose all levels of {@condition Drunkenness|HFN2025} inflicted by this spell.

spell.name: Raiko's Rending Rage
entries: You imbue yourself with the swift rage of a tempest, turning your movement into a storm of thunder and lightning. Until the end of your turn, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 40 feet, your movement doesn't provoke {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB}, and you can make up to four attacks with Melee weapons or {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} (no action required), but each attack must be against a different target. On a hit, an attack deals an extra {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage.

At the end of your turn, a thunderous boom follows in your wake, which can be heard up to 2,500 feet away. Each creature you attacked with this spell makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d8} Thunder damage and is knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Lightning and Thunder damage increases by {@dice 1d8} each for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Riptide
entries: Choose one creature you can see within range that is in at least 2 feet of water and choose a direction. Turbulent currents wrap around the creature. For the duration, the target must make a Strength saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On a failure, it is dragged 30 feet in the direction you chose, and its Swim {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. On a success, the creature can move normally until the start of its next turn.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can change the direction of the underwater currents. The spell ends if the creature is ever out of the spell's range or is no longer in water at least 2 feet deep.

spell.name: Summon Plant

m.text: a pouch of silver seeds worth 200+ GP

entries: You call forth a botanical spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. This corporeal form uses the {@creature Botanical Spirit|HFN2025} stat block. When you cast the spell, choose a classification: Arboreal, Floral, or Herbal. The creature resembles an animated plant of your choice that would belong to the chosen classification, which determines certain traits in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your companions. In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and uses its move to avoid danger.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: The Bends
entries: You assault the internal chemistry of a creature you can see within range, causing bubbles of nitrogen to precipitate in its blood, forcing it to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 5d8} Poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}. If the target is completely submerged in water, increase the damage dice from d8s to d10s.
At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} target repeats the save, ending the condition on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.

entries.name: Age
entries: Your age is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Anatomy
entries: Your anatomy is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Your creature type is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Driving Goals
entries: The ambitions of your bound psyche empower your adventures. The origin of this drive has an impact on your abilities, which manifests in one of several ways. Choose one of the following origins:

items.name: Creative
entries: Your ambitions lie in perfecting your craft and creating something worthwhile. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks using {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you're proficient.

items.name: Noble
entries: Someone or something still needs you, and your cause drives you to seek the good of those around you. When a willing creature within 30 feet of you makes an ability check using a skill in which you're proficient, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to project your emotion and memories, allowing the creature to add half your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the ability check. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Vengeful
entries: A part of you has unfinished business. Your rage drives you and can empower you in combat. When a creature hits you with an attack, you can declare vengeance on it, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all attack rolls against it, and {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against all other creatures, until the end of your next turn. Once you have used this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Muscle Memory
entries: You can draw on the combined memories within your mind to remember skills from your pasts. When you choose this species, pick one weapon, one tool, and one skill. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose one. You have proficiency with it until you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Psychic Amplifier
entries: Your strongest emotions can become contagious, especially when magic is involved. When you unwillingly become {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, each other creature within 15 feet of you makes a Wisdom Saving Throw, becoming {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by you (same as the triggering condition) on a failure. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + your Wisdom modifier.

entries.name: What Came Before
entries: You gain one special trait from your host species. This trait cannot increase your ability scores or grant a feat. It also can't be a trait that grants you access to other species' traits, such as the mycelian's Symbiotic Assimilation.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Wisdom score increases by 2, and your Intelligence score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Your age is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Anatomy
entries: Your anatomy is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: Your creature type is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is the same as your host species.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice—one for your host and one for your soul fragment.

entries.name: Driving Goals
entries: The ambitions of your bound psyche empower your adventures. The origin of this drive has an impact on your abilities, which manifests in one of several ways. Choose one of the following origins:

items.name: Creative
entries: Your ambitions lie in perfecting your craft and creating something worthwhile. You have {@bold Advantage} on ability checks using Artisan's Tools with which you're proficient.

items.name: Noble
entries: Someone or something still needs you, and your cause drives you to seek the good of those around you. When a willing creature within 30 feet of you makes an ability check using a skill in which you're proficient, you can take a Reaction to project your emotion and memories, allowing the creature to add half your Proficiency Bonus to the ability check. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

items.name: Vengeful
entries: A part of you has unfinished business. Your rage drives you and can empower you in combat. When a creature hits you with an attack, you can declare vengeance on it, gaining {@bold Advantage} on all attack rolls against it, and {@bold Disadvantage} on attack rolls against all other creatures, until the end of your next turn. Once you have used this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

entries.name: Muscle Memory
entries: You can draw on the combined memories within your mind to remember skills from your pasts. When you choose this species, pick one weapon, one tool, and one skill. When you finish a Long Rest, choose one. You have proficiency with it until you finish your next Long Rest.

entries.name: Psychic Amplifier
entries: Your strongest emotions can become contagious, especially when magic is involved. When you unwillingly become {@condition Charmed} or {@condition Frightened}, each other creature within 15 feet of you makes a Wisdom saving throw, becoming {@condition Charmed} or {@condition Frightened} by you (same as the triggering condition) on a failure. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Wisdom modifier.

entries.name: What Came Before
entries: You gain one special trait from your host species. This trait cannot increase your ability scores or grant a feat. It also can't be a trait that grants you access to other species' traits, such as the mycelian's Symbiotic Assimilation.

entries.name: Age
entries: Entomorphs reach maturity at 16 years and can live up to 70 years.

entries.name: Anatomy
entries: You have four arms and two legs.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium, about 3 to 6 feet tall.

entries.name: Four-Armed
entries: You have four arms, two of which are smaller. Each of your smaller arms can only be used to manipulate objects or wield weapons with the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property.

entries.name: Entomorph Lineage
entries: Entomorphs exist in many different varieties. The Formigan is presented here.

items.name: Inborn Alchemy
entries: Your plate produces a small amount of acid, enough to burn attackers. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid damage. When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a Melee Attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to deal {@damage 1d4} Acid damage to it. You can use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Protective Plate
entries: Your head plate protects you from attacks while you're low to the ground. While {@condition Prone|XPHB}, you provide {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} to yourself and creatures directly behind you.

entries.name: Age
entries: Entomorphs reach maturity at 16 years and can live up to 70 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium, about 3 to 6 feet tall.

entries.name: Four-Armed
entries: You have four arms, two of which are smaller. Each of your smaller arms can only be used to manipulate objects or wield weapons with the {@itemProperty L|PHB|Light} property.

entries.name: Entomorph Lineage
entries: Entomorphs exist in many different varieties. The Formigan is presented here.

entries.name: Age
entries: Coralborn reach maturity at creation and can live up to 200 years.

entries.name: Anatomy
entries: You have two arms and two legs.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium.

entries.name: Aquatic Aquifer
entries: You always have the {@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast the spell once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so again when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any level 3+ spell slots you have. Constitution is your spellcasting ability when you cast it with this trait.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Distributed Body
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB|Saving Throws} you make to avoid or end being {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.

entries.name: Obligate Amphibian
entries: You can breathe both air and water. When you take a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must spend it at least halfway immersed in water to gain its benefits.

entries.name: Stony Stingskin
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you are {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or are grappling a creature to fire thousands of tiny needles into one creature that is touching you. That creature makes a Constitution {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB} (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} target repeats the save, ending the condition on itself on a success. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Coralborn Lineage
entries: Coralborn are as diverse as the biomes in which they grow, with bodies well-adapted to their environments. The Barrier lineage is presented here, but more options, like Deadwhite and Deepbloom will be available in VoidSea!

items.name: Recalcification
entries: You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} at the start of each of your turns.

items.name: Reef Barrier
entries: You can cast the {@spell Coralskin|HFN2025} spell once without expending a spell slot and without requiring spell components or {@variantrule Concentration|XPHB}. Instead of targeting yourself, you must target a willing creature that you touch. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for it. You regain the ability to cast the spell in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Sustenance from the Sun
entries: You do not need to eat. Instead, spending 8 hours in sunlight relieves you of the hazard of Malnutrition for that day.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2.

entries.name: Age
entries: Coralborn reach maturity at creation and can live up to 200 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Aquan and Common.

entries.name: Aquatic Aquifer
entries: You always have the {@spell create food and water|PHB} spell prepared. You can cast the spell once without expending a spell slot, and you regain the ability to do so again when you finish a Long Rest. You can also cast it using any level 3+ spell slots you have. Constitution is your spellcasting ability when you cast it with this trait.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have {@sense Darkvision} with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Distributed Body
entries: You have resistance to poison damage. You also have {@bold Advantage} on saving throws you make to avoid or end being {@condition Poisoned|PHB}.

entries.name: Obligate Amphibian
entries: You can breathe both air and water. When you take a Long Rest, you must spend it at least halfway immersed in water to gain its benefits.

entries.name: Stony Stingskin
entries: You can take a Bonus Action while you are {@condition Grappled|PHB} or are grappling a creature to fire thousands of tiny needles into one creature that is touching you. That creature makes a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Constitution modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 1d6} poison damage and is {@condition Poisoned|PHB} for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Poisoned|PHB} target repeats the save, ending the condition on itself on a success. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Coralborn Lineage
entries: Coralborn are as diverse as the biomes in which they grow, with bodies well-adapted to their environments. The Barrier lineage is presented here, but more options, like Deadwhite and Deepbloom will be available in VoidSea!

entries.name: Age
entries: Pengkin reach maturity at 14 years and can live up to 60 years.

entries.name: Anatomy
entries: You have two flippers, which function as arms and hands, and two legs.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium, about 5 feet tall.

entries.name: Downy Insulation
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage.

entries.name: Natural Diver
entries: You can hold your breath for twice as long as normal.

entries.name: Sliding Belly
entries: While {@condition Prone|XPHB}, you can slide instead of crawl, which costs you no extra movement. While sliding, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet while you're moving across a flat surface, or 20 feet if the surface is wet or frozen. If you voluntarily fall {@condition Prone|XPHB} on your turn, you can stand up on that same turn without expending any movement.

entries.name: Rescue Ride
entries: When you slide within 5 feet of a willing creature of your size or smaller, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to carry it. The creature moves with you until the end of the turn and doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} while doing so. You can only carry one creature in this way at a time.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 2, and your Wisdom score increases by 1.

entries.name: Age
entries: Pengkin reach maturity at 14 years and can live up to 60 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium, about 5 feet tall.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

entries.name: Downy Insulation
entries: You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Natural Diver
entries: You can hold your breath for twice as long as normal.

entries.name: Sliding Belly
entries: While {@condition Prone|PHB}, you can slide instead of crawl, which costs you no extra movement. While sliding, your Speed increases by 5 feet while you're moving across a flat surface, or 20 feet if the surface is wet or frozen. If you voluntarily fall {@condition Prone|PHB} on your turn, you can stand up on that same turn without expending any movement.

entries.name: Rescue Ride
entries: When you slide within 5 feet of a willing creature of your size or smaller, you can take a Reaction to carry it. The creature moves with you until the end of the turn and doesn't provoke Opportunity Attacks while doing so. You can only carry one creature in this way at a time.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength score increases by 2 and your Intelligence score increases by 1.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one other language of your choice.

entries.name: Inborn Alchemy
entries: Your plate produces a small amount of acid, enough to burn attackers. You have resistance to acid damage. When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, you can take a Reaction to deal {@damage 1d4} acid damage to it. You can use this Reaction a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Protective Plate
entries: Your head plate protects you from attacks while you're low to the ground. While {@condition Prone|PHB}, you provide half cover to yourself and creatures directly behind you.

entries.name: Ability Score Increase
entries: Your Strength score increases by 1.

entries.name: Recalcification
entries: You gain a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to half your Proficiency Bonus at the start of each of your turns.

entries.name: Reef Barrier
entries: You can cast the {@spell Coralskin|HFN2025} spell once without expending a spell slot and without requiring spell components or Concentration. Instead of targeting yourself, you must target a willing creature that you touch. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for it. You regain the ability to cast the spell in this way when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Sustenance from the Sun
entries: You do not need to eat. Instead, spending 8 hours in sunlight relieves you of the hazard of Malnutrition for that day.

raceFluff.name: Intertwined
entries: It's a well-known truth that death separates body and soul, leaving one to decay while the other flies off to its final rest, but what becomes of the mind? In most cases, it's assumed to leave with the soul, but in the presence of powerful magic, such as a planar gateway, the psyche might linger long enough to form an intertwined.

raceFluff.name: Entomorph
entries: Six limbs, mandibles, and an exoskeleton that shimmers beneath the desert sun—from a distance, entomorphs are easily mistaken for oversized ants. Balking at the comparison, these insectoid dune-dwellers rush to explain that there is more to them than meets the compound eye.

items.name: Formigan Entomorph
entries: Easily identifiable thanks to the large, dish-like crowns atop their heads, formigans are an insectoid species renowned for their alchemical ingenuity and remote desert societies. Members of this species are commonly found in the astringentum, a theocracy of metal-haters, The Wandering Oasis (a nomadic group that feeds and waters any they come across), and Bards of the College of Mixology.

entries.name: The Outpouring
entries: Making their homes within the narrow slot canyons and winding tunnels that cut through desert sandstone, formigans have a special relationship with water. One desert storm can flood an entire colony, killing thousands, but a lack of water will have an equally high toll. Thus, during a deluge, formigans perform the Outpouring, where chosen members of the colony use their headplates to direct the water in a cascade down to the rain wells. To be the one at the entrance, The First of the Storm, is considered among the greatest honours in the colony.

entries.name: Alchemical Evolution
entries: Beyond their use in the Outpouring, formigan headplates are integral to everyday life. They can block tunnels from invaders, shade against the heat, and with practice, eventually produce useful chemicals and compounds. The discovery of these chemicals and their abilities sparked a wave of interest in alchemy. To this day, it's not uncommon to come across a formigan alchemist purveying unique, homegrown compounds and concoctions.

raceFluff.name: Coralborn

entries: Ohoho, no. You don't get it. They don't live in the reef. They are the reef! So tread lightly, and be polite. Those ‘pretty little rocks' heard every word you just said.

When mutated predators descend upon the enduring, beautiful reefs of the VoidSea's shallows, the coralborn are waiting to receive them. In an instant, hunting grounds transform into an ambush as the ambulatory corals stretch their jewel-toned limbs and rise from the seabed. From calcite latticework of their skin fire a multitude of tiny stingers, each launched from one of the thousands of polyps that comprise their bodies. Once the stuff of legend, coralborn are a walking reminder that the ocean once held beauty—and one day will again.

items.name: Barrier Coralborn
entries: Barrier coralborn were the first walking reefs, born to shield and shepherd their descendants in times of crisis. When outside enemies threaten their soft-fleshed cnidaran cousins, these kaleidoscopic warriors hurtle across the seabed to take the blow themselves. With frenzied polyps hard at work beneath their calcite shells, barrier coralborn regenerate from superficial wounds in seconds and can even temporarily spread their calciferous armour to others in case of emergencies.

raceFluff.name: Pengkin
entries: Pengkin keep to the arctic regions of the Voidsea, deftly manoeuvring along the ice and through the water with an avian grace that leaves most outsiders flailing. Watch your step when wandering into a pengkin town or you'll wind up with a bruised and frosty tailbone in the middle of the road, the locals skidding and cursing as they swerve around you.

entries.name: Above and Beneath
entries: Pengkin build their homes above the waves, but spend nearly as much of their lives beneath them. With the frozen earth of their homeland unfit for agriculture, they turn to the sea for sustenance. Hunting parties dive for days at a time to scour the depths for food, enhancing the capacity of their prodigious lungs with magic or ingenious gear. They rarely return empty-flippered—in addition to a bounty of fish and shellfish, they retrieve all sorts of curious treasures from coral reefs and sunken shipwrecks, working them into many of their traditional crafts.

entries.name: Language of Gifts
entries: Pengkin have long upheld a storied tradition of gift giving, which has only strengthened as new shipping lanes bring a wider array of exotic wares to their icy shores. Gifts are exchanged on nearly every occasion, and a touch of competitiveness elevates the practice to an art of excess, with each pengkin striving to outdo the last in extravagance. In contrast, the token of betrothal remains a humble rounded stone, once a rarity on the ice—a practice that has led to more than a few accidental engagements between pengkin and visiting dwarves.

entries.name: Wind and Water
entries: Pengkin legend traces their origins to the distant rocborn, before that proud people's fall from the skies. The most commonly repeated tale speaks of a wayward rocborn family that found itself far from their arid homeland, lost upon the Voidsea. Caught in a raging tempest, they cried out to the spirits of the ocean for deliverance and were offered a bargain: salvation in exchange for their powers of flight. By the time the last desperate words of assent left their lips, they had been remade into creatures of ice and water. Thus, pengkin lorekeepers claim that their people never truly lost their power like the rocborn eventually would—they merely exchanged it for something new.

condition.name: Wet
entries: A Wet creature is one that has recently been swimming or soaked in water, or who has been affected by an effect such as the {@spell Freezing Fog|HFN2025} spell.
While you have the Wet condition, you experience the following effects.

entries.name: Cold and Lightning Susceptibility
entries: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other effects that deal Cold or Lightning damage, and {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against extreme cold.

entries.name: Fire Protection
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other effects that deal Fire damage.

entries.name: Ending the Wet Condition
entries: When you take 5 or more Fire damage in a single turn or spend 1 hour around a source of heat, this condition ends.

condition.name: Drunkenness
entries: Drunkenness is measured in six levels. If an already drunk creature suffers another effect that causes drunkenness, its current level of drunkenness increases by a number indicated by the effect.

A creature suffers the effect of its current level of Drunkenness as well as all lower levels. For example, a creature suffering level 3 Drunkenness has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity, Charisma, and Wisdom checks and saving throws.

entries.caption: Drunkenness & Effects
rows: The creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks.
The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and saving throws.
The creature loses the effects of level 1 Drunkenness, and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma and Wisdom checks and saving throws.
The creature is {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} and retains no memory of events that occur between this point and when they are no longer drunk.

The creature falls {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} for {@dice 1d4 + 8} hours. When it wakes, it is no longer drunk, but doesn't gain the benefits of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, and gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

sense.name: Arcanovision
entries: Unlike the {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} spell, which lets a creature sense magic's presence but not see its point of origin, Arcanovision is the ability to see magic itself. Creatures and objects under the effect of spells, including those that cause invisibility, have their forms outlined in glowing twists of weave. When a creature casts a spell or activates a magic item, a creature with Arcanovision can see that creature, regardless of if it has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition, until the end of the highlighted creature's next turn.

Creatures devoid of the weave's touch are {@condition Invisible|XPHB} to Arcanovision. Uncovered magic items (for example, an unsheathed +1 Sword) are always visible within the radius of a creature's Arcanovision, though the creature wielding them is not. For this reason, creatures wielding or wearing magic items other than armour (not including shields) gain the benefits of invisibility with respect to Arcanovision. Creatures wearing magical armour other than shields do not gain the benefits of being {@condition Invisible|XPHB}.
